components.title: Text
components.summary: Displays one or more lines of read-only text, with full control over font, weight, design, color, spacing, line limits, and truncation.

properties.name: content
properties.defaultValue: "To be, or not to be, that is the question."
properties.notes: String literal is treated as a LocalizedStringKey; use init(verbatim:) to bypass localization.

properties.name: font
properties.notes: Use semantic text styles so Dynamic Type scales automatically. Maps via .font(_:).

properties.name: fontWeight
properties.notes: Applied with .fontWeight(_:).

properties.name: fontDesign
properties.notes: .fontDesign(_:) on iOS 16.1+; .monospaced() is a shorthand for monospaced design.

properties.name: fontWidth
properties.notes: .fontWidth(_:) on iOS 16+.

properties.name: foregroundStyle
properties.notes: Prefer .primary / .secondary or design tokens over raw colors. Uses .foregroundStyle(_:).

properties.name: bold
properties.notes: .bold() modifier.

properties.name: italic
properties.notes: .italic() modifier.

properties.name: underline
properties.notes: .underline(_:) on iOS 16+.

properties.name: strikethrough
properties.notes: .strikethrough(_:) on iOS 16+.

properties.name: lineLimit
properties.notes: 0 = unlimited; range 1...10 typical. Use .lineLimit(_:) or the range variant .lineLimit(_:reservesSpace:).

properties.name: truncationMode
properties.notes: Applies when text exceeds lineLimit. .truncationMode(_:).

properties.name: multilineTextAlignment
properties.notes: .multilineTextAlignment(_:) — affects wrapped multi-line text only.

properties.name: lineSpacing
properties.notes: Range 0...20. Points of space between wrapped lines via .lineSpacing(_:).

properties.name: kerning
properties.notes: Range -5...20. Per-character spacing via .kerning(_:).

properties.name: tracking
properties.notes: Range -5...20. Adds spacing after each character; overrides kerning. .tracking(_:).

properties.name: minimumScaleFactor
properties.notes: Range 0.3...1.0. Allows text to shrink to fit. .minimumScaleFactor(_:).

properties.name: allowsTightening
properties.notes: Lets the layout tighten inter-character spacing before truncating. .allowsTightening(_:).

properties.name: isMarkdown
properties.notes: String literals support inline Markdown (**bold**, *italic*, [links]) automatically when localized.

properties.name: textSelection
properties.notes: .textSelection(.enabled) makes text user-selectable; iOS 15+.

properties.name: dateReference
properties.notes: Text has live-updating date/time initializers omitted from the draft. Text(_:style:) renders a self-updating .relative / .timer / .date / .time / .offset string.

properties.name: dateStyle
properties.notes: Used via Text(_:style:) (iOS 15+). .timer and .relative auto-update over time without a Timer.

properties.name: timerInterval
properties.notes: Text(timerInterval:pauseTime:countsDown:) renders a count-up/count-down timer that updates automatically. iOS 16+.

properties.name: format
properties.notes: Text(_:format:) renders a value through a FormatStyle (e.g. Text(price, format: .currency(code: "USD"))). iOS 15+.

properties.name: baselineOffset
properties.notes: Range -10...10. .baselineOffset(_:) shifts text vertically from its baseline — omitted from the draft though it sits in the same Text layout group as kerning/tracking.

properties.name: textCase
properties.notes: .textCase(_:) applies upper/lowercasing in a locale-aware way; commonly used on section headers. Omitted from the draft.

components.higNotes: Use semantic text styles (title, body, caption) so text respects Dynamic Type. Limit decorative weights/designs; never disable Dynamic Type scaling. Keep contrast WCAG-compliant against the background.

components.title: Label
components.summary: A standard title + icon pairing for list rows, menus, and toolbar items, with switchable presentation via labelStyle.

properties.name: title
properties.notes: Title text; localized when a literal.

properties.name: systemImage
properties.notes: SF Symbol name for the icon. Use init(_:image:) for a custom asset instead.

properties.name: labelStyle
properties.notes: .labelStyle(_:) — context decides the automatic presentation (e.g. toolbars show icon-only).

properties.name: symbolRenderingMode
properties.notes: Applies to the SF Symbol icon. .symbolRenderingMode(_:).

properties.name: foregroundStyle
properties.notes: Tints both title and icon unless overridden per element.

components.higNotes: Pair a clear SF Symbol with concise text. Let containers choose presentation; only force iconOnly/titleOnly when space or rotation demands. Provide an accessibilityLabel when using iconOnly so VoiceOver reads the meaning.

components.title: TextField
components.summary: An editable single- or multi-line text input bound to a string or formatted value, with styles, prompt, axis, and submit configuration.

properties.name: titleKey
properties.notes: Label / placeholder. Used as placeholder on iOS when no prompt is supplied.

properties.name: text
properties.notes: Bound value; updates continuously for String, on commit for formatted non-String values.

properties.name: prompt
properties.notes: Guidance text shown as placeholder; pass via prompt: parameter. iOS 15+.

properties.name: axis
properties.notes: axis: .vertical grows to multiple lines (combine with lineLimit range). iOS 16+.

properties.name: textFieldStyle
properties.notes: roundedBorder unavailable on tvOS; squareBorder is macOS-only. .textFieldStyle(_:).

properties.name: lineLimit
properties.notes: Used with axis: .vertical, e.g. .lineLimit(1...5). iOS 16+.

properties.name: submitLabel
properties.notes: .submitLabel(_:) sets the keyboard return-key text. iOS 15+.

properties.name: keyboardType
properties.notes: iOS/iPadOS only. .keyboardType(_:).

properties.name: textContentType
properties.notes: Enables AutoFill / QuickType. .textContentType(_:).

properties.name: autocapitalization
properties.notes: .textInputAutocapitalization(_:) — iOS/iPadOS/tvOS only.

properties.name: autocorrectionDisabled
properties.notes: .autocorrectionDisabled(_:).

properties.name: isFocused
properties.notes: Drive via @FocusState + .focused(_:) to show the focused state.

properties.name: disabled
properties.notes: .disabled(_:).

properties.name: value
properties.notes: Missing from draft: TextField(_:value:format:) binds to a non-String type via a FormatStyle (e.g. numbers, PersonNameComponents). Commits on Return. A primary TextField initializer worth showcasing.

properties.name: onSubmit
properties.notes: Missing from draft: .onSubmit { } fires when the user submits (Return key). Pairs with submitLabel and the focused/error flow.

components.higNotes: Provide a clear prompt and an appropriate keyboardType / textContentType to enable AutoFill. Use submitLabel that matches the action. Validate at the boundary and surface errors near the field. Inside a Form, prefer label + prompt for platform-correct placement.

components.title: SecureField
components.summary: A masked text input for passwords and sensitive data; behaves like TextField but obscures characters.

properties.name: titleKey
properties.notes: Label / placeholder.

properties.name: text
properties.notes: Updates on every keystroke; never log this value.

properties.name: prompt
properties.notes: Placeholder guidance, e.g. Text("Required"). iOS 15+.

properties.name: textContentType
properties.notes: newPassword triggers Strong Password suggestions; oneTimeCode enables SMS autofill.

properties.name: submitLabel
properties.notes: .submitLabel(_:).

properties.name: isFocused
properties.notes: Drive via @FocusState.

properties.name: disabled
properties.notes: .disabled(_:).

components.higNotes: Set textContentType(.password / .newPassword) to get Keychain AutoFill and Strong Password. Never store the bound string outside the Keychain. Pair with a username TextField using .username for proper credential association.

components.title: TextEditor
components.summary: A scrollable, multi-line editor for long-form text; on iOS 26 it also binds to AttributedString for rich text editing.
components.minOS: iOS 14.0 / iPadOS 14.0 / macOS 11.0 (AttributedString binding: iOS 26 / macOS 26)

properties.name: text
properties.notes: Plain-text binding. iOS 26 adds an init(text: Binding<AttributedString>) for rich text.

properties.name: font
properties.notes: Inherited from environment via .font(_:).

properties.name: foregroundColor
properties.notes: .foregroundStyle(_:).

properties.name: lineSpacing
properties.notes: Range 0...20. .lineSpacing(_:).

properties.name: lineLimit
properties.notes: Caps visible lines. .lineLimit(_:).

properties.name: multilineTextAlignment
properties.notes: .multilineTextAlignment(_:).

properties.name: scrollContentBackground
properties.notes: .scrollContentBackground(.hidden) to apply a custom .background. iOS 16+.

properties.name: findNavigatorEnabled
properties.notes: .findNavigator(isPresented:) drives the find/replace UI. iOS 16+.

properties.name: autocorrectionDisabled
properties.notes: .autocorrectionDisabled(_:).

properties.name: disabled
properties.notes: .disabled(_:).

properties.name: textEditorStyle
properties.notes: Missing from draft: .textEditorStyle(_:) (iOS 17+ / macOS 14+ / visionOS 1+). The draft's styles array is empty — TextEditor does have native styles via TextEditorStyle.

components.higNotes: Use for composing notes, comments, or messages — not single-value entry (use TextField). Give it generous height, support find/replace on iPad/Mac, and respect Dynamic Type. On iOS 26 prefer the AttributedString binding for formatting toolbars.

components.title: Link
components.summary: A control that opens a URL — a Universal Link in its app, otherwise the default browser; overridable via OpenURLAction.

properties.name: titleKey
properties.notes: Link label; can also be a custom view label.

properties.name: destination
properties.notes: Target URL opened on tap/click.

properties.name: foregroundStyle
properties.notes: Style the Text label; default uses the accent/tint color.

properties.name: font
properties.notes: Applied to a Text label.

properties.name: openInApp
properties.notes: Override behavior by setting \.openURL with a custom OpenURLAction returning .handled / .systemAction.

components.higNotes: Label links by purpose, not the raw URL. Keep tap targets at least 44pt. For external destinations consider opening in SafariView; for in-app navigation use the coordinator's Button + push, not Link.

components.title: ShareLink
components.summary: Presents the system share sheet for any Transferable item, with optional preview, subject, and message.

properties.name: title
properties.notes: Convenience initializer creates a Label for you; omit to get the system-standard appearance.

properties.name: item
properties.notes: URL and String conform out of the box; custom types provide a TransferRepresentation.

properties.name: subject
properties.notes: Pre-fills the subject field for activities that support it (e.g. Mail).

properties.name: message
properties.notes: Pre-fills the message body where supported.

properties.name: hasPreview
properties.notes: Provide a SharePreview(title, image:) for non-URL/non-string items, or to show an instant preview.

properties.name: labelStyle
properties.notes: Customize when supplying a Label as the link content.

components.higNotes: Place in a toolbar or near the content being shared. Use the default system appearance unless you have a reason to customize. Supply a SharePreview so the sheet shows meaningful content immediately; add a FileRepresentation if file-only services (AirDrop, Mail) should appear.

components.title: AttributedString (in Text)
components.summary: A Unicode string with typed, run-based attributes — rendered by Text for mixed styling, Markdown, links, and per-run color.

properties.name: markdown
properties.notes: Initialize via AttributedString(markdown:) to parse inline Markdown into styled runs.

properties.name: baseFont
properties.notes: SwiftUI font attribute applied to the whole string; per-run attributes take priority.

properties.name: foregroundColor
properties.notes: Set the .foregroundColor attribute on the AttributeContainer or specific ranges.

properties.name: inlinePresentationIntent
properties.notes: Apply to a range to mark italic / bold / code / strikethrough runs.

properties.name: link
properties.notes: Set the .link attribute on a run to make it tappable inside Text.

components.higNotes: Use AttributedString to style portions of localized text while keeping the whole string translatable via Text interpolation. Keep attribute use semantic (emphasis, links) rather than purely decorative so it survives localization and respects Dynamic Type.

components.title: Button
components.summary: A control that initiates an action when tapped, clicked, or activated.
components.summaryLong: The fundamental action control. Combine with a role, a button style (including the iOS 26 .glass / .glassProminent), tint, controlSize, buttonBorderShape, and buttonSizing to cover every native variant.

properties.name: title
properties.notes: Text label. Prefer the title+systemImage initializer so the button can adapt icon/title rendering per container (toolbar, menu) and for VoiceOver.

properties.name: systemImage
properties.notes: SF Symbol name. Optional. Enables iconOnly rendering in toolbars while preserving an accessibility title.

properties.name: role
properties.notes: .destructive renders red; .cancel/.confirm/.close gain semantic styling in dialogs and sheets. .confirm and .close are iOS 26+.

properties.name: style
properties.notes: Applied via .buttonStyle(_:). .glass and .glassProminent are iOS 26 Liquid Glass styles. .borderedProminent is the standard call-to-action.

properties.name: tint
properties.notes: Applied via .tint(_:). Drives accent for bordered/prominent/glass styles. Use design-system accent, never raw white/black.

properties.name: controlSize
properties.notes: Applied via .controlSize(_:). .extraLarge is iOS 26+. Affects padding and height across button-like controls.

properties.name: buttonBorderShape
properties.notes: Applied via .buttonBorderShape(_:). .circle is iOS 26+. Only affects bordered/glass styles.

properties.name: buttonSizing
properties.notes: iOS 26+. Applied via .buttonSizing(_:). .flexible expands to fill available width; .fitted hugs content.

properties.name: repeatBehavior
properties.notes: Applied via .buttonRepeatBehavior(_:). .enabled fires the action repeatedly while held. iOS 17+.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: Driven by .disabled(!isEnabled). Disabled buttons dim and stop receiving input.

properties.name: labelStyle
properties.notes: Applied via .labelStyle(_:). .iconOnly keeps the title for VoiceOver while hiding it visually.

components.higNotes: Use a clear verb for the title. One prominent (call-to-action) button per screen region. Reserve .destructive red for irreversible actions and pair with a confirmation. Maintain a 44pt minimum tap target. On tvOS prefer .card style.
components.codeTemplate:
Button("{title}", systemImage: "{systemImage}"{role}) {
    // action
}
.buttonStyle(.{style})
.controlSize(.{controlSize})
.buttonBorderShape(.{buttonBorderShape})
.buttonSizing(.{buttonSizing})
.buttonRepeatBehavior(.{repeatBehavior})
.labelStyle(.{labelStyle})
.tint({tint})
.disabled({isDisabled})

components.title: Button Role
components.summary: A semantic value describing a button's purpose, driving system styling.

properties.name: role
properties.notes: .destructive and .cancel are iOS 15+. .confirm and .close are iOS 26+ and gain glass-era semantic treatment in sheets and dialogs.

components.higNotes: Roles let the system place and color buttons correctly automatically. In a confirmationDialog, mark the dangerous option .destructive and the safe escape .cancel so the system orders and emphasizes them per platform conventions.

components.title: Menu
components.summary: A control that presents a menu of actions, optionally with a primary action.

properties.name: title
properties.notes: Menu label. Can also be a Label with systemImage for icon+title.

properties.name: systemImage
properties.notes: Optional SF Symbol for the menu's label.

properties.name: menuStyle
properties.notes: Applied via .menuStyle(_:). .button renders the menu trigger as a button; .borderlessButton (macOS) is borderless. Deprecated bordered/borderlessButton variants exist.

properties.name: hasPrimaryAction
properties.notes: When true, use the primaryAction: initializer: tap fires the primary action; menu opens on long-press / indicator click.

properties.name: useSections
properties.notes: Group items with Section to create visually separated blocks within the menu.

properties.name: useNestedMenu
properties.notes: Nest a Menu inside the content closure to create a submenu.

properties.name: menuOrder
properties.notes: Applied via .menuOrder(_:). .fixed keeps declared order top-to-bottom; .priority surfaces the first item nearest the trigger. iOS 16+.

properties.name: menuIndicator
properties.notes: Applied via .menuIndicator(_:). Controls the disclosure chevron. iOS 15+.

properties.name: controlSize
properties.notes: Applies when the menu adopts a button style.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: Driven by .disabled(!isEnabled).

components.higNotes: Group related items with Section and dividers. Put the most common action first. Use a primaryAction when one action dominates but advanced options should stay one gesture away. Avoid deep nesting beyond one submenu level. Mark destructive items with role: .destructive.

components.title: Toggle
components.summary: A control that switches between on and off states bound to a Boolean.

properties.name: title
properties.notes: Describes what the toggle controls. A second Text in the label closure becomes a subtitle.

properties.name: systemImage
properties.notes: Optional SF Symbol via the title+systemImage initializer.

properties.name: isOn
properties.notes: The bound state. In a showroom, drive the live value directly.

properties.name: toggleStyle
properties.notes: Applied via .toggleStyle(_:). .switch is the iOS default; .button renders an on/off pill (great in toolbars); .checkbox is macOS-only.

properties.name: tint
properties.notes: Applied via .tint(_:). Colors the 'on' track of the switch and the filled state of the button style.

properties.name: controlSize
properties.notes: Most impactful on macOS and with the .button style.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: Driven by .disabled(!isEnabled).

components.higNotes: Use a toggle for an immediate, reversible binary setting (no Apply step). Label states clearly; the label should read as the thing being enabled, not as a question. The .button style suits toolbars; .checkbox belongs to macOS forms. Keep the 'on' tint consistent with the app accent.
components.codeTemplate:
Toggle("{title}", systemImage: "{systemImage}", isOn: ${isOn})
    .toggleStyle(.{toggleStyle})
    .controlSize(.{controlSize})
    .tint({tint})
    .disabled({isDisabled})

components.title: Stepper
components.summary: A control that increments and decrements a value via plus/minus buttons.

properties.name: title
properties.notes: Label shown beside the stepper controls.

properties.name: value
properties.notes: Bound value. Use the value:in:step: initializer to clamp to a range.

properties.name: range
properties.notes: Bounds passed to in:. Buttons auto-disable at the limits.

properties.name: step
properties.notes: Increment per tap. Pass to step:.

properties.name: controlSize
properties.notes: Affects the +/- glyph footprint, most visible on macOS.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: Driven by .disabled(!isEnabled).

components.higNotes: Use a stepper for small, discrete adjustments where exact values matter and the range is narrow. For wide ranges prefer a Slider. Always show the current value next to the control. Not available on tvOS.

components.title: EditButton
components.summary: A button that toggles the editMode environment value for an editable container.

properties.name: placement
properties.notes: Typically placed in a toolbar. The button auto-switches its title between Edit and Done.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: Driven by .disabled(!isEnabled).

components.higNotes: Pair with a List/ForEach that declares onDelete and onMove so Edit mode reveals delete and reorder affordances. The button reflects state automatically (Edit ↔ Done) — never hardcode the title. iOS/iPadOS/Mac Catalyst only.

components.title: RenameButton
components.summary: A button that triggers a rename action registered via the renameAction modifier.

properties.name: context
properties.notes: Commonly placed in a contextMenu or toolbar. Activation calls the nearest .renameAction { } in the environment.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: Driven by .disabled(!isEnabled).

components.higNotes: RenameButton has no closure of its own — it dispatches to the enclosing .renameAction(_:). Use it so the system supplies the correct localized label and triggers inline rename (e.g. a focused TextField). Pair with @FocusState to begin editing.

components.title: PasteButton
components.summary: A system button that reads Transferable items from the pasteboard into a closure.

properties.name: payloadType
properties.notes: The Transferable type the button accepts; the closure receives an array of that type.

properties.name: tint
properties.notes: Applied via .tint(_:). One of the few customizations the system button allows.

properties.name: buttonBorderShape
properties.notes: Applied via .buttonBorderShape(_:).

properties.name: labelStyle
properties.notes: Applied via .labelStyle(_:). The label text is system-provided and localized.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: On iOS the button auto-validates/invalidates against pasteboard contents; on macOS it does not.

components.higNotes: Use PasteButton instead of a custom paste control so the system grants pasteboard access without a permission prompt (privacy-preserving paste). The system supplies the label and icon — do not relabel. iOS auto-disables it when the pasteboard has no matching content.
components.codeTemplate:
PasteButton(payloadType: {payloadType}) { items in
    // handle items
}
.buttonBorderShape(.{buttonBorderShape})
.labelStyle(.{labelStyle})
.tint({tint})
.disabled({isDisabled})

components.title: ControlGroup
components.summary: A container that lays out semantically related controls in a context-appropriate cluster.

properties.name: label
properties.notes: Used when the group collapses into a toolbar overflow menu. Often a Label with systemImage.

properties.name: controlGroupStyle
properties.notes: Applied via .controlGroupStyle(_:). .palette shows a row of icon buttons; .compactMenu collapses into a single menu. .navigation/.compactMenu are iOS 16+.

properties.name: controlSize
properties.notes: Sizes the clustered controls together.

properties.name: tint
properties.notes: Applied via .tint(_:) to color the grouped controls uniformly.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: Driven by .disabled(!isEnabled) on the whole group.

components.higNotes: Group only controls that belong together (e.g. share + favorite + delete). The group adopts the right look per context — segmented in a toolbar, an overflow menu when space is tight. Provide a label so the overflow menu has a name. Not available on watchOS.

components.title: Link
components.summary: A control that navigates to a URL, opening it via the system or the app's openURL handler.

properties.name: title
properties.notes: The visible label for the link.

properties.name: destination
properties.notes: Target URL. Opens in the default handler unless intercepted by an openURL environment action.

properties.name: style
properties.notes: Link is button-like and honors .buttonStyle(_:).

properties.name: tint
properties.notes: Applied via .tint(_:).

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: Driven by .disabled(!isEnabled).

components.higNotes: Use Link for navigation to external URLs, not for in-app routing (use a coordinator-driven Button for that). Override behavior app-wide with the openURL environment value to handle deep links.
components.codeTemplate:
Link("{title}", destination: {destination})
    .buttonStyle(.{style})
    .tint({tint})
    .disabled({isDisabled})

components.title: ShareLink
components.summary: A button that presents the system share sheet for one or more Transferable items.

properties.name: item
properties.notes: The item(s) to share. Any Transferable: URL, String, Image, custom type.

properties.name: label
properties.notes: Optional custom label. Default is a localized Share label with the share icon.

properties.name: subject
properties.notes: Optional subject line passed to share targets like Mail.

properties.name: message
properties.notes: Optional default message body for the share sheet.

properties.name: style
properties.notes: ShareLink honors .buttonStyle(_:).

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: Driven by .disabled(!isEnabled).

components.higNotes: Prefer ShareLink over presenting UIActivityViewController manually — it adapts the label per platform and supplies SharePreview for rich previews. Provide a SharePreview when sharing images or files so recipients see a thumbnail and title.

components.title: HelpLink
components.summary: A button with a system-standard help appearance (the circled question mark) that opens documentation relevant to its context. macOS-only.

properties.name: anchor
properties.notes: Help-book anchor opened on activation. Use the anchor: initializer for documentation, or the action closure initializer for a custom action (open a URL / window).

properties.name: useAction
properties.notes: When true, use the HelpLink { } action initializer instead of anchor: to perform an arbitrary action such as openURL.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: Driven by .disabled(!isEnabled).

components.higNotes: HelpLink has a fixed standard appearance — do not relabel or restyle it. The system places it automatically: top-trailing in an alert or confirmationDialog, lower-leading in a sheet toolbar. macOS only; gate with #if os(macOS).
components.codeTemplate:
HelpLink(anchor: "{anchor}")
    .disabled({isDisabled})

components.title: TextFieldLink
components.summary: A watchOS control that presents the system text-input interface (scribble / dictation / keyboard) when pressed and returns the entered text. watchOS-only.

properties.name: prompt
properties.notes: Optional prompt shown in the text-input interface.

properties.name: label
properties.notes: The button label. Can also be a Label with systemImage.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: Driven by .disabled(!isEnabled).

components.higNotes: Use on watchOS to collect short text without a custom keyboard. The system supplies the input UI (dictation, scribble, emoji). watchOS only; gate with #if os(watchOS).

components.title: NewDocumentButton
components.summary: A button for the document launch experience that creates a new document, optionally via a template picker. Lives in a DocumentGroupLaunchScene. Not available on tvOS or watchOS.

properties.name: label
properties.notes: Custom label text. Omit to get the system default "Create Document" label.

properties.name: usesTemplate
properties.notes: When true, use the for:Document.self initializer with an async closure to present a template picker before creating the document.

components.higNotes: Only valid inside a DocumentGroupLaunchScene. Provide one button for a blank document and optionally a second for a template chooser. iOS 18 / iPadOS 18 / macOS 15 / visionOS 2+.

components.title: Picker
components.summary: A control for selecting from a set of mutually exclusive values, bound to a Hashable selection.
components.higNotes: Use .menu for compact forms, .segmented for 2-5 short mutually-exclusive options, .wheel/.inline inside forms or wheels. .segmented should hold few short labels or SF Symbols; avoid mixing text and images of very different widths. On watchOS prefer .navigationLink or .wheel. .palette pairs SF Symbols with paletteSelectionEffect for tinted multi/single icon selection.

properties.name: style
properties.notes: Applied via .pickerStyle(_:). radioGroup is macOS-only; segmented/wheel availability varies by platform (no wheel on macOS, no segmented on watchOS).

properties.name: titleKey
properties.notes: Label text. Use a String Catalog key; omit visually with .labelsHidden() when context conveys meaning.

properties.name: selection
properties.notes: Bound Hashable value. Each content view must carry a matching .tag(_:) unless ForEach derives it from Identifiable id.

properties.name: labelsHidden
properties.notes: Applies .labelsHidden() to drop the visible label while keeping it for VoiceOver.

properties.name: paletteSelectionEffect
properties.notes: Only meaningful with .palette style; controls how selection is indicated on symbols.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: Drives .disabled(!isEnabled).

components.codeTemplate:
Picker("{titleKey}", selection: {selection}) {
    Text("Chocolate").tag(Flavor.chocolate)
    Text("Vanilla").tag(Flavor.vanilla)
    Text("Strawberry").tag(Flavor.strawberry)
}
.pickerStyle(.{style})
.paletteSelectionEffect(.{paletteSelectionEffect})
.labelsHidden({labelsHidden})
.disabled({isDisabled})

components.title: DatePicker
components.summary: A control for selecting an absolute date and/or time, bound to a Date with optional range limits.
components.higNotes: Use .compact in forms (tap to reveal calendar/time). Use .graphical for prominent calendar selection (event creation). Limit selectable range with `in:` to prevent invalid input. Choose displayedComponents to show only date, only time, or both. .wheel is heavy; reserve for legacy/space-constrained flows. Respect the user's locale, calendar, and time zone via the environment.

properties.name: style
properties.notes: Applied via .datePickerStyle(_:). field & stepperField are macOS-only.

properties.name: titleKey
properties.notes: Label text.

properties.name: selection
properties.notes: Bound Date value.

properties.name: displayedComponents
properties.notes: OptionSet selecting which fields appear. .date shows day/month/year; .hourAndMinute shows time.

properties.name: rangeMode
properties.notes: Drives the `in:` parameter. closed = a...b, from = a..., through = ...b. Prevents out-of-range selection.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: Drives .disabled(!isEnabled).

components.codeTemplate:
DatePicker(
    "{titleKey}",
    selection: {selection},
    in: {range},
    displayedComponents: {displayedComponents}
)
.datePickerStyle(.{style})
.disabled({isDisabled})

components.title: MultiDatePicker
components.summary: A calendar control for selecting multiple dates as a Set of DateComponents.
components.higNotes: Use only when users genuinely select multiple discrete dates (availability calendars, multi-day bookings). Always presents as a graphical month calendar. Constrain with `in:` (a Range<Date> or open range) to bound selectable dates. Respect environment locale/calendar/timeZone. Not available on tvOS or watchOS.

properties.name: titleKey
properties.notes: Accessibility/section label for the calendar.

properties.name: selection
properties.notes: Set of selected DateComponents; empty set is the empty state.

properties.name: rangeMode
properties.notes: Drives the `in:` parameter. range = a..<b (half-open), from = a..., upTo = ..<b.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: Drives .disabled(!isEnabled).

components.title: ColorPicker
components.summary: A control that presents the system color picker UI to select a Color, with optional opacity.
styles: automatic (system color picker; no style modifier)
components.higNotes: Shows a swatch of the current color; tapping opens the full system picker (grid, spectrum, sliders, eyedropper on macOS). Set supportsOpacity: false when alpha is meaningless (e.g. solid theme color). There is one canonical presentation; do not build a custom color UI when this suffices. Not available on tvOS or watchOS. Can bind to Color or to a CGColor.

properties.name: titleKey
properties.notes: Label text shown beside the swatch.

properties.name: selection
properties.notes: Bound Color (or CGColor variant). The live control is itself a colorPicker.

properties.name: supportsOpacity
properties.notes: When false, hides opacity controls and strips alpha from set/selected colors.

properties.name: labelsHidden
properties.notes: Applies .labelsHidden() to show only the swatch.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: Drives .disabled(!isEnabled).

components.codeTemplate:
ColorPicker(
    "{titleKey}",
    selection: {selection},
    supportsOpacity: {supportsOpacity}
)
.labelsHidden({labelsHidden})
.disabled({isDisabled})

components.title: Slider
components.summary: A control for selecting a value from a bounded linear range, with optional step, labels, and tick marks.
components.minOS: iOS 13.0 / iPadOS 13.0 / macOS 10.15 / watchOS 6.0 (neutralValue & tick marks iOS 26)
styles: default (no slider style protocol; configured via initializer)
components.higNotes: Use for continuous values where exact precision isn't critical (volume, brightness). Provide minimumValueLabel/maximumValueLabel icons to communicate direction of increase. Use `step` for discrete increments; the step is also used by VoiceOver adjust actions. iOS 26 adds a neutral origin (e.g. center-zero for pan/balance) and tick marks via the `.ticks` initializer. Not available on tvOS (use focus-based controls instead).

properties.name: value
properties.notes: Bound value; the live driver is a slider over the same range.

properties.name: range
properties.notes: The `in:` bounds. Min maps to leading edge, max to trailing.

properties.name: step
properties.notes: Optional discrete increment via `step:`. Omit for fully continuous. Range 0.01...50 typical.

properties.name: neutralValue
properties.notes: iOS 26+: visual origin the fill emanates from (e.g. 0 for a -50...50 pan control). Use the `neutralValue:` initializer.

properties.name: minimumValueLabel
properties.notes: Leading label/icon for the minimum extreme.

properties.name: maximumValueLabel
properties.notes: Trailing label/icon for the maximum extreme.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: Drives .disabled(!isEnabled).

components.title: Stepper
components.summary: A control that performs increment and decrement actions on a value, with optional range and step.
components.higNotes: Use for small, precise integer or fine adjustments where a slider is too coarse (quantity, count). Pair with a value label so the current value is always visible. Bound the value with `in:` to disable the +/- buttons at the extremes automatically. On watchOS the Digital Crown is the idiomatic alternative for larger ranges. Not available on tvOS.

properties.name: titleKey
properties.notes: Label text shown beside the +/- controls.

properties.name: value
properties.notes: Bound Strideable value (Int/Double).

properties.name: range
properties.notes: The `in:` bounds; buttons disable at extremes.

properties.name: step
properties.notes: Increment applied per tap via `step:`.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: Drives .disabled(!isEnabled).

components.title: Toggle
components.summary: A control that toggles between on and off states, bound to a Bool.
components.higNotes: Use a Toggle for a single binary setting. .switch is the iOS default; .button renders a selectable button (great in toolbars with an SF Symbol label) where the on-state shows tinted/filled; .checkbox is macOS-only for forms/lists. Always give a clear label describing the on-state. Use a subtitle Text for extra context. Tint via .tint(_:).

properties.name: style
properties.notes: Applied via .toggleStyle(_:). checkbox is macOS-only; button is best with a systemImage label.

properties.name: titleKey
properties.notes: Label text.

properties.name: systemImage
properties.notes: Optional SF Symbol name for the convenience label initializer; pairs well with .button style.

properties.name: isOn
properties.notes: Bound on/off state.

properties.name: tint
properties.notes: Applied via .tint(_:); colors the on-state track or filled button.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: Drives .disabled(!isEnabled).

components.codeTemplate:
Toggle(
    "{titleKey}",
    systemImage: "{systemImage}",
    isOn: {isOn}
)
.toggleStyle(.{style})
.tint({tint})
.disabled({isDisabled})

components.title: Gauge
components.summary: A view that shows a current value within a finite range, with labels and a tintable indicator.
components.higNotes: Use a Gauge to show a value relative to a known capacity (battery, heart-rate zone, progress toward a goal). accessory* styles are designed for watchOS complications and Lock Screen widgets; capacity variants fill proportionally while non-capacity variants show a moving indicator. Apply a Gradient via the style initializer (e.g. CircularGaugeStyle(tint:)) to convey severity. circular is watchOS-only; on iOS use accessoryCircular.

properties.name: style
properties.notes: Applied via .gaugeStyle(_:). circular is watchOS-only. linearCapacity is the default linear bar on iOS.

properties.name: value
properties.notes: Current value, driven live by a slider over `range`.

properties.name: range
properties.notes: The `in:` capacity bounds (defaults to 0...1 when omitted).

properties.name: label
properties.notes: Describes the gauge's purpose; can be an SF Symbol.

properties.name: currentValueLabel
properties.notes: Shown inside/near the indicator on most styles.

properties.name: minimumValueLabel
properties.notes: Label for the range minimum.

properties.name: maximumValueLabel
properties.notes: Label for the range maximum.

properties.name: tint
properties.notes: Pass via the style initializer, e.g. CircularGaugeStyle(tint:), or .tint(_:).

components.title: ProgressView
components.summary: A view that shows progress toward completion of a task — determinate (value/total), indeterminate, or time-based.
components.higNotes: Use determinate (value:total:) when you can measure progress, otherwise the indeterminate spinner. .circular often renders as an indeterminate spinner on non-macOS platforms; use .linear for measurable file/download progress. The timerInterval: initializer auto-advances over a Date range — ideal for countdowns. Provide a Text label for context; tint with .tint(_:). For full-screen branded loading prefer a custom LoadingView per house rules.

properties.name: style
properties.notes: Applied via .progressViewStyle(_:). circular may appear indeterminate off macOS.

properties.name: mode
properties.notes: Selects the initializer family: ProgressView(), ProgressView(value:total:), or ProgressView(timerInterval:).

properties.name: value
properties.notes: Determinate progress fraction; live driver is a slider 0...total.

properties.name: total
properties.notes: Completion value (default 1.0). value/total drives the fill.

properties.name: label
properties.notes: Optional descriptive label.

properties.name: currentValueLabel
properties.notes: Optional label showing the formatted current value.

properties.name: tint
properties.notes: Applied via .tint(_:); colors the bar/spinner.

components.title: TextField
components.summary: A control that displays an editable single-line text interface, bound to a String or — via a FormatStyle — to a non-string value.
components.minOS: iOS 13.0 / iPadOS 13.0 / Mac Catalyst 13.0 / macOS 10.15 / tvOS 13.0 / visionOS 1.0 / watchOS 6.0

properties.name: style
properties.notes: Applied via .textFieldStyle(_:). roundedBorder is the common boxed look; squareBorder is macOS-only.

properties.name: titleKey
properties.notes: Label / placeholder. Used as placeholder text when no separate prompt is supplied.

properties.name: text
properties.notes: Bound string updated continuously as the user types. For non-string values use the value:format: initializer instead.

properties.name: prompt
properties.notes: Optional guidance text. In a Form, macOS keeps the label at the leading edge and uses the prompt as placeholder; iOS shows the prompt as placeholder.

properties.name: axis
properties.notes: iOS 16+/macOS 13+: pass axis: .vertical to let the field grow into multiple lines as text wraps. Pair with lineLimit(_:reservesSpace:).

properties.name: keyboardType
properties.notes: iOS/iPadOS only, via .keyboardType(_:). Not available on macOS/tvOS.

properties.name: textContentType
properties.notes: Drives autofill suggestions via .textContentType(_:).

properties.name: submitLabel
properties.notes: Return-key label via .submitLabel(_:). Handle the action with .onSubmit { }.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: Drives .disabled(!isEnabled).

components.higNotes: Use TextField for short, single-line input. Provide a clear label or prompt; in Forms the placement of label vs. prompt differs by platform. Manage focus with @FocusState and .focused(_:); commit edits with .onSubmit. Set keyboardType, textContentType, textInputAutocapitalization, and autocorrectionDisabled to match the data being entered. Use axis: .vertical (iOS 16+) for input that may wrap to multiple lines instead of reaching for TextEditor. Bind non-string types with the value:format: initializer so validation happens on commit.
components.codeTemplate:
TextField(
    "{titleKey}",
    text: {text},
    prompt: {prompt},
    axis: .{axis}
)
.textFieldStyle(.{style})
.keyboardType({keyboardType})
.textContentType({textContentType})
.submitLabel({submitLabel})
.focused($isFocused)
.onSubmit { submit() }
.disabled({isDisabled})

components.title: SecureField
components.summary: A control into which people securely enter private text such as passwords; behaves like TextField but masks the entered characters.
components.minOS: iOS 13.0 / iPadOS 13.0 / Mac Catalyst 13.0 / macOS 10.15 / tvOS 13.0 / visionOS 1.0 / watchOS 6.0

properties.name: style
properties.notes: Shares TextFieldStyle with TextField, applied via .textFieldStyle(_:).

properties.name: titleKey
properties.notes: Label / placeholder text.

properties.name: text
properties.notes: Bound string updated on every keystroke; characters are visually masked.

properties.name: prompt
properties.notes: Optional guidance text; same label-vs-prompt placement rules as TextField.

properties.name: textContentType
properties.notes: Use .password / .newPassword to enable Passwords autofill and Strong Password suggestions; .oneTimeCode for SMS codes.

properties.name: submitLabel
properties.notes: Return-key label via .submitLabel(_:); handle with .onSubmit.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: Drives .disabled(!isEnabled).

components.higNotes: Use SecureField only for sensitive text (passwords, PINs). Set textContentType to .password or .newPassword so the system offers AutoFill and strong-password generation; use .oneTimeCode for verification codes. Always pair with a visible username TextField for AutoFill to associate credentials. Never log or persist the bound string in plaintext. Manage focus and submission the same way as TextField.

components.title: TextEditor
components.summary: A view that displays and edits long-form, multiline, scrollable text bound to a String (or, on newer OSes, an AttributedString for rich text).
components.minOS: iOS 14.0 / iPadOS 14.0 / Mac Catalyst 14.0 / macOS 11.0 / visionOS 1.0
styles: default (no TextEditorStyle protocol; styled via environment modifiers like .font, .foregroundStyle, .lineSpacing)

properties.name: text
properties.notes: Bound multiline string. iOS 26 / macOS adds an AttributedString-bound initializer for rich text editing.

properties.name: font
properties.notes: Inherited from environment via .font(_:); TextEditor has no dedicated style protocol.

properties.name: lineSpacing
properties.notes: Spacing between lines via .lineSpacing(_:). Range 0...20 typical.

properties.name: foregroundStyle
properties.notes: Text color via .foregroundStyle(_:).

properties.name: scrollContentBackground
properties.notes: Use .scrollContentBackground(.hidden) plus .background(_:) to recolor the editor's default container background.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: Drives .disabled(!isEnabled).

components.higNotes: Use TextEditor for multiline, free-form, scrollable text (notes, comments, descriptions). It inherits styling from the environment — there is no TextEditorStyle protocol, so configure appearance with .font, .foregroundStyle, .lineSpacing, and .multilineTextAlignment. There is no built-in placeholder; overlay a Text prompt when the binding is empty. Not available on tvOS or watchOS. For short input that merely wraps, prefer TextField(axis: .vertical) instead. Recolor the container with .scrollContentBackground(.hidden) + .background(_:).
components.codeTemplate:
TextEditor(text: {text})
    .font(.{font})
    .foregroundStyle({foregroundStyle})
    .lineSpacing({lineSpacing})
    .scrollContentBackground(.{scrollContentBackground})
    .frame(minHeight: 120)
    .disabled({isDisabled})

components.title: HStack
components.summary: A view that arranges its subviews in a horizontal line.
components.higNotes: Use for laying out a small, known number of subviews in a row. It eagerly loads all children, so do not use for long scrollable content (use LazyHStack inside a horizontal ScrollView instead). Respect Dynamic Type by letting content reflow; consider ViewThatFits for adaptive layouts.

properties.name: alignment
properties.notes: Guides vertical alignment of subviews relative to each other.

properties.name: spacing
properties.notes: Distance between adjacent subviews. nil uses the platform default spacing.

components.title: VStack
components.summary: A view that arranges its subviews in a vertical line.
components.higNotes: Use for a small, known number of vertically stacked subviews. Eagerly loads all children; for long scrollable lists use LazyVStack inside a ScrollView or a List. Keep alignment consistent with reading direction.

properties.name: alignment
properties.notes: Guides horizontal alignment of subviews relative to each other. Custom alignment guides also supported.

properties.name: spacing
properties.notes: Distance between adjacent subviews. nil uses the platform default spacing.

components.title: ZStack
components.summary: A view that overlays its subviews, aligning them on both axes.
components.higNotes: Use to layer content (e.g. a background behind foreground). Later subviews draw on top of earlier ones. Prefer .background/.overlay modifiers for two-element layering as they do not affect the layout of the primary view.

properties.name: alignment
properties.notes: Aligns subviews on both the horizontal and vertical axes.

components.title: LazyVStack
components.summary: A vertical stack that lazily creates its subviews as they become visible inside a ScrollView.
components.higNotes: Use inside a vertical ScrollView for long content where eager VStack would be wasteful. Use pinnedViews to keep Section headers/footers stuck to the edge while scrolling. For row separators, selection, and swipe actions prefer List.

properties.name: alignment
properties.notes: Horizontal alignment of each subview.

properties.name: spacing
properties.notes: Distance between rows. nil uses default spacing.

properties.name: pinnedViews
properties.notes: Which Section header/footer views pin to the scroll bounds while scrolling.

components.title: LazyHStack
components.summary: A horizontal stack that lazily creates its subviews as they scroll into view.
components.higNotes: Use inside a horizontal ScrollView for carousels and long horizontal content. Combine with .scrollTargetLayout() and .scrollTargetBehavior(.viewAligned) for snapping carousels.

properties.name: alignment
properties.notes: Vertical alignment of each subview.

properties.name: spacing
properties.notes: Distance between columns. nil uses default spacing.

properties.name: pinnedViews
properties.notes: Which Section header/footer views pin to the leading/trailing edge while scrolling.

components.title: Grid
components.summary: A container that arranges other views in a two-dimensional layout, aligning cells across rows and columns.
components.higNotes: Use for a small, fixed grid where columns must align across rows (like a form or spreadsheet-style layout). Loads all cells eagerly; for large scrollable grids use LazyVGrid/LazyHGrid. Use .gridCellColumns() to span and .gridCellAnchor() for per-cell alignment.

properties.name: alignment
properties.notes: Default alignment for cells that do not specify their own anchor.

properties.name: horizontalSpacing
properties.notes: Horizontal distance between columns. nil uses default spacing.

properties.name: verticalSpacing
properties.notes: Vertical distance between rows. nil uses default spacing.

components.title: GridRow
components.summary: A horizontal row in a two-dimensional Grid container.
components.higNotes: Place GridRow directly inside a Grid. Views not wrapped in a GridRow span the full grid width (useful for dividers or section headers). Use .gridCellColumns(n) on a child to span multiple columns.

properties.name: alignment
properties.notes: Overrides the parent Grid's vertical alignment for this row only.

components.title: LazyVGrid
components.summary: A grid that grows vertically, lazily creating items as needed from an array of column definitions.
components.higNotes: Use inside a vertical ScrollView for photo grids and collections. Columns are defined with GridItem (.adaptive, .flexible, .fixed). Adaptive packs as many items as fit per row; flexible distributes available width; fixed pins exact widths.

properties.name: columnSizing
properties.notes: Each GridItem can be .adaptive(minimum:maximum:), .flexible(minimum:maximum:), or .fixed(size). Count of items determines column count for flexible/fixed.

properties.name: gridItemSpacing
properties.notes: Per-GridItem spacing argument: trailing spacing between this and the next column.

properties.name: alignment
properties.notes: Horizontal alignment of the grid within its container.

properties.name: spacing
properties.notes: Vertical spacing between rows of the grid.

properties.name: pinnedViews
properties.notes: Which Section headers/footers pin while scrolling.

components.title: LazyHGrid
components.summary: A grid that grows horizontally, lazily creating items as needed from an array of row definitions.
components.higNotes: Use inside a horizontal ScrollView. Rows are defined with GridItem the same way columns are for LazyVGrid. Good for horizontally scrolling multi-row shelves.

properties.name: rowSizing
properties.notes: Each GridItem defines a row: .adaptive, .flexible, or .fixed.

properties.name: gridItemSpacing
properties.notes: Per-GridItem spacing between this and the next row.

properties.name: alignment
properties.notes: Vertical alignment of the grid within its container.

properties.name: spacing
properties.notes: Horizontal spacing between columns of the grid.

properties.name: pinnedViews
properties.notes: Which Section headers/footers pin while scrolling.

components.title: GridItem
components.summary: A description of a single column (or row) sizing rule for a lazy grid.
components.higNotes: Compose arrays of GridItem to define LazyVGrid columns / LazyHGrid rows. .adaptive is best for responsive photo grids; .flexible for even distribution; .fixed for exact sizes. Provide a minimum to avoid overcrowding on small screens.

properties.name: size
properties.notes: The sizing strategy for this track.

properties.name: spacing
properties.notes: Spacing to the next track. nil uses default.

properties.name: alignment
properties.notes: Alignment of items placed within this track.

components.codeTemplate: GridItem(.{size}, spacing: {spacing}, alignment: {alignment})

components.title: ScrollView
components.summary: A scrollable container that lets users move through content larger than the visible area.
components.higNotes: Pair with LazyVStack/LazyHStack for long content. Use scrollPosition + scrollTargetLayout for programmatic scrolling, and scrollTargetBehavior(.paging or .viewAligned) for snapping. Use scrollIndicators to hide/show indicators, and scrollClipDisabled for overflowing decorations. Avoid nesting same-axis scroll views.

properties.name: axes
properties.notes: Scroll directions the view allows.

properties.name: showsIndicators
properties.notes: Legacy init parameter. Prefer the .scrollIndicators(_:) modifier on iOS 16+.

properties.name: scrollIndicators
properties.notes: .scrollIndicators(_:) modifier (iOS 16+).

properties.name: scrollTargetBehavior
properties.notes: .scrollTargetBehavior(_:) (iOS 17+). viewAligned requires .scrollTargetLayout() on the inner lazy stack.

properties.name: scrollBounceBehavior
properties.notes: .scrollBounceBehavior(_:axes:) (iOS 16.4+).

properties.name: scrollDisabled
properties.notes: .scrollDisabled(_:) (iOS 16+).

properties.name: scrollClipDisabled
properties.notes: .scrollClipDisabled(_:) (iOS 17+). Lets content draw outside the scroll bounds.

properties.name: scrollPosition
properties.notes: .scrollPosition(_:anchor:) (iOS 18+) drives and reads scroll offset programmatically. iOS 17 used scrollPosition(id:).

properties.name: defaultScrollAnchor
properties.notes: .defaultScrollAnchor(_:) (iOS 17+) sets where content is initially scrolled; documented as the primary initial-position control. Missing from the draft.

properties.name: scrollPositionID
properties.notes: .scrollPosition(id:) (iOS 17+) — the id-based variant. The draft only lists the iOS 18 ScrollPosition binding; the id-based form is the widely used one and should be its own property/note, not folded into a footnote.

properties.name: scrollIndicatorsFlash
properties.notes: .scrollIndicatorsFlash(onAppear:) / (trigger:) (iOS 17+) briefly flashes indicators to hint scrollability.

components.title: ScrollViewReader
components.summary: A view that provides programmatic scrolling to a target view identified by ID via a ScrollViewProxy.
components.higNotes: Use when you must scroll to a specific child by id with proxy.scrollTo(id:anchor:). On iOS 18+ prefer the scrollPosition(_:) binding for offset/edge tracking; keep ScrollViewReader for id-targeted jumps. Wrap the ScrollView, not the inner content.

properties.name: targetAnchor
properties.notes: Anchor passed to proxy.scrollTo(_:anchor:) to position the target within the viewport.

components.title: List
components.summary: A container that presents rows of data in a single scrollable column, with native row styling, selection, and editing.
components.higNotes: Prefer List over a ScrollView+LazyVStack when you need separators, selection, swipe actions, or edit mode. Use insetGrouped for settings-style screens, sidebar for source lists / NavigationSplitView columns. Provide ContentUnavailableView for the empty state. Each interactive row needs an accessibility label.

properties.name: style
properties.notes: .listStyle(_:). insetGrouped/grouped iOS-only; bordered macOS-only; carousel/elliptical watchOS-only; sidebar all but watchOS.

properties.name: selectionMode
properties.notes: Single binds to ID?, multiple binds to Set<ID>. Multi-select on iOS appears in edit mode.

properties.name: editMode
properties.notes: Drive via .environment(\.editMode) or an EditButton. Enables reorder (onMove) and delete (onDelete).

properties.name: rowSeparatorVisibility
properties.notes: .listRowSeparator(_:edges:).

properties.name: rowSeparatorTint
properties.notes: .listRowSeparatorTint(_:edges:).

properties.name: rowSpacing
properties.notes: .listRowSpacing(_:) (iOS 17+).

properties.name: sectionSpacing
properties.notes: .listSectionSpacing(_:) (iOS 17+). Accepts .default, .compact, or a CGFloat.

properties.name: rowBackground
properties.notes: .listRowBackground(_:) on each row.

properties.name: swipeActionsEnabled
properties.notes: .swipeActions(edge:allowsFullSwipe:) on rows. Leading/trailing edges; mark destructive with role: .destructive.

properties.name: refreshable
properties.notes: .refreshable { } adds pull-to-refresh.

components.title: Table
components.summary: A container presenting rows of data in one or more sortable, selectable columns.
components.higNotes: Best on macOS and regular-width iPad for multi-column data. On iPhone / compact width it collapses to the first column only and hides headers, so design the first column to carry the essential info. Provide selection and sortOrder bindings for interactivity; re-sort your data in onChange of the sort order.

properties.name: style
properties.notes: .tableStyle(_:). bordered is macOS-only. inset supports alternatesRowBackgrounds:.

properties.name: selectionMode
properties.notes: Single binds Value.ID?, multiple binds Set<Value.ID>.

properties.name: sortOrder
properties.notes: Providing a sortOrder binding makes value-backed columns tappable to sort. Re-sort data on change.

properties.name: columnCustomization
properties.notes: .tableColumnHeaders and columnCustomization(_:) let users hide/reorder columns (macOS, iPadOS 17+).

properties.name: columnHeadersVisibility
properties.notes: .tableColumnHeaders(_:).

components.title: TableColumn
components.summary: A column that displays a value or custom cell for each row of a Table, optionally sortable.
components.higNotes: Use a value-keypath column for plain text plus free sorting. Use a content-closure column for custom cells (badges, images). Set width/min/max/ideal to control sizing on macOS; use .defaultVisibility to start columns hidden.

properties.name: title
properties.notes: Header text for the column.

properties.name: value
properties.notes: Key path used for sorting when a sortOrder binding is present.

properties.name: width
properties.notes: .width(_:) / .width(min:ideal:max:). Primarily affects macOS.

properties.name: defaultVisibility
properties.notes: .defaultVisibility(_:) for user-customizable columns.

components.codeTemplate:
TableColumn("{title}", value: {value}) { row in
    Text(row.title)
}
.width({width})
.defaultVisibility(.{defaultVisibility})

components.title: Form
components.summary: A container for grouping controls used for data entry, with platform-appropriate styling.
components.higNotes: Use Form for settings and data-entry screens; it styles labels, controls, and sections natively per platform. On macOS use .formStyle(.grouped) for the modern card-grouped look. Use LabeledContent for read-only key/value rows. Group related fields into Sections with headers.

properties.name: style
properties.notes: .formStyle(_:) (iOS 16+/macOS 13+). grouped gives the inset card look; columns aligns labels and fields (macOS).

components.title: Section
components.summary: A grouping of content with an optional header and footer, used inside List, Form, Table, Picker, and lazy stacks.
components.higNotes: Use Sections to group related rows and give them headers/footers. On iOS 17+ use isExpanded for collapsible sidebar sections. Header text is automatically styled per the container's list style. Use .headerProminence(.increased) for a more prominent header.

properties.name: header
properties.notes: Header label; can also be a full view via the header: trailing closure.

properties.name: footer
properties.notes: Footer label; commonly used for explanatory captions.

properties.name: isExpanded
properties.notes: Collapsible section (iOS 17+). Provide a Bool binding to expand/collapse.

properties.name: headerProminence
properties.notes: .headerProminence(_:). Increased renders a larger, bolder header.

components.title: GroupBox
components.summary: A stylized container that visually groups a label with related content in a bordered card.
components.higNotes: Use GroupBox to set off a chunk of related controls or information with a subtle background and optional title, outside of a List/Form. Common in settings panes and inspectors. Not available on tvOS/watchOS. Provide a clear label for the group.

properties.name: label
properties.notes: Title shown above the grouped content; omit for an unlabeled card.

properties.name: style
properties.notes: .groupBoxStyle(_:). Only the automatic system style ships; custom styles can be defined.

components.title: DisclosureGroup
components.summary: A container that shows or hides its content behind a disclosure control bound to an expansion state.
components.higNotes: Use for progressive disclosure of secondary content (advanced settings, nested outlines). Bind isExpanded for programmatic control. Not on tvOS/watchOS. Animate expansion with withAnimation when toggling the binding. Keep labels concise.

properties.name: label
properties.notes: Title of the disclosure row; can also be a full view.

properties.name: isExpanded
properties.notes: Two-way binding for expansion state. Omit to let it manage state internally.

properties.name: style
properties.notes: .disclosureGroupStyle(_:). Only the automatic system style ships.

components.title: Group
components.summary: A transparent container that collects views together so modifiers and the ten-child limit can be applied collectively, without affecting layout.
components.higNotes: Use Group to apply a shared modifier to several views or to exceed the implicit child limit inside a container. Group itself imposes no layout; it inherits the layout behavior of its parent container.
components.codeTemplate:
Group {
    Text("One")
    Text("Two")
    Text("Three")
}
.font(.headline)
.foregroundStyle(.secondary)

components.title: ViewThatFits
components.summary: A container that picks the first child view that fits the available space along the specified axes.
components.higNotes: Provide candidate layouts from most-expansive to most-compact; ViewThatFits renders the first one that fits. Ideal for adaptive labels, toolbars, and responsive layouts that must collapse on small screens or large Dynamic Type sizes. Limit candidate count to keep evaluation cheap.

properties.name: axes
properties.notes: Which dimensions are measured for the fit test.

components.title: Spacer
components.summary: A flexible space that expands along the major axis of its containing stack, pushing adjacent views apart.
components.higNotes: Use inside HStack/VStack to push content to the edges. Set minLength to guarantee a minimum gap. A Spacer outside a stack expands in both directions. Avoid using many Spacers to fake alignment; prefer alignment, padding, or frame for precise layout.

properties.name: minLength
properties.notes: Minimum length the spacer will occupy even when space is constrained. nil uses default spacing.

components.title: Divider
components.summary: A visual line that separates content; horizontal inside a VStack, vertical inside an HStack.
components.higNotes: Orientation is inferred from the enclosing stack. Use to break up grouped content outside a List (List provides its own separators). Tint with .overlay or .background sparingly; the default uses the system separator color for correct contrast in light and dark mode.

properties.name: tint
properties.notes: Applied via .overlay(color) or foreground style. nil keeps the adaptive system separator color (recommended).

components.title: LabeledContent
components.summary: A container pairing a label with a value or content view, styled consistently inside Forms and Lists.
components.higNotes: Use for read-only key/value rows in a Form or settings list (e.g. Version: 1.0). It applies the platform label-and-value layout and respects label-hidden contexts. Prefer it over a manual HStack + Spacer for these rows.

properties.name: label
properties.notes: Leading descriptive text.

properties.name: value
properties.notes: Trailing value. Can also be a full content view via the trailing closure.

properties.name: labelHidden
properties.notes: .labelsHidden() hides the label visually while keeping it for accessibility.

components.codeTemplate: LabeledContent("{label}", value: "{value}")

components.title: GeometryReader
components.summary: A container view that defines its content as a function of its own size and coordinate space, exposing a GeometryProxy for reading dimensions, safe-area insets, and frames.
components.minOS: iOS 13.0 / iPadOS 13.0 / macOS 10.15 / tvOS 13.0 / watchOS 6.0 / visionOS 1.0
components.higNotes: Use sparingly. GeometryReader returns a flexible preferred size to its parent and greedily fills available space, which frequently breaks surrounding layout; prefer ViewThatFits, the Layout protocol, containerRelativeFrame, or onGeometryChange(for:of:action:) (iOS 18+) where possible. Read proxy.size, proxy.frame(in:), and proxy.safeAreaInsets. Note GeometryReader3D for visionOS depth. Common modern replacement for size-reactive logic is the lighter onGeometryChange.

properties.name: coordinateSpace
properties.notes: Space passed to proxy.frame(in:). .local is the reader's own space; .global is the screen; .named(_:) targets an ancestor tagged with .coordinateSpace(_:).

properties.name: readsSafeArea
properties.notes: Whether the example reads proxy.safeAreaInsets. Demonstrates inset-aware layout.

components.codeTemplate:
GeometryReader { proxy in
    Color.accentColor
        .overlay(Text("w: \(Int(proxy.size.width)) h: \(Int(proxy.size.height))"))
        .frame(width: proxy.size.width, height: proxy.size.height)
}

components.title: Layout (custom layout)
components.summary: A protocol for defining a custom layout container by implementing sizeThatFits and placeSubviews, letting you arrange subviews with fully custom geometry like flow layouts or radial arrangements.
components.minOS: iOS 16.0 / iPadOS 16.0 / macOS 13.0 / tvOS 16.0 / watchOS 9.0 / visionOS 1.0
components.higNotes: Conform a value type to Layout and implement sizeThatFits(proposal:subviews:cache:) and placeSubviews(in:proposal:subviews:cache:). Use it the same way as HStack/VStack via a ViewBuilder. Read each subview proxy's sizeThatFits, dimensions(in:), priority, and spacing; place with subview.place(at:anchor:proposal:). Add a cache for expensive measurements, expose layoutProperties for axis, and use AnyLayout to animate between two layouts. Prefer the Layout protocol over GeometryReader hacks for custom arrangements such as flow/wrapping/tag layouts.

properties.name: exampleLayout
properties.notes: Which custom layout the showcase demonstrates.

properties.name: spacing
properties.notes: Inter-subview spacing the custom layout applies between placed subviews.

properties.name: animatesBetweenLayouts
properties.notes: When true, wraps the chosen layout in AnyLayout to animate transitions between two layout types.

components.title: ControlGroup
components.summary: A container that displays semantically related controls grouped together in a way appropriate for the surrounding context, such as a segmented cluster in a toolbar or an overflow menu.
components.higNotes: Use to cluster a small set of related controls (commonly buttons) so the system styles and spaces them as a unit. In a toolbar, the optional label is used when the group collapses into the overflow menu. .palette / .compactMenu styles render the children as a compact picker-like cluster. Not available on watchOS.

properties.name: style
properties.notes: .controlGroupStyle(_:). palette/compactMenu available iOS 17+; navigation styles affect toolbar grouping.

properties.name: label
properties.notes: Optional label used when the group is moved into a toolbar overflow menu.

components.title: OutlineGroup
components.summary: A container that computes views and nested DisclosureGroups on demand from tree-structured, identified data, rendering an expandable/collapsible hierarchy.
components.higNotes: Use for hierarchical data (file trees, nested categories) where each node can expand/collapse. Provide the root collection and the children key path. For a scrollable hierarchical list with selection, prefer List(_:children:) which builds outline rows automatically. Not available on tvOS/watchOS. Each node's data must be Identifiable/Hashable.

properties.name: childrenKeyPath
properties.notes: Optional key path to a node's children. nil children mark leaf nodes.

properties.name: startsExpanded
properties.notes: Whether branches render initially expanded in the showcase.

components.title: TableRow
components.summary: A row that represents a single data value within a Table built from static rows.
components.higNotes: Use TableRow inside a Table's rows: closure when you build a table from static, hand-listed rows (Table(of:columns:rows:)) rather than from a collection. The row's value is matched against each TableColumn's content/value. Combine with TableRowContent and Group/ForEach to build static row sets. macOS/iPadOS/iOS only.

properties.name: value
properties.notes: The data value this row represents; columns read their content from it.

components.title: NavigationStack
components.summary: Push-based navigation container that displays a root view and a stack of value-driven destination views.
components.higNotes: Use a single NavigationStack per column/tab. Drive destinations with value-based NavigationLink + navigationDestination, never with deprecated NavigationView. Keep the back affordance system-provided. On iOS 26 the bar adopts Liquid Glass automatically.

properties.name: pathMode
properties.notes: none = init(root:); typedArray = init(path: Binding<[Data]>); navigationPath = init(path: Binding<NavigationPath>) for heterogeneous deep links.

properties.name: depth
properties.notes: Number of pre-pushed destinations to demonstrate stack depth and back navigation. Range 0...6.

properties.name: navigationTitle
properties.notes: Applied to the root content via .navigationTitle(_:).

properties.name: titleDisplayMode
properties.notes: iOS/iPadOS/watchOS only via .navigationBarTitleDisplayMode(_:). Large is unavailable on macOS.

properties.name: barHidden
properties.notes: Drives .toolbar(.hidden, for: .navigationBar) — hides the navigation bar.

properties.name: barBackgroundVisibility
properties.notes: .toolbarBackgroundVisibility(_:for: .navigationBar). On iOS 26 controls the Liquid Glass bar background.

properties.name: backButtonHidden
properties.notes: Applied to a pushed destination via .navigationBarBackButtonHidden(_:).

properties.name: scrollEdgeEffectStyle
properties.notes: MISSING (iOS 26): .scrollEdgeEffectStyle(_:for:) tunes how the Liquid Glass navigation bar reacts to scrolled-under content. Relevant to demonstrating the iOS 26 bar the higNotes already mention.

components.title: NavigationPath
components.summary: Type-erased, heterogeneous navigation state container for programmatic, codable deep-link navigation.
components.higNotes: Use NavigationPath when a single stack pushes more than one value type or when you need state restoration. Persist via its CodableRepresentation when every element is Codable & Hashable.

properties.name: elementCount
properties.notes: Demonstrates append/removeLast. Reflects path.count. Range 0...8.

properties.name: mixedTypes
properties.notes: When true, appends both Int and String values to show heterogeneous routing.

properties.name: codablePersistence
properties.notes: Encodes/decodes via path.codable (NavigationPath.CodableRepresentation) for state restoration.

components.title: navigationDestination
components.summary: Associates a data type or boolean/item trigger with a destination view inside a NavigationStack.
components.higNotes: Declare navigationDestination once per type, attached inside the stack's view hierarchy (not on the stack itself). Prefer for:destination: with value links over isPresented:/item: triggers for back-button correctness.

properties.name: triggerMode
properties.notes: forType = navigationDestination(for:); isPresented = navigationDestination(isPresented:); item = navigationDestination(item:).

properties.name: isPresented
properties.notes: Only used when triggerMode is isPresented. Pushes a single destination.

properties.name: itemBinding
properties.notes: AVAILABILITY: the navigationDestination(item:destination:) variant the triggerMode 'item' references is iOS 17.0+ / macOS 14.0+, NOT iOS 16. The for: and isPresented: variants are iOS 16+. The component minOS of iOS 16 is fine for the component, but the 'item' trigger should be gated to iOS 17+.

components.title: NavigationLink
components.summary: A control that triggers a push by presenting a value (stack-driven) or, legacy, a destination view directly.
components.higNotes: Prefer the value-based initializer (init(_:value:)) with a matching navigationDestination. The destination-view initializer is legacy and discouraged inside NavigationStack. Inside a List with selection, NavigationLink also drives NavigationSplitView column selection.

properties.name: linkMode
properties.notes: value = init(_:value:) (recommended); destination = init(destination:) (legacy direct view).

properties.name: label
properties.notes: Visible link text.

properties.name: useCustomLabel
properties.notes: When true, uses a Label with systemImage instead of plain text.

properties.name: systemImage
properties.notes: SF Symbol name for the custom label variant.

properties.name: isDisabled
properties.notes: Applied via .disabled(_:).

components.title: NavigationSplitView
components.summary: Two- or three-column container where leading-column selections drive subsequent columns; collapses to a stack in compact widths.
components.higNotes: Use as the root view on iPad/Mac for list-detail apps. Bind List selection to coordinate columns. Use columnVisibility for programmatic control and preferredCompactColumn to pick the column shown when collapsed. Remove the default sidebarToggle with .toolbar(removing:) only when justified.

properties.name: columnCount
properties.notes: two = init(sidebar:detail:); three = init(sidebar:content:detail:).

properties.name: style
properties.notes: .navigationSplitViewStyle(_:). balanced gives equal prominence; prominentDetail keeps detail full width when sidebar shows.

properties.name: columnVisibility
properties.notes: Binding passed to init(columnVisibility:...). Ignored when collapsed to a stack.

properties.name: preferredCompactColumn
properties.notes: Binding passed to init(preferredCompactColumn:...). Picks the top column when collapsed (iPhone/Slide Over).

properties.name: sidebarColumnWidth
properties.notes: .navigationSplitViewColumnWidth(_:) on the sidebar. Range 180...420.

properties.name: sidebarTitle
properties.notes: navigationTitle on the sidebar List.

components.title: NavigationSplitView (three-column)
components.summary: Three-column sidebar/content/detail layout where each selection cascades to the next column.
components.higNotes: Use the three-column form for hierarchical apps (e.g. account > mailbox > message). Sidebar single-selection drives content; content selection drives detail. Provide placeholder views for empty content and detail columns.

properties.name: style
properties.notes: .navigationSplitViewStyle(_:).

properties.name: columnVisibility
properties.notes: Three-column visibility control. all shows every column; doubleColumn hides the sidebar.

properties.name: contentColumnWidthMin
properties.notes: min of .navigationSplitViewColumnWidth(min:ideal:max:) on content. Range 150...320.

components.title: TabView
components.summary: Top-level container switching between child views via a tab bar; on iOS 26 supports Liquid Glass bar and minimize-on-scroll.
components.higNotes: Use the iOS 18+ Tab/TabSection value API over the legacy .tabItem modifier. Limit to 5 primary tabs on iPhone; overflow goes to More. Assign a .search role Tab on iOS 26 for the dedicated search experience. Use sidebarAdaptable on iPad/Mac for sidebar-promotable hierarchies.

properties.name: tabCount
properties.notes: Number of Tab items. Range 2...6.

properties.name: style
properties.notes: .tabViewStyle(_:). page = .page; pageNoIndicator = .page(indexDisplayMode: .never); grouped is macOS-only; sidebarAdaptable is iOS18+.

properties.name: programmaticSelection
properties.notes: When true uses init(selection:) with value-bearing Tab items.

properties.name: selectedTab
properties.notes: Drives the selection binding.

properties.name: showBadges
properties.notes: Adds .badge(_:) to tabs (iOS only).

properties.name: tabBarMinimizeBehavior
properties.notes: iOS 26+. .tabBarMinimizeBehavior(_:) collapses the Liquid Glass tab bar while scrolling.

properties.name: useSearchRole
properties.notes: iOS 26+. Adds a Tab(role: .search) for the bottom-anchored search tab.

properties.name: defaultAdaptableTabBarPlacement
properties.notes: MISSING: .defaultAdaptableTabBarPlacement(_:) picks whether a .sidebarAdaptable TabView starts as a sidebar or tab bar on iPad/Mac. Complements the existing sidebarAdaptable style option.

components.title: Tab
components.summary: Declarative tab content item for TabView, carrying a label, optional value, and an optional system role.
components.higNotes: Prefer Tab over .tabItem. Use a stable value for selection. Use role: .search for the standardized search tab (iOS 18+, prominent on iOS 26). Provide a clear SF Symbol and concise title.

properties.name: title
properties.notes: Tab label text.

properties.name: systemImage
properties.notes: SF Symbol for the tab.

properties.name: role
properties.notes: TabRole.search gives the system search tab treatment.

properties.name: badge
properties.notes: .badge(_:); 0 hides the badge. Range 0...99.

properties.name: customizationID
properties.notes: .customizationID(_:) required for sidebarAdaptable customization persistence.

components.title: TabSection
components.summary: Groups secondary tabs into a labeled section for the sidebarAdaptable TabView style.
components.higNotes: Use TabSection only with .tabViewStyle(.sidebarAdaptable). Sections render as sidebar groups on iPad/Mac and as flat secondary tabs on compact iOS (header omitted). Give each section a customizationID for reordering persistence.

properties.name: header
properties.notes: Section header shown in the sidebar.

properties.name: childCount
properties.notes: Number of nested Tab items. Range 1...6.

properties.name: customizationID
properties.notes: .customizationID(_:) on the section.

components.title: TabView bottom accessory
components.summary: Attaches a persistent accessory view (e.g. now-playing bar) above the Liquid Glass tab bar.
components.higNotes: Use for a single persistent transport/now-playing style control. Read TabViewBottomAccessoryPlacement from the environment to adapt layout when the tab bar minimizes. Keep the accessory compact and glass-compatible.

properties.name: enabled
properties.notes: Toggles presence of the accessory.

properties.name: label
properties.notes: Accessory primary text.

properties.name: platformsCorrection
properties.notes: PLATFORM CORRECTION: draft lists platforms [iOS, iPadOS, macOS, visionOS]. tabViewBottomAccessory(content:) is iOS 26 / iPadOS 26 / Mac Catalyst 26 ONLY. Native macOS and visionOS are NOT supported. Change platforms to [iOS, iPadOS].

components.title: Toolbar
components.summary: Populates navigation bar, bottom bar, and platform toolbars with placement-based items.
components.higNotes: Prefer semantic placements (.confirmationAction, .cancellationAction, .primaryAction, .topBarLeading/Trailing) over hardcoded positions.
