The system manages overflow into a menu when items do not fit. On iOS 26 toolbar items render on Liquid Glass; group related items and separate with ToolbarSpacer.

properties.name: primaryPlacement
properties.notes: Placement of the leading ToolbarItem. topBarLeading/topBarTrailing/bottomBar are iOS-family; principal places a custom title view.

properties.name: itemCount
properties.notes: Number of trailing action items. Range 1...5.

properties.name: showPrincipal
properties.notes: Adds a ToolbarItem(placement: .principal) with a custom title view.

properties.name: showBottomBar
properties.notes: Adds a .bottomBar ToolbarItemGroup (iOS/iPadOS).

properties.name: useGroup
properties.notes: Wraps trailing items in a ToolbarItemGroup vs separate ToolbarItems.

properties.name: itemsDisabled
properties.notes: Disables the action items.

components.title: ToolbarItem
components.summary: A single placed toolbar element, optionally identified for customization.
components.higNotes: Use one ToolbarItem per discrete action. Provide an id when supporting customizable toolbars (toolbar(id:)). Give image-only buttons an accessibility label.

properties.name: placement
properties.notes: Where the item appears. keyboard places above the software keyboard (iOS).

properties.name: label
properties.notes: Button title.

properties.name: customizationID
properties.notes: Sets ToolbarItem(id:) for customizable toolbars; empty for non-customizable.

components.title: ToolbarItemGroup
components.summary: Groups multiple controls under a single placement, so the system lays them out and overflows together.
components.higNotes: Group logically related actions. The system inserts a glass capsule background around the group on iOS 26. Items overflow into a menu together when space is tight.

properties.name: placement
properties.notes: Shared placement for the group.

properties.name: itemCount
properties.notes: Number of buttons in the group. Range 2...5.

components.title: ToolbarSpacer
components.summary: Inserts a fixed or flexible space between toolbar items, separating glass item groups on iOS 26.
components.higNotes: Use ToolbarSpacer(.fixed) to break two glass capsules apart and .flexible to push items to opposite ends. In customizable toolbars users can add/remove/duplicate spacers.

properties.name: sizing
properties.notes: .fixed = standard gap separating glass groups; .flexible = expands to push items apart.

properties.name: platformsNote
properties.notes: VERIFIED: ToolbarSpacer is iOS 26 / iPadOS 26 / Mac Catalyst 26 / macOS 26. Correctly excludes tvOS & watchOS. Draft platforms [iOS, iPadOS, macOS] are correct.

components.title: Navigation title
components.summary: Sets the title for the enclosing navigation container, with iOS display modes and an optional editable/menu binding.
components.higNotes: Use a large title for top-level screens and inline for pushed/detail screens. On macOS the title appears in the window title bar. Pair with navigationSubtitle on macOS and an editable binding for document names.

properties.name: title
properties.notes: The navigation title text.

properties.name: displayMode
properties.notes: .navigationBarTitleDisplayMode(_:). iOS/iPadOS/watchOS only; large unavailable on macOS.

properties.name: subtitle
properties.notes: macOS .navigationSubtitle(_:); empty omits it.

properties.name: editable
properties.notes: When true uses navigationTitle($binding) for an inline-editable title (document-style).

properties.name: titleMenu
properties.notes: MISSING: .toolbarTitleMenu(content:) (iOS 16+) attaches a menu opened by tapping the title — a core navigation-title capability the draft omits.

components.codeTemplate:
Text("Content")
    .navigationTitle("{title}")
    .navigationBarTitleDisplayMode(.{displayMode})

components.title: searchable
components.summary: Adds a system search field to a navigation container, with placement, prompt, suggestions, scopes, and tokens.
components.higNotes: Attach to the navigation container (stack or split view), not a child. Use .searchScopes for category filtering and search tokens for structured filters. On iOS 26 prefer a Tab(role: .search) for top-level search; the field adopts the Liquid Glass treatment. Use .searchSuggestions for completion.

properties.name: placement
properties.notes: navigationBarDrawer variants are iOS-only; sidebar targets the split-view sidebar.

properties.name: prompt
properties.notes: Localized placeholder prompt.

properties.name: showSuggestions
properties.notes: Adds a .searchSuggestions { } block with completion rows.

properties.name: showScopes
properties.notes: Adds .searchScopes(_:) { } with a segmented scope bar.

properties.name: scopeActivation
properties.notes: .searchScopes(_:activation:) controls when the scope bar appears.

properties.name: useTokens
properties.notes: Switches to searchable(text:tokens:placement:prompt:token:) for structured filter tokens.

properties.name: isPresented
properties.notes: Uses searchable(text:isPresented:placement:prompt:) to programmatically present/dismiss the field (iOS 17+).

components.title: Search tokens
components.summary: Renders structured, removable filter tokens inside the search field bound to an Identifiable collection.
components.higNotes: Use tokens to represent committed filters (people, tags, scopes) as chips the user can delete. Each token type must be Identifiable. Use the editableTokens variant when tokens carry user-editable text.

properties.name: tokenCount
properties.notes: Number of active filter tokens to display. Range 0...4.

properties.name: editable
properties.notes: Switches to searchable(text:editableTokens:...) for in-place token text editing.

properties.name: prompt
properties.notes: Search field prompt.

components.title: Zoom navigation transition
components.summary: Animates a pushed view zooming from a matched source view, paired with matchedTransitionSource.
components.higNotes: Use .zoom for hero-style transitions from a cell/thumbnail to its detail. Mark the source with matchedTransitionSource(id:in:) using the same id and namespace passed to .zoom. Fall back to .automatic when no source exists.

properties.name: transition
properties.notes: automatic = system default push; zoom = hero zoom requiring a matched source.

properties.name: sourceID
properties.notes: Identifier shared between matchedTransitionSource(id:) and .zoom(sourceID:in:).

components.title: Navigation back button & bar items
components.summary: Controls back-button visibility, a custom back affordance, and the navigation bar's editor/browser role.
components.higNotes: Hide the default back button only when providing an equivalent gesture/control; the swipe-back gesture is disabled when the button is hidden unless re-enabled. Use .toolbarRole(.editor) on iPad to reclaim the back-button label space and center the title.

properties.name: backButtonHidden
properties.notes: .navigationBarBackButtonHidden(_:).

properties.name: toolbarRole
properties.notes: .toolbarRole(_:). editor centers the title and drops the back label on iPad.

properties.name: customBackButton
properties.notes: When hidden, adds a custom .topBarLeading back ToolbarItem with dismiss action.

properties.name: navigationLinkIndicatorVisibility
properties.notes: MISSING (iOS 26): environment value navigationLinkIndicatorVisibility / .navigationLinkIndicatorVisibility(_:) controls the disclosure chevron on NavigationLinks. A meaningful bar/link configuration knob.

components.title: TabView (page style)
components.summary: Horizontally paging TabView with a dot page indicator, commonly used for onboarding and galleries.
components.higNotes: Use the page style for swipeable sequential content, not primary app navigation. Control the index dots with indexDisplayMode. On watchOS this is the vertical paging style. Provide a way to reach each page non-gesturally for accessibility.

properties.name: pageCount
properties.notes: Number of pages. Range 2...8.

properties.name: indexDisplayMode
properties.notes: .page(indexDisplayMode:) controls the dot indicator.

properties.name: backgroundDisplayMode
properties.notes: .indexViewStyle(.page(backgroundDisplayMode:)) sets the index dots' backing.

components.title: Toolbar removing default item
components.summary: Removes a system-provided default toolbar item, most commonly the NavigationSplitView sidebar toggle.
components.higNotes: Use only to remove a default affordance you are deliberately replacing. The most common case is .toolbar(removing: .sidebarToggle) on a NavigationSplitView sidebar when you provide your own column control. Removing the sidebar toggle without a replacement strands users in a collapsed column.

properties.name: defaultItemKind
properties.notes: ToolbarDefaultItemKind to remove. .sidebarToggle removes the NavigationSplitView column toggle; .title (iOS 26) removes the default title item; nil/none keeps all defaults.

components.title: Toolbar title menu
components.summary: Attaches a menu to the navigation title, revealed by tapping the title in the navigation bar.
components.higNotes: Use the title menu for actions that act on the content the title represents (rename, duplicate, move, print). On iOS/iPadOS the menu opens by tapping the navigation title. Pair with navigationDocument for document-based apps so the title shows the document proxy icon.

properties.name: itemCount
properties.notes: Number of menu actions. Range 1...6.

properties.name: useDefaultMenu
properties.notes: When true, places a bare ToolbarTitleMenu() (system-populated) instead of custom content.

components.title: Customizable toolbar
components.summary: A user-customizable toolbar whose items carry stable ids so people can add, remove, reorder, and duplicate them.
components.higNotes: Use toolbar(id:) with id-bearing ToolbarItems for toolbars users can personalize (primarily iPad and Mac). Set ToolbarCustomizationBehavior to pin essential items and ToolbarCustomizationOptions to allow spacer duplication. Non-customizable toolbars should use the plain toolbar(content:).

properties.name: itemCount
properties.notes: Number of id-bearing items. Range 1...6.

properties.name: defaultVisibility
properties.notes: defaultCustomization(_:) controls whether an item appears by default before the user customizes.

properties.name: pinnedBehavior
properties.notes: customizationBehavior(_:) — disabled pins an item so users cannot remove or move it.

components.title: Navigation subtitle
components.summary: Adds a secondary subtitle line alongside the navigation title in the bar (iOS 26) or window title bar (macOS).
components.higNotes: Use a subtitle for short contextual status under the title (e.g. sync state, item count). On macOS the subtitle appears in the window title bar (available since macOS 11); on iOS/iPadOS it appears under the title only from iOS 26. Keep it short — it truncates aggressively. tvOS/watchOS unsupported.

properties.name: title
properties.notes: The primary navigation title.

properties.name: subtitle
properties.notes: .navigationSubtitle(_:) secondary line.

components.title: TabView (sidebar adaptable)
components.summary: A TabView that renders as a tab bar in compact widths and promotes to a sidebar on iPad and Mac, with reorderable sections and customization.
components.higNotes: Use sidebarAdaptable for apps with many destinations that benefit from a sidebar on large screens. Group secondary destinations with TabSection. Enable user reordering with tabViewCustomization and a customizationID per Tab/TabSection. Provide sidebar header/footer via tabViewSidebarHeader/Footer.

properties.name: primaryTabCount
properties.notes: Number of top-level tabs above the section. Range 1...4.

properties.name: sectionChildCount
properties.notes: Number of Tab items inside the TabSection. Range 1...6.

properties.name: enableCustomization
properties.notes: Applies .tabViewCustomization(_:) so users can reorder/hide tabs (persisted via customizationID).

properties.name: showSidebarHeader
properties.notes: Adds .tabViewSidebarHeader { } content shown above the sidebar tabs.

components.title: NavigationView (deprecated)
components.summary: The legacy single- and multi-column navigation container, deprecated in favor of NavigationStack and NavigationSplitView.
components.higNotes: Deprecated since iOS 16. Do not use in new code; it does not adopt value-based navigation, NavigationPath, or programmatic deep links cleanly. Migrate to NavigationStack (single column) or NavigationSplitView (multi-column). Included here only to document the migration target.

properties.name: style
properties.notes: .navigationViewStyle(_:) — all deprecated. stack forces single-column; columns forces multi-column on iPad/Mac.

components.title: Sheet
components.summary: Modally presents a view as a sheet that slides up from the bottom, resizable via detents on iPhone.
components.summaryLong: The canonical modal presentation. Two flavors: isPresented-driven (Bool) and item-driven (Identifiable). Configure size with presentationDetents, chrome with presentationBackground/presentationCornerRadius/presentationDragIndicator, and dismissal with interactiveDismissDisabled.

properties.name: isPresented
properties.notes: Drives presentation. The showroom toggle presents/dismisses the sheet.

properties.name: detents
properties.notes: Multi-select set via .presentationDetents(_:). Multiple detents enable user resizing. iOS 16+.

properties.name: selectedDetent
properties.notes: Optional programmatic selection binding via .presentationDetents(_:selection:). iOS 16+.

properties.name: dragIndicator
properties.notes: .presentationDragIndicator(_:). Shows the grabber. iOS 16+.

properties.name: cornerRadius
properties.notes: .presentationCornerRadius(_:). nil uses system default. iOS 16.4+.

properties.name: background
properties.notes: .presentationBackground(_:). iOS 16.4+. Use opaque fallback under Reduce Transparency.

properties.name: backgroundInteraction
properties.notes: .presentationBackgroundInteraction(_:). Lets the content behind a non-full detent stay interactive. iOS 16.4+.

properties.name: contentInteraction
properties.notes: .presentationContentInteraction(_:). Controls whether a swipe resizes the sheet or scrolls its content. iOS 16.4+.

properties.name: compactAdaptation
properties.notes: .presentationCompactAdaptation(_:). Controls how the presentation adapts in compact size classes. iOS 16.4+.

properties.name: interactiveDismissDisabled
properties.notes: .interactiveDismissDisabled(_:). Blocks swipe-to-dismiss; force an explicit dismiss button.

properties.name: sizing
properties.notes: .presentationSizing(_:). Sizes the sheet to its content on macOS/iPad. iOS 18+, macOS 15+.

components.higNotes: Use sheets for self-contained, non-immersive tasks. Default to .large with a visible drag indicator when multiple detents exist. Always provide an explicit Cancel/Done in the toolbar. Avoid forcing presentationDragIndicator(.visible) when the default already shows it.
components.codeTemplate:
.sheet(isPresented: ${isPresented}) {
    SheetContentView()
        .presentationDetents([${detents}], selection: ${selectedDetent})
        .presentationDragIndicator(${dragIndicator})
        .presentationCornerRadius(${cornerRadius})
        .presentationBackground(${background})
        .presentationBackgroundInteraction(${backgroundInteraction})
        .presentationContentInteraction(${contentInteraction})
        .presentationCompactAdaptation(${compactAdaptation})
        .presentationSizing(${sizing})
        .interactiveDismissDisabled(${interactiveDismissDisabled})
}

components.title: Sheet (Item-driven)
components.summary: Presents a sheet bound to an Identifiable optional, rebuilding content whenever the item identity changes.

properties.name: item
properties.notes: Item must conform to Identifiable. Setting non-nil presents; nil dismisses. Reidentifies content on identity change.

properties.name: onDismiss
properties.notes: Closure run after dismissal. Toggle wires a logging closure in the showroom.

components.higNotes: Prefer the item-driven overload when the sheet content depends on a specific value; it avoids the stale-capture bug common with isPresented + separate state.

components.title: Full Screen Cover
components.summary: Covers the entire screen with a modal view, used for immersive or onboarding flows.
components.summaryLong: Unlike a sheet, this covers the whole screen and is not swipe-dismissable; you must provide an explicit dismiss. Not available on macOS (use sheet/window instead). Both isPresented and item overloads exist.

properties.name: isPresented
properties.notes: Drives presentation.

properties.name: item
properties.notes: Identifiable-driven overload.

properties.name: onDismiss
properties.notes: Runs after the cover dismisses.

properties.name: background
properties.notes: .presentationBackground(_:) works here too. iOS 16.4+.

components.higNotes: Reserve for content that needs the whole screen and a deliberate, explicit exit (onboarding, media, sign-in). Always include a clear dismiss control since there is no swipe-to-dismiss. Not on macOS.

components.title: Popover
components.summary: Presents content in a popover anchored to the source view, adapting to a sheet in compact widths by default.
components.summaryLong: Anchored transient presentation with an arrow. On iPhone (compact) it adapts to a sheet unless overridden with .presentationCompactAdaptation(.popover). Configure the anchor point and arrow direction.

properties.name: isPresented
properties.notes: Drives presentation. Item overload also available.

properties.name: attachmentAnchor
properties.notes: Where the popover attaches relative to the source view.

properties.name: arrowEdge
properties.notes: Edge of the source the arrow points from. nil lets the system choose.

properties.name: compactAdaptation
properties.notes: Use .presentationCompactAdaptation(.popover) to keep a true popover on iPhone. iOS 16.4+.

properties.name: background
properties.notes: .presentationBackground(_:) tints the popover body. iOS 16.4+.

properties.name: breakthroughEffect
properties.notes: .presentationBreakthroughEffect(_:) controls how the popover breaks through occluding content. visionOS only.

components.higNotes: Use popovers on iPad/Mac for transient, contextual content tied to a control. On iPhone they adapt to sheets by default; only force .popover when the content is genuinely small. Keep popover content compact and dismiss on outside tap.

components.title: Alert
components.summary: Presents a modal alert with a title, optional message, action buttons (with roles), and optional text fields.
components.summaryLong: The modern alert API takes an actions builder and a message builder. Buttons carry roles (.destructive, .cancel) that style and position them. TextField/SecureField inside the actions builder add input (iOS 16+). An error-driven overload renders LocalizedError content automatically.

properties.name: title
properties.notes: First positional argument. Keep short and action-oriented.

properties.name: isPresented
properties.notes: Drives presentation.

properties.name: message
properties.notes: Message builder. Provide context not in the title.

properties.name: primaryButtonRole
properties.notes: Role styles the button (destructive = red, cancel = bold/bottom).

properties.name: includesCancel
properties.notes: Adds a Button with role .cancel. A cancel button is auto-added if you provide none.

properties.name: includesTextField
properties.notes: Adds a TextField inside the actions builder for inline input. iOS 16+.

properties.name: errorDriven
properties.notes: Switch to alert(isPresented:error:actions:) which derives title/message from a LocalizedError.

components.higNotes: Alerts interrupt; use only for critical information requiring acknowledgement. Limit to two buttons when possible; three buttons stack vertically. Use .destructive for irreversible actions and .cancel for the safe default. Don't use alerts for routine confirmations - prefer confirmationDialog.

components.title: Confirmation Dialog
components.summary: Presents an action sheet of choices (bottom sheet on iPhone, popover on iPad), replacing the deprecated ActionSheet.
components.summaryLong: Modern replacement for ActionSheet. Multiple action buttons with roles; a cancel button is added automatically on iOS. titleVisibility controls whether the title text shows above the buttons.

properties.name: title
properties.notes: First positional argument.

properties.name: isPresented
properties.notes: Drives presentation.

properties.name: titleVisibility
properties.notes: Whether the title appears above the buttons.

properties.name: includesDestructive
properties.notes: Adds a Button with role .destructive (rendered red, at top of the action list).

properties.name: message
properties.notes: Optional message builder shown under the title.

components.higNotes: Use for a short list of choices in response to a user action, especially destructive ones. The system supplies the Cancel button on iPhone; do not add your own. On iPad it appears as a popover anchored to the source.

components.title: Inspector
components.summary: Presents a context-sensitive detail pane as a trailing column on wide layouts and a sheet in compact widths.
components.summaryLong: Shows supplementary detail/properties for the current selection. On large iPad and Mac it docks as a trailing column whose width is set via inspectorColumnWidth; in compact it adapts to a sheet.

properties.name: isPresented
properties.notes: Drives presentation of the inspector pane.

properties.name: columnWidth
properties.notes: .inspectorColumnWidth(_:) fixed width, or the min/ideal/max overload for a resizable column.

properties.name: minWidth
properties.notes: Lower bound for .inspectorColumnWidth(min:ideal:max:).

properties.name: maxWidth
properties.notes: Upper bound for the resizable column overload.

components.higNotes: Use an inspector for editable attributes of the current selection that should remain visible alongside content. It is not a navigation surface. Set a sensible default and bounded width so the main content stays usable.

components.title: File Importer
components.summary: Presents the system document picker to import one or more files of allowed UTTypes.
components.summaryLong: Opens the Files document browser. Returns security-scoped URLs in a Result; call startAccessingSecurityScopedResource before reading. Overloads support single vs multiple selection and an onCancellation closure.

properties.name: isPresented
properties.notes: Drives presentation of the picker.

properties.name: allowedContentTypes
properties.notes: Restricts selectable file types. Import UniformTypeIdentifiers.

properties.name: allowsMultipleSelection
properties.notes: Multi-select overload returns [URL]; single returns URL.

components.higNotes: Limit allowedContentTypes to what the app can actually handle. Begin security-scoped access immediately and copy needed data; the URL access is temporary. Handle the cancellation and error Result cases gracefully.

components.title: File Exporter
components.summary: Presents the system save dialog to export a FileDocument/Transferable item to a chosen location.
components.summaryLong: Saves a document conforming to FileDocument, or a Transferable item (iOS 17+ item overload). Lets the user pick a destination and filename. .fileExporterFilenameLabel customizes the filename field label.

properties.name: isPresented
properties.notes: Drives presentation of the save dialog.

properties.name: contentType
properties.notes: The exported file's content type.

properties.name: defaultFilename
properties.notes: Suggested filename prefilled in the dialog.

properties.name: filenameLabel
properties.notes: .fileExporterFilenameLabel(_:) customizes the filename field's label. iOS 17+.

components.higNotes: Provide a meaningful defaultFilename. Match contentType to the document's real format. Handle the onCompletion failure case and report errors to the user.

components.title: File Mover
components.summary: Presents the document picker to move an existing file URL to a new location chosen by the user.

properties.name: isPresented
properties.notes: Drives presentation.

properties.name: file
properties.notes: The existing file to move. A files-collection overload also exists.

components.higNotes: Use to let users relocate a file the app owns. Handle the resulting URL with security-scoped access and report failures.

components.title: Photos Picker
components.summary: Presents the privacy-preserving system photo library picker, returning PhotosPickerItem values to load as Transferable.
components.summaryLong: From the PhotosUI framework. A button that presents the system Photos picker out-of-process (no library permission prompt). Bind selection to PhotosPickerItem(s); filter by .images/.videos via matching; load data with loadTransferable. Configure photo library, max selection, and preferred encoding.

properties.name: selection
properties.notes: Single-selection binding; a [PhotosPickerItem] overload supports multiple with maxSelectionCount.

properties.name: maxSelectionCount
properties.notes: Multi-selection overload only. nil means unlimited.

properties.name: matching
properties.notes: Restricts the asset types shown.

properties.name: preferredItemEncoding
properties.notes: Controls transcoding of HEIC etc. on export.

properties.name: photoLibrary
properties.notes: Use .shared() to enable extra features like search.

properties.name: style
properties.notes: .photosPickerStyle(_:). .inline/.compact embed the picker rather than presenting modally.

components.higNotes: Prefer PhotosPicker over a custom library UI: it runs out of process and needs no photo-library permission. Show a thumbnail/preview of the chosen asset. Load asynchronously via loadTransferable and show a loading state.

components.title: Share Link
components.summary: Presents the system share sheet for one or more Transferable items via a standard share control.
components.summaryLong: A control that, when activated, presents the share sheet (UIActivityViewController-equivalent) for any Transferable payload - URLs, strings, images, custom types. Supports subject, message, and a SharePreview.

properties.name: item
properties.notes: The payload to share. An items-collection overload shares multiple.

properties.name: subject
properties.notes: Subject line used by destinations like Mail.

properties.name: message
properties.notes: Default message body where supported.

properties.name: preview
properties.notes: Required for non-self-previewing types; provides title and image.

components.higNotes: Use the standard ShareLink so users get the familiar share sheet. Provide a SharePreview with a representative title and thumbnail for richer destinations. Don't rebuild a custom share UI.

components.title: Interactive Dismiss Disabled
components.summary: Conditionally blocks swipe / interactive dismissal of the enclosing sheet or popover.

properties.name: isDisabled
properties.notes: true prevents pull-to-dismiss; bind to a 'has unsaved changes' flag.

components.higNotes: Disable interactive dismissal only when discarding would lose data. Pair with a confirmationDialog (see dismissalConfirmationDialog) and always keep an explicit Cancel/Done so users are never trapped.
components.codeTemplate:
SheetContentView()
    .interactiveDismissDisabled(${isDisabled})

components.title: Dismissal Confirmation Dialog
components.summary: Shows a confirmation dialog when the user attempts to interactively dismiss a presentation with unsaved work.

properties.name: title
properties.notes: Dialog title shown on dismissal attempt.

properties.name: shouldPresent
properties.notes: When true, intercepts the dismissal gesture to confirm.

components.higNotes: Preferred over a bare interactiveDismissDisabled when you want to confirm rather than block. Offer a clear Discard (destructive) and Keep Editing (cancel) choice.

components.title: Dismiss Action (Environment)
components.summary: Environment action a presented view calls to dismiss itself programmatically.
components.summaryLong: Read @Environment(\.dismiss) inside any presented view (sheet, cover, popover, pushed stack view) and call dismiss() to remove the current presentation or pop the stack. Replaces the deprecated presentationMode.

properties.name: callSite
properties.notes: Showroom wires dismiss() to a Done button to demonstrate programmatic dismissal.

components.higNotes: Use the environment dismiss action rather than passing bindings down. It dismisses the nearest presentation or pops the current navigation stack view.

components.title: Popover Tip (TipKit)
components.summary: Presents a TipKit tip as a popover anchored to a control to teach a feature in context.
components.summaryLong: Attaches a TipKit Tip to a view, surfacing it as a popover when its display rules are satisfied. Pair with .tipViewStyle and an action handler for tip buttons.

properties.name: tip
properties.notes: The Tip whose rules govern when the popover appears.

properties.name: arrowEdge
properties.notes: Direction the popover arrow points.

components.higNotes: Use tips sparingly for genuinely discoverable features. Let TipKit's frequency and display rules govern presentation; never spam. Provide a clear dismiss affordance.

components.title: Presentation Detents
components.summary: Defines the set of resizable heights a sheet can snap to, with optional programmatic selection.
components.summaryLong: Applied inside a sheet's content. Built-in detents (.medium, .large), proportional (.fraction), fixed (.height), and CustomPresentationDetent for app-defined logic. Multiple detents enable the resize grabber.

properties.name: detents
properties.notes: Multi-select. Include two or more to allow user resizing.

properties.name: selection
properties.notes: Optional two-way binding to read/set the active detent.

components.higNotes: Offer .medium plus .large for a flexible bottom sheet. Avoid more than three detents. Use .fraction/.height for content-driven sizing; CustomPresentationDetent when sizing depends on context.
components.codeTemplate:
SheetContentView()
    .presentationDetents([${detents}], selection: ${selection})

components.title: Quick Look Preview
components.summary: Presents a system Quick Look preview of a file URL, dismissing automatically when the bound URL becomes nil.

properties.name: item
properties.notes: Set to a non-nil URL to present; Quick Look resets it to nil on dismissal. A quickLookPreview(_:in:) overload previews one item within a collection.

components.higNotes: Use Quick Look for read-only previews of documents, images, PDFs, and media the app already has on disk. It is system-driven and handles a wide range of file types; do not rebuild a custom previewer. Available on iOS and macOS only.
components.codeTemplate:
SomeView()
    .quickLookPreview(${item})

components.title: Translation Presentation
components.summary: Presents the system translation popover for a string, optionally offering a Replace with Translation action.
components.summaryLong: From the Translation framework integration in SwiftUI. Shows a system UI translation popover anchored to the view holding the text. Provide a replacementAction closure to surface a Replace with Translation button that returns the translated string.

properties.name: isPresented
properties.notes: Drives presentation of the translation popover.

properties.name: text
properties.notes: The text to translate. Changing it after presentation has no effect.

properties.name: attachmentAnchor
properties.notes: Anchor point of the translation popover.

properties.name: arrowEdge
properties.notes: macOS arrow direction. iOS ignores this parameter.

properties.name: includesReplacement
properties.notes: When true, wires a replacementAction so a Replace with Translation button appears returning the translated string.

components.higNotes: Use for in-context translation of short strings (a sentence or two). Provide replacementAction only when the surrounding text is editable so the user can substitute the translation. iOS and macOS only.

components.title: Dialog Configuration
components.summary: Customizes confirmation dialogs and alerts with a custom icon, severity styling, and a Do-not-ask-again suppression toggle.
components.summaryLong: A family of modifiers that decorate alerts and confirmation dialogs. dialogIcon supplies a custom image; dialogSeverity sets .standard/.critical styling; dialogSuppressionToggle adds a 'Do not ask again' checkbox bound to a flag. Primarily macOS, with severity also on watchOS.

properties.name: icon
properties.notes: .dialogIcon(_:) sets a custom alert/dialog icon. macOS only.

properties.name: severity
properties.notes: .dialogSeverity(_:). .critical adds a caution affordance. macOS 13 / watchOS 10.

properties.name: isSuppressed
properties.notes: .dialogSuppressionToggle(_:isSuppressed:) adds a 'Don't ask again' checkbox. macOS only.

components.higNotes: Reserve .critical severity for genuinely destructive or dangerous outcomes. Offer a suppression toggle only for repetitive confirmations the user can safely silence. These modifiers are macOS-centric.

components.title: File Dialog Configuration
components.summary: Customizes the system file importer/exporter/mover dialogs: confirmation button label, prompt message, starting directory, and browser options.
components.summaryLong: A set of modifiers that decorate a fileImporter/fileExporter/fileMover presentation. fileDialogConfirmationLabel renames the action button; fileDialogMessage adds prompt text; fileDialogDefaultDirectory sets the initial folder; fileDialogBrowserOptions toggles options like .includeHiddenFiles or .enumeratePackages.

properties.name: confirmationLabel
properties.notes: .fileDialogConfirmationLabel(_:) overrides the default Open/Save button text.

properties.name: message
properties.notes: .fileDialogMessage(_:) shows guidance text in the dialog.

properties.name: defaultDirectory
properties.notes: .fileDialogDefaultDirectory(_:) sets the folder the picker opens in.

properties.name: browserOptions
properties.notes: .fileDialogBrowserOptions(_:) toggles browser behaviors. Option set.

components.higNotes: Attach these modifiers to the same view carrying the fileImporter/fileExporter/fileMover. Use a meaningful confirmation label and message so the dialog's purpose is clear. iOS 17 / macOS 14.
components.codeTemplate:
SomeView()
    .fileImporter(isPresented: $isPresented, allowedContentTypes: [.item]) { _ in }
    .fileDialogConfirmationLabel(${confirmationLabel})
    .fileDialogMessage(${message})
    .fileDialogDefaultDirectory(${defaultDirectory})
    .fileDialogBrowserOptions(${browserOptions})

components.title: Custom Presentation Background
components.summary: Sets a fully custom view as the background of the enclosing sheet/popover, instead of a shape style.
components.summaryLong: Companion to presentationBackground(_:) that takes a @ViewBuilder so you can layer gradients, images, or custom decoration behind a presentation's content, aligned via the alignment parameter.

properties.name: alignment
properties.notes: How the custom background content aligns within the presentation.

properties.name: content
properties.notes: The view used as the presentation background.

components.higNotes: Provide an opaque fallback under Reduce Transparency. Keep the background subtle so it never competes with the sheet content. Prefer the shape-style overload presentationBackground(_:) for plain materials.

components.title: Image
components.summary: Displays a bitmap, asset, system symbol, or rendered graphic with resizing, scaling, and rendering controls.
components.summaryLong: Image is the core view for static imagery. It supports asset catalog images, SF Symbols, CGImage/UIImage/NSImage sources, and decorative vs. labeled variants. The most-exposed showroom knobs are resizing mode, content mode (scaledToFit/scaledToFill), rendering mode, interpolation, and antialiasing.
components.higNotes: Always provide an accessibilityLabel for meaningful images; use Image(decorative:) for purely ornamental art so VoiceOver skips it. Use resizable() before applying frame; without it the image renders at intrinsic size. Prefer scaledToFit to avoid cropping content, scaledToFill inside a clipped container for edge-to-edge art.

properties.name: source
properties.notes: Drives which Image initializer is emitted: Image("name"), Image(systemName:), or Image(decorative:).

properties.name: resizable
properties.notes: Applies .resizable(). Required before frame() resizing has any effect on the rendered bitmap.

properties.name: resizingMode
properties.notes: Argument to resizable(resizingMode:). tile repeats the image; stretch scales it.

properties.name: capInsets
properties.notes: resizable(capInsets:) protects edge regions from stretching (9-slice). Expose four numeric steppers 0...100.

properties.name: contentMode
properties.notes: Emits .scaledToFit() or .scaledToFill(). fill usually needs a clipped frame.

properties.name: renderingMode
properties.notes: .renderingMode(_:). template tints with foregroundStyle; original keeps source colors.

properties.name: interpolation
properties.notes: .interpolation(_:). Use none for pixel art to keep hard edges.

properties.name: antialiased
properties.notes: .antialiased(_:) smooths scaled edges.

properties.name: foregroundStyle
properties.notes: Tints template images and monochrome symbols.

properties.name: frameWidth
properties.notes: Container width 20...400 to demonstrate fit vs fill.

properties.name: frameHeight
properties.notes: Container height 20...400.

properties.name: clipped
properties.notes: .clipped() trims overflow; essential to make scaledToFill behave.

properties.name: accessibilityLabel
properties.notes: VoiceOver label; omit only for decorative images.

properties.name: imageSourceKind
properties.notes: Extends the existing 'source' picker: Image also initializes from a UIImage/NSImage (Image(uiImage:)) and a CGImage (Image(_:scale:orientation:label:)). The draft only lists asset/systemSymbol/decorative, omitting the platform-image and CGImage initializers.

components.codeTemplate:
Image({source})
    {resizable}
    .interpolation(.{interpolation})
    .antialiased({antialiased})
    .renderingMode(.{renderingMode})
    .aspectRatio(contentMode: .{contentMode})
    .foregroundStyle({foregroundStyle})
    .frame(width: {frameWidth}, height: {frameHeight})
    {clipped}
    .accessibilityLabel("{accessibilityLabel}")

components.title: SF Symbol Image
components.summary: Renders an SF Symbol with rendering mode, variable value, font sizing, and per-layer color palettes.
components.summaryLong: SF Symbols are vector glyphs that scale with Dynamic Type and align with text. Configure rendering mode (monochrome/hierarchical/palette/multicolor), variable value 0...1 for partial-fill symbols (wifi, speaker, battery), font weight and scale, and palette colors. Pairs with symbolEffect for animation and contentTransition(.symbolEffect) for swaps.
components.higNotes: Use the symbol that best matches the action's meaning; keep weight consistent with adjacent text. Hierarchical and palette modes convey depth without full color. Use variableValue to reflect a real measured quantity (signal, volume), not decoration. Always provide an accessibilityLabel since the glyph name is not spoken meaningfully.

properties.name: systemName
properties.notes: SF Symbol identifier. Variable-value symbols (wifi, speaker.wave.3, battery) react to variableValue.

properties.name: variableValue
properties.notes: Image(systemName:variableValue:). 0...1 drives partial fill on variable symbols. nil to omit.

properties.name: symbolRenderingMode
properties.notes: .symbolRenderingMode(_:). palette requires foregroundStyle with 1-3 colors.

properties.name: primaryColor
properties.notes: First foregroundStyle color (monochrome tint / palette layer 1 / hierarchical base).

properties.name: secondaryColor
properties.notes: Palette layer 2 (foregroundStyle(_:_:)).

properties.name: tertiaryColor
properties.notes: Palette layer 3 (foregroundStyle(_:_:_:)).

properties.name: fontTextStyle
properties.notes: .font(.<style>) — symbols scale with Dynamic Type.

properties.name: fontWeight
properties.notes: .fontWeight(_:) controls symbol stroke weight.

properties.name: imageScale
properties.notes: .imageScale(_:) relative size within the current font.

properties.name: symbolVariant
properties.notes: .symbolVariant(_:) swaps to the filled/enclosed variant when available.

properties.name: accessibilityLabel
properties.notes: Spoken description of the symbol's meaning.

components.codeTemplate:
Image(systemName: {systemName}, variableValue: {variableValue})
    .symbolRenderingMode(.{symbolRenderingMode})
    .symbolVariant(.{symbolVariant})
    .foregroundStyle({primaryColor}, {secondaryColor}, {tertiaryColor})
    .font(.{fontTextStyle})
    .fontWeight(.{fontWeight})
    .imageScale(.{imageScale})
    .accessibilityLabel("{accessibilityLabel}")

components.title: Symbol Effect
components.summary: Animates an SF Symbol with bounce, pulse, variableColor, scale, breathe, rotate, wiggle, appear/disappear, or replace.
components.summaryLong: symbolEffect drives the Symbols framework animation engine. Discrete effects (bounce) fire on a value change; indefinite effects (pulse, variableColor, breathe, rotate, wiggle) animate while isActive is true. Effects expose submodifiers: byLayer/wholeSymbol scope, up/down direction, iterative/cumulative & reversing for variableColor, clockwise/counterClockwise for rotate. wiggle, breathe, rotate are iOS 18+. replace is a contentTransition used when swapping the symbol image.
components.higNotes: Use motion to communicate state change, not for decoration. Honor Reduce Motion — gate indefinite effects behind @Environment(\.accessibilityReduceMotion). bounce confirms a tap; pulse signals ongoing activity; variableColor shows progress; replace animates a meaningful icon swap (play→pause).

properties.name: effect
properties.notes: Which effect to apply. bounce/scale are discrete (use value:); the rest can run indefinitely (use isActive:).

properties.name: trigger
properties.notes: Chooses the symbolEffect overload: isActive: Bool for indefinite, value: for discrete-on-change.

properties.name: isActive
properties.notes: When trigger==isActive, runs the indefinite effect while true.

properties.name: scope
properties.notes: Submodifier on the effect: animate each layer separately or as one.

properties.name: direction
properties.notes: bounce/scale direction submodifier.

properties.name: variableColorMode
properties.notes: variableColor: iterative lights one layer at a time, cumulative keeps them lit.

properties.name: variableColorReversing
properties.notes: variableColor .reversing vs .nonReversing submodifier.

properties.name: rotateDirection
properties.notes: rotate effect direction (iOS 18+).

properties.name: repeating
properties.notes: options: .repeating vs .nonRepeating via SymbolEffectOptions.

properties.name: speed
properties.notes: SymbolEffectOptions.speed(_:) multiplier 0.25...3.0.

properties.name: repeatCount
properties.notes: SymbolEffectOptions.repeat(_:) for a finite count of plays.

components.codeTemplate:
Image(systemName: "heart.fill")
    .font(.largeTitle)
    .symbolEffect(
        .{effect},
        options: .repeating.speed({speed}),
        isActive: {isActive}
    )

components.title: AsyncImage
components.summary: Asynchronously loads and displays a remote image via URLSession, with placeholder and phase-based content.
components.summaryLong: AsyncImage loads from a URL on the shared URLSession. Three initializers: bare (url:scale:) with a default placeholder; (url:scale:content:placeholder:) for a custom placeholder and image transform; and (url:scale:transaction:content:) which receives an AsyncImagePhase (.empty, .success(Image), .failure(Error)) so you can render loading, success, and error states with an animated transaction.
components.higNotes: Show a sized placeholder (skeleton or ProgressView) so layout does not jump when the image arrives. Handle the .failure phase with a fallback rather than blank space. AsyncImage has no built-in caching beyond URLSession's cache — for heavy lists use a dedicated image loader. Animate the success transition with a transaction for a polished reveal.

properties.name: url
properties.notes: Remote image URL. nil renders the placeholder/empty phase.

properties.name: scale
properties.notes: Pixel-to-point scale; 2 or 3 for @2x/@3x assets.

properties.name: usePhaseContent
properties.notes: Switches between the phase-driven init (handles error) and the simpler content+placeholder init.

properties.name: contentMode
properties.notes: Applied to the resizable success image inside content closure.

properties.name: placeholderStyle
properties.notes: What to render while loading / on empty phase.

properties.name: animateTransition
properties.notes: Wraps the phase content in a Transaction with animation for a fade-in.

properties.name: frameSize
properties.notes: Square frame so placeholder and image share footprint.

properties.name: cornerRadius
properties.notes: clipShape(RoundedRectangle) radius.

components.codeTemplate:
AsyncImage(url: {url}, scale: {scale}, transaction: Transaction(animation: {animateTransition} ? .easeInOut : nil)) { phase in
    switch phase {
    case .success(let image):
        image.resizable().aspectRatio(contentMode: .{contentMode})
    case .failure:
        Image(systemName: "photo.badge.exclamationmark").font(.largeTitle).foregroundStyle(.secondary)
    case .empty:
        ProgressView()
    @unknown default:
        EmptyView()
    }
}
.frame(width: {frameSize}, height: {frameSize})
.clipShape(RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius: {cornerRadius}))

components.title: Rectangle
components.summary: A rectangular shape filling its frame, used for fills, strokes, dividers, and backgrounds.
components.summaryLong: Rectangle is the simplest Shape, aligned to its frame's edges. Drive fill style, stroke color/width, frame, opacity, rotation, and shadow. Combine with .fill, .stroke, .strokeBorder (inset stroke), and shape styles (Color, Gradient, Material).
components.higNotes: Use Rectangle for backgrounds and separators rather than drawing with Path. For rounded corners use RoundedRectangle, not a Rectangle with a clip. strokeBorder keeps the stroke inside the bounds so it isn't clipped by neighbors.

properties.name: renderStyle
properties.notes: Emits .fill(_:), .stroke(_:lineWidth:), or .strokeBorder(_:lineWidth:).

properties.name: fillStyle
properties.notes: Which ShapeStyle fills the shape.

properties.name: fillColor
properties.notes: Color used when fillStyle == color or as gradient base.

properties.name: lineWidth
properties.notes: Stroke width when renderStyle is stroke/strokeBorder.

properties.name: width
properties.notes: Frame width.

properties.name: height
properties.notes: Frame height.

properties.name: opacity
properties.notes: .opacity(_:).

properties.name: rotation
properties.notes: .rotationEffect(.degrees(_:)) in degrees.

components.codeTemplate:
Rectangle()
    .{renderStyle}({fillColor}, lineWidth: {lineWidth})
    .opacity({opacity})
    .rotationEffect(.degrees({rotation}))
    .frame(width: {width}, height: {height})

components.title: RoundedRectangle
components.summary: A rectangle with rounded corners, configurable via corner radius or width/height, with circular or continuous style.
components.summaryLong: RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius:style:) or (cornerSize:style:). The style is RoundedCornerStyle.circular or .continuous (Apple's squircle). It is the workhorse for cards, buttons, and clipShape. Pair with .fill/.stroke/.strokeBorder and shape styles.
components.higNotes: Prefer .continuous corner style for the smooth Apple squircle look on cards and large surfaces. Use a corner radius proportional to the element size. For clipping content use clipShape(RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius:style:)).

properties.name: cornerRadius
properties.notes: Corner radius in points.

properties.name: cornerStyle
properties.notes: circular = quarter-circle corners; continuous = squircle.

properties.name: renderStyle
properties.notes: fill / stroke / inset strokeBorder.

properties.name: fillColor
properties.notes: Primary shape style color.

properties.name: lineWidth
properties.notes: Stroke width for stroke/strokeBorder.

properties.name: width
properties.notes: Frame width.

properties.name: height
properties.notes: Frame height.

components.codeTemplate:
RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius: {cornerRadius}, style: .{cornerStyle})
    .{renderStyle}({fillColor}, lineWidth: {lineWidth})
    .frame(width: {width}, height: {height})

components.title: UnevenRoundedRectangle
components.summary: A rectangle with independently configurable corner radii per corner.
components.summaryLong: UnevenRoundedRectangle(topLeadingRadius:bottomLeadingRadius:bottomTrailingRadius:topTrailingRadius:style:) gives per-corner control for asymmetric cards, sheet tops, and grouped list cells. Honors RoundedCornerStyle.
components.higNotes: Use for surfaces that meet a screen edge or a neighbor on one side — round only the exposed corners. Keep continuous style consistent with sibling shapes.

properties.name: topLeadingRadius
properties.notes: Top-leading corner radius.

properties.name: topTrailingRadius
properties.notes: Top-trailing corner radius.

properties.name: bottomLeadingRadius
properties.notes: Bottom-leading corner radius.

properties.name: bottomTrailingRadius
properties.notes: Bottom-trailing corner radius.

properties.name: cornerStyle
properties.notes: Corner curve style.

properties.name: fillColor
properties.notes: Fill color.

properties.name: width
properties.notes: Frame width.

properties.name: height
properties.notes: Frame height.

components.codeTemplate:
UnevenRoundedRectangle(
    topLeadingRadius: {topLeadingRadius},
    bottomLeadingRadius: {bottomLeadingRadius},
    bottomTrailingRadius: {bottomTrailingRadius},
    topTrailingRadius: {topTrailingRadius},
    style: .{cornerStyle}
)
.fill({fillColor})
.frame(width: {width}, height: {height})

components.title: Capsule
components.summary: A pill shape — a rectangle whose shorter dimension's ends are fully rounded.
components.summaryLong: Capsule(style:) rounds the ends along the shorter axis. It is the standard background for tags, chips, toggles, and prominent buttons. style picks circular or continuous end curvature.
components.higNotes: Capsule is the canonical chip/tag background. Keep vertical padding generous so text isn't cramped against the rounded ends. Use continuous style for a softer pill.

properties.name: cornerStyle
properties.notes: End-cap curve style.

properties.name: renderStyle
properties.notes: fill / stroke / strokeBorder.

properties.name: fillColor
properties.notes: Shape style color.

properties.name: lineWidth
properties.notes: Stroke width.

properties.name: width
properties.notes: Frame width.

properties.name: height
properties.notes: Frame height (shorter axis drives the rounding).

components.codeTemplate:
Capsule(style: .{cornerStyle})
    .{renderStyle}({fillColor}, lineWidth: {lineWidth})
    .frame(width: {width}, height: {height})

components.title: Circle
components.summary: A circle inscribed in its frame, centered on the frame's center.
components.summaryLong: Circle fits the largest circle inside its frame. Used for avatars, badges, dots, and progress rings (with trim + stroke). Combine with strokeBorder for inset rings.
components.higNotes: Use Circle for avatars and status dots. For ring/progress indicators combine Circle().trim(from:to:).stroke(style: StrokeStyle(lineCap: .round)). Size avatars with a square frame so the circle stays round.

properties.name: renderStyle
properties.notes: fill / stroke / strokeBorder.

properties.name: fillColor
properties.notes: Shape style color.

properties.name: lineWidth
properties.notes: Stroke width for ring styles.

properties.name: trimFrom
properties.notes: .trim(from:to:) start, for arcs/progress.

properties.name: trimTo
properties.notes: .trim(from:to:) end.

properties.name: lineCap
properties.notes: StrokeStyle line cap for trimmed arcs.

properties.name: diameter
properties.notes: Square frame size.

components.codeTemplate:
Circle()
    .trim(from: {trimFrom}, to: {trimTo})
    .{renderStyle}({fillColor}, style: StrokeStyle(lineWidth: {lineWidth}, lineCap: .{lineCap}))
    .frame(width: {diameter}, height: {diameter})

components.title: Ellipse
components.summary: An ellipse inscribed in its frame, stretching with non-square dimensions.
components.summaryLong: Ellipse fits an ellipse to the frame's width and height. Unlike Circle it is not constrained to square. Useful for organic shapes, soft highlights, and decorative blobs.
components.higNotes: Use for decorative or organic accents. For circular content use Circle to guarantee roundness regardless of frame.

properties.name: renderStyle
properties.notes: fill / stroke / strokeBorder.

properties.name: fillColor
properties.notes: Shape style color.

properties.name: lineWidth
properties.notes: Stroke width.

properties.name: width
properties.notes: Frame width.

properties.name: height
properties.notes: Frame height.

components.codeTemplate:
Ellipse()
    .{renderStyle}({fillColor}, lineWidth: {lineWidth})
    .frame(width: {width}, height: {height})

components.title: Path
components.summary: A custom 2D outline built from move/line/curve/arc commands, fillable and strokable.
components.summaryLong: Path is the lowest-level vector primitive. Build it with a closure: move(to:), addLine(to:), addQuadCurve, addCurve, addArc, addRect, addEllipse, closeSubpath. Render with .fill or .stroke. Used for custom shapes, charts, and signature-style drawing. For reusable shapes wrap Path logic in a custom Shape conformance.
components.higNotes: Reserve Path for shapes the built-in primitives can't express. Wrap nontrivial paths in a Shape struct so they animate and resize cleanly. Combine fill (even-odd or non-zero) and stroke deliberately; specify fillStyle for self-intersecting paths.

properties.name: preset
properties.notes: Which sample path the showroom builds in the closure.

properties.name: renderStyle
properties.notes: .fill(_:) vs .stroke(_:style:).

properties.name: fillColor
properties.notes: Fill / stroke color.

properties.name: lineWidth
properties.notes: Stroke width.

properties.name: lineJoin
properties.notes: StrokeStyle line join.

properties.name: fillRule
properties.notes: fill(style: FillStyle(eoFill:)) for self-intersecting paths like stars.

properties.name: size
properties.notes: Drawing canvas square size.

components.codeTemplate:
Path { path in
    // {preset} geometry inside a {size}x{size} box
    path.move(to: CGPoint(x: {size}/2, y: 0))
    path.addLine(to: CGPoint(x: {size}, y: {size}))
    path.addLine(to: CGPoint(x: 0, y: {size}))
    path.closeSubpath()
}
.{renderStyle}({fillColor}, style: StrokeStyle(lineWidth: {lineWidth}, lineJoin: .{lineJoin}))
.frame(width: {size}, height: {size})

components.title: Canvas
components.summary: Immediate-mode 2D drawing surface using GraphicsContext for high-performance custom graphics.
components.summaryLong: Canvas gives a GraphicsContext and CGSize in its renderer closure for immediate-mode drawing: fill/stroke paths, draw text, images, and resolved SwiftUI symbols, apply filters, blend modes, transforms, masks, and layers. The symbols variant init(opaque:colorMode:rendersAsynchronously:renderer:symbols:) lets you reference views by tag. No per-element accessibility or hit-testing — best for dense, dynamic visuals.
components.higNotes: Use Canvas for complex/dynamic drawings that would be costly as a view tree. Provide an accessibilityLabel or representation since individual drawn elements aren't accessible. Honor Reduce Motion for animated canvases. Set opaque true when you draw a full background for better performance.

properties.name: opaque
properties.notes: init opaque: — true if the canvas fully covers its background (faster).

properties.name: colorMode
properties.notes: Color blending space for the canvas.

properties.name: rendersAsynchronously
properties.notes: Allow off-main-thread rendering; trades latency for throughput.

properties.name: drawingPrimitive
properties.notes: Which GraphicsContext operation the demo performs.

properties.name: strokeColor
properties.notes: GraphicsContext stroke/fill color.

properties.name: lineWidth
properties.notes: Stroke width passed to context.stroke.

properties.name: blendMode
properties.notes: context.blendMode for layered drawing.

properties.name: canvasHeight
properties.notes: Frame height of the canvas.

components.title: LinearGradient
components.summary: A gradient that interpolates colors along a straight line between two unit points.
components.summaryLong: LinearGradient(colors:startPoint:endPoint:) or (gradient:startPoint:endPoint:) or (stops:...). Unit points (.top, .bottom, .leading, .topTrailing, UnitPoint(x:y:)) set direction. Use as a ShapeStyle for fill/background/foregroundStyle. Supports an explicit color space via Gradient(stops:)/colorSpace.
components.higNotes: Keep gradients subtle for backgrounds; high-contrast gradients fight foreground text. For consistent perceptual blending across hues use a .perceptual or device color space. Avoid pure black-to-white over text.

properties.name: colorStart
properties.notes: First gradient color.

properties.name: colorEnd
properties.notes: Last gradient color.

properties.name: useThreeStops
properties.notes: Insert a middle color for a 3-stop gradient.

properties.name: colorMid
properties.notes: Middle color when useThreeStops is on.

properties.name: startPoint
properties.notes: Gradient start UnitPoint.

properties.name: endPoint
properties.notes: Gradient end UnitPoint.

properties.name: width
properties.notes: Frame width of the previewed swatch.

properties.name: height
properties.notes: Frame height of the swatch.

components.title: RadialGradient
components.summary: A gradient that interpolates colors outward from a center point between a start and end radius.
components.summaryLong: RadialGradient(colors:center:startRadius:endRadius:). Colors blend in concentric rings from center. Use for spotlights, glows, and soft vignettes. A ShapeStyle usable in fill/background.
components.higNotes: Great for a focal glow or vignette. Keep endRadius proportional to the frame; an undersized endRadius creates a hard ring. Center can be offset for directional light.

properties.name: colorCenter
properties.notes: Inner color.

properties.name: colorEdge
properties.notes: Outer color.

properties.name: center
properties.notes: Gradient origin.

properties.name: startRadius
properties.notes: Inner radius where the first color sits.

properties.name: endRadius
properties.notes: Outer radius where the last color sits.

properties.name: size
properties.notes: Square frame size.

components.codeTemplate:
RadialGradient(
    colors: [{colorCenter}, {colorEdge}],
    center: .{center},
    startRadius: {startRadius},
    endRadius: {endRadius}
)
.frame(width: {size}, height: {size})

components.title: AngularGradient
components.summary: A gradient that sweeps colors around a center point (conic gradient).
components.summaryLong: AngularGradient(colors:center:) or (colors:center:startAngle:endAngle:) or (colors:center:angle:). Sweeps colors around the center; with a full 0...360 wrap it forms a color wheel. Use for conic progress rings and decorative sweeps.
components.higNotes: Match start and end colors when sweeping a full circle to avoid a visible seam. Pair with Circle().trim + stroke for conic progress arcs.

properties.name: colorStart
properties.notes: First sweep color.

properties.name: colorEnd
properties.notes: Last sweep color (match colorStart for seamless wheel).

properties.name: useRainbow
properties.notes: Emit a full hue spectrum array instead of two colors.

properties.name: center
properties.notes: Sweep center.

properties.name: startAngle
properties.notes: Sweep start angle in degrees.

properties.name: endAngle
properties.notes: Sweep end angle in degrees.

properties.name: size
properties.notes: Square frame size.

components.codeTemplate:
AngularGradient(
    colors: [{colorStart}, {colorEnd}],
    center: .{center},
    startAngle: .degrees({startAngle}),
    endAngle: .degrees({endAngle})
)
.frame(width: {size}, height: {size})

components.title: EllipticalGradient
components.summary: A radial-style gradient whose rings are elliptical, matching a non-square frame.
components.summaryLong: EllipticalGradient(colors:center:startRadiusFraction:endRadiusFraction:). Like RadialGradient but stretches to an ellipse, so the glow follows a wide/tall frame. Radii are fractions of the frame.
components.higNotes: Prefer over RadialGradient when the surface isn't square so the falloff matches the frame's aspect. Keep endRadiusFraction near 0.5 for a centered glow.

properties.name: colorCenter
properties.notes: Inner color.

properties.name: colorEdge
properties.notes: Outer color.

properties.name: center
properties.notes: Gradient origin.

properties.name: startRadiusFraction
properties.notes: Inner radius as a fraction of frame size.

properties.name: endRadiusFraction
properties.notes: Outer radius as a fraction of frame size.

properties.name: width
properties.notes: Frame width.

properties.name: height
properties.notes: Frame height.

components.codeTemplate:
EllipticalGradient(
    colors: [{colorCenter}, {colorEdge}],
    center: .{center},
    startRadiusFraction: {startRadiusFraction},
    endRadiusFraction: {endRadiusFraction}
)
.frame(width: {width}, height: {height})

components.title: MeshGradient
components.summary: A 2D grid of positioned colors interpolated across Bezier patches for rich multi-color blends.
components.summaryLong: MeshGradient(width:height:points:colors:background:smoothsColors:colorSpace:). Each grid vertex has a position (SIMD2<Float>) and a color; colors interpolate across a tessellated Bezier mesh. Animate vertex positions for a flowing background. width/height are the grid dimensions; points.count must equal width*height.
components.higNotes: Animate vertex positions subtly for a living background; respect Reduce Motion by freezing the mesh. Keep colors within a harmonious palette — meshes amplify clashing hues. Use smoothsColors true for the softest transitions.

properties.name: gridWidth
properties.notes: Columns in the mesh grid.

properties.name: gridHeight
properties.notes: Rows in the mesh grid.

properties.name: cornerColorA
properties.notes: Seeds one corner of the generated color matrix.

properties.name: cornerColorB
properties.notes: Seeds a second corner.

properties.name: cornerColorC
properties.notes: Seeds a third corner.

properties.name: smoothsColors
properties.notes: Cubic color interpolation for smoother transitions.

properties.name: animateVertices
properties.notes: Demo-only: jitter interior vertex positions over time (gate on Reduce Motion).

properties.name: size
properties.notes: Square frame size.

components.codeTemplate:
MeshGradient(
    width: {gridWidth},
    height: {gridHeight},
    points: [
        [0,0], [0.5,0], [1,0],
        [0,0.5], [0.5,0.5], [1,0.5],
        [0,1], [0.5,1], [1,1]
    ],
    colors: [
        {cornerColorA}, .blue, {cornerColorB},
        .green, .white, .yellow,
        {cornerColorC}, .red, .mint
    ],
    smoothsColors: {smoothsColors}
)
.frame(width: {size}, height: {size})

components.title: Material (Blur)
components.summary: A translucent blur backdrop (ultraThin to thick) used as a ShapeStyle for backgrounds and overlays.
components.summaryLong: Material is a ShapeStyle producing system blur with vibrancy: .ultraThinMaterial, .thinMaterial, .regularMaterial, .thickMaterial, .ultraThickMaterial, plus .bar. Apply via .background(_:) or as a fill. Text/symbols over a material should use .foregroundStyle(.secondary/.tertiary) or a vibrancy hierarchy. On iOS 26 prefer Liquid Glass (glassEffect) for floating controls; materials remain for static panels.
components.higNotes: Materials adapt to light/dark and the content behind them. Provide an opaque fallback when Reduce Transparency is enabled. Use thinner materials over vivid content, thicker over busy backgrounds for legibility. Don't stack materials on materials. On iOS 26, floating/interactive surfaces should adopt Liquid Glass instead.

properties.name: material
properties.notes: Which system material blur to apply as the background.

properties.name: clipShape
properties.notes: Shape the material is clipped to.

properties.name: cornerRadius
properties.notes: Radius when clipShape is roundedRectangle.

properties.name: labelStyle
properties.notes: foregroundStyle hierarchy for vibrant text over the material.

properties.name: reduceTransparencyFallback
properties.notes: Demo-only: swap to an opaque background when accessibilityReduceTransparency is true.

properties.name: padding
properties.notes: Inner padding around the label content.

components.codeTemplate:
Text("Material")
    .foregroundStyle(.{labelStyle})
    .padding({padding})
    .background(.{material}, in: RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius: {cornerRadius}, style: .continuous))

components.title: ContainerRelativeShape
components.summary: An adaptive shape that inherits its container's corner shape (e.g. widget or grouped background).
components.summaryLong: ContainerRelativeShape resolves to the concentric corner shape of the nearest containerShape ancestor — primarily for Home Screen/Lock Screen widgets and any view that sets .containerShape(_:). It keeps nested cards visually concentric with the container's rounded corners.
components.higNotes: Use inside widgets to keep inset content concentric with the widget's rounded corners. Set .containerShape on an ancestor to define the reference shape. Falls back to a rectangle when no container shape is defined.

properties.name: fillColor
properties.notes: Fill color of the relative shape.

properties.name: renderStyle
properties.notes: fill or inset strokeBorder.

properties.name: inset
properties.notes: Padding from the container edge to show the concentric effect.

properties.name: containerCornerRadius
properties.notes: Reference container corner radius set via .containerShape.

components.codeTemplate:
RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius: {containerCornerRadius}, style: .continuous)
    .fill(.gray.opacity(0.3))
    .containerShape(RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius: {containerCornerRadius}, style: .continuous))
    .overlay {
        ContainerRelativeShape()
            .{renderStyle}({fillColor})
            .padding({inset})
    }

components.title: VideoPlayer
components.summary: VideoPlayer (AVKit) embeds an AVPlayer and renders Apple's standard playback controls. Two initializers: VideoPlayer(player:) and VideoPlayer(player:videoOverlay:) which layers SwiftUI content over the video. The player itself (AVPlayer) is created in a .task and held in @State so SwiftUI builds it once. This is the only video-media primitive in the Media & Shapes category and is currently absent from the draft.
components.higNotes: Create the AVPlayer lazily inside a .task and store it in @State so it is built once, not on every body evaluation (per Apple's own sample). Give the player a fixed aspect-correct frame (16:9 commonly) so layout does not jump before the asset loads. Provide an accessibilityLabel describing the video content; the transport controls are accessible but the content is not. Keep custom overlays out of the way of the system controls. Gate autoplay and looping behind user intent; honor the system mute switch and Now Playing where relevant.

properties.name: urlString
properties.notes: Source media URL used to build AVPlayer(url:) inside a .task. Local bundle URL also valid.

properties.name: autoplay
properties.notes: Demo-only: call player.play() when the view appears.

properties.name: showsOverlay
properties.notes: Switches between VideoPlayer(player:) and VideoPlayer(player:videoOverlay:) to render SwiftUI content over the video.

properties.name: overlayText
properties.notes: Label rendered in the videoOverlay closure when showsOverlay is on.

properties.name: width
properties.notes: Player frame width.

properties.name: height
properties.notes: Player frame height; keep ~16:9 with width.

properties.name: cornerRadius
properties.notes: clipShape(RoundedRectangle) radius applied to the player surface.

properties.name: accessibilityLabel
properties.notes: VoiceOver description of the video content.

components.title: Color (as View)
components.summary: SwiftUI treats Color as a View, so it can be placed in a hierarchy where it expands to fill the space its frame defines — the simplest fill primitive. The category showcases all three gradients but omits the base case: a solid adaptive Color swatch. Color is also a ShapeStyle and resolves to concrete values per environment (light/dark, asset-catalog variants). Color exposes .opacity(_:), .gradient (auto-derived subtle gradient, iOS 16+), and named/system/custom RGB initializers.
components.higNotes: Color resolves context-dependently — define light/dark variants in an Asset Catalog rather than hardcoding hex so the swatch adapts automatically. A bare Color expands to fill all offered space, so constrain it with a frame. Prefer semantic system colors (.primary, .accentColor, Color(.systemBackground)) over raw RGB for adaptive, accessible UI. Use .gradient for a quick subtle sheen without authoring a LinearGradient.

properties.name: colorSource
properties.notes: Drives the initializer: a system color, Color(red:green:blue:), or Color("name") from the asset catalog.

properties.name: systemColor
properties.notes: Selected color when colorSource is system/custom.

properties.name: opacity
properties.notes: .opacity(_:) applied to the color.

properties.name: asGradient
properties.notes: Emit color.gradient (iOS 16+) for an auto-derived subtle gradient fill instead of a flat color.

properties.name: clipShape
properties.notes: Shape the color swatch is clipped to.

properties.name: cornerRadius
properties.notes: Radius when clipShape is roundedRectangle.

properties.name: width
properties.notes: Swatch frame width.

properties.name: height
properties.notes: Swatch frame height.

components.codeTemplate:
{systemColor}
    .opacity({opacity})
    .frame(width: {width}, height: {height})
    .clipShape(RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius: {cornerRadius}, style: .continuous))

components.title: Content Unavailable View
components.summary: An empty-state interface with a label, description, and optional actions shown when content can't be displayed (no data, error, no network).

properties.name: title
properties.notes: The headline shown below the icon. Convenience init ContentUnavailableView(_:systemImage:description:).

properties.name: systemImage
properties.notes: SF Symbol name shown above the title. Use a symbol that communicates the empty state (tray, magnifyingglass, wifi.slash, exclamationmark.triangle).

properties.name: description
properties.notes: Secondary descriptive text. Pass via the description: closure or the Text convenience parameter. Optional.

properties.name: hasAction
properties.notes: When true, render a call-to-action Button in the actions closure. The actions closure is only available in the full label/description/actions builder init.

properties.name: actionTitle
properties.notes: Title of the CTA button shown when hasAction is true.

properties.name: preset
properties.notes: custom = builder init; search = ContentUnavailableView.search; searchText = ContentUnavailableView.search(text:) which interpolates the query into the default message.

components.higNotes: Use for genuinely empty or unreachable content, not transient loading (use ProgressView for that). Keep the title short, the description actionable, and provide a single clear recovery action. Pair with .searchable's empty results using the built-in .search variant. Place inside an .overlay { } over a List/ScrollView so it centers.

components.title: Progress View
components.summary: Shows progress toward task completion: determinate (value/total), indeterminate (spinner), or time-based (timerInterval).

properties.name: mode
properties.notes: indeterminate = ProgressView(); determinate = ProgressView(value:total:); timer = ProgressView(timerInterval:countsDown:).

properties.name: value
properties.notes: Current progress for determinate mode. Bound 0...total. Nil makes it indeterminate.

properties.name: total
properties.notes: The value representing completion. Defaults to 1.0.

properties.name: label
properties.notes: Describes the task. Hidden by some styles (.circular on iOS shows it below the spinner).

properties.name: showsCurrentValueLabel
properties.notes: When true, render a currentValueLabel: closure (e.g. percentage). Only meaningful with .linear style on most platforms.

properties.name: style
properties.notes: On platforms other than macOS, .circular renders as an indeterminate spinner; determinate ring requires macOS or a custom style.

properties.name: tint
properties.notes: Applied via .tint(_:). Colors the bar fill or spinner.

properties.name: countsDown
properties.notes: Timer mode only: whether the fill depletes (true) or fills (false) over the interval.

components.higNotes: Use determinate when you can estimate completion; indeterminate when you can't. Prefer a label so the task is clear. Don't combine a spinner with a separately drawn one. On watchOS prefer circular. Bare full-screen indeterminate spinners read as stalls; show a skeleton for content-heavy screens.

components.title: Gauge
components.summary: Displays a value within a finite range (like a fuel or BPM gauge) with optional current, minimum, and maximum value labels.

properties.name: value
properties.notes: Current value mapped into the bounds. Drives the indicator position.

properties.name: minValue
properties.notes: Lower bound of the gauge's range (in:). Shown by minimumValueLabel.

properties.name: maxValue
properties.notes: Upper bound of the gauge's range (in:). Shown by maximumValueLabel.

properties.name: label
properties.notes: Describes the gauge's purpose. Can be a Text or an SF Symbol Image.

properties.name: showsBoundsLabels
properties.notes: When true, render minimumValueLabel:/maximumValueLabel: closures. Ignored by accessory*Capacity styles.

properties.name: showsCurrentValueLabel
properties.notes: When true, render a currentValueLabel: closure showing the live value.

properties.name: style
properties.notes: accessory* styles are designed for compact contexts (watchOS complications, widgets). .circular and .accessoryCircularCapacity unavailable on tvOS; Gauge itself is unavailable on tvOS.

properties.name: tint
properties.notes: A Color, Gradient, or ShapeStyle passed to the style initializer (e.g. CircularGaugeStyle(tint:)) to color the track by value.

components.higNotes: Use a gauge for a value within known bounds where the relationship to the range matters (battery, speed, heart rate). For pure task progress use ProgressView instead. Use accessory styles in widgets and watch complications. Apply a value-mapped gradient tint so users read severity at a glance. Not available on tvOS.

components.title: Redaction (Placeholder / Skeleton)
components.summary: Replaces text and images with neutral placeholder shapes to build skeleton loading states without bespoke views.

properties.name: reason
properties.notes: .placeholder = skeleton; .privacy = obscure sensitive content; .invalidated (iOS 17+) = data no longer valid (Always-On / hidden). OptionSet, so reasons can combine.

properties.name: isRedacted
properties.notes: Drives whether the reason set is applied. Toggle to compare loading vs loaded layout. Apply empty reasons [] to disable.

properties.name: unredactInteractive
properties.notes: Apply .unredacted() to a child (e.g. a control) to keep it visible/interactive while the rest is redacted.

components.higNotes: Skeleton-first: redact a real, populated layout so the placeholder matches the final size and prevents layout jumps. Read .redactionReasons from the environment in custom views to render bespoke placeholders. Redaction disables interaction by default; use .unredacted() for elements that must stay live. Don't reveal sections piecemeal.

components.title: Privacy Sensitive
components.summary: Marks a view's content as private so the system redacts it automatically during screen sharing, recording, or the Always-On display.

properties.name: sensitive
properties.notes: Whether the content is privacy-sensitive. The system applies .privacy redaction when the context demands it (screen capture, remote control, Always-On).

properties.name: demoForcePrivacy
properties.notes: Showroom-only: apply .redacted(reason: .privacy) directly to preview how sensitive content looks when obscured, since real capture context can't be simulated live.

components.higNotes: Wrap account balances, health values, codes, and PII with .privacySensitive(). The system decides when to obscure (screen recording, AirPlay, watch Always-On). Combine with the .privacy redaction reason. Read \.isSceneCaptured from the environment to react to active screen sharing.

components.title: Sensory Feedback (Haptics)
components.summary: Plays haptic and/or audio feedback when a monitored Equatable value changes, with optional condition gating.

properties.name: feedback
properties.notes: Semantic feedback type. success/warning/error map to notification haptics; impact is configurable by weight (.light/.medium/.heavy) or flexibility (.rigid/.solid/.soft) plus intensity.

properties.name: impactWeight
properties.notes: Only used when feedback is .impact(weight:intensity:).

properties.name: impactIntensity
properties.notes: Strength multiplier for .impact variants. 0 = none, 1 = full.

properties.name: triggerOnChange
properties.notes: Showroom driver: tapping a button flips a monitored @State value to fire the feedback. The modifier plays only when trigger changes.

properties.name: useCondition
properties.notes: When true, use sensoryFeedback(_:trigger:condition:) to gate playback on a predicate of (old, new) values.

components.higNotes: Haptics confirm meaningful events, not every tap. Use semantic types (.success/.error) so the system maps to the right pattern and respects the user's haptics setting. Tie feedback to a state change you already model. No haptic engine on most macOS/tvOS hardware; the modifier is a no-op there. Don't overuse impact.

components.title: Badge
components.summary: Attaches a small count or short text badge to a view in a list row, tab item, toolbar item, or menu to convey supplementary status.

properties.name: kind
properties.notes: count = badge(Int) (hidden automatically when 0); text = badge(LocalizedStringKey)/badge(Text?); none = badge(nil) hides the badge.

properties.name: count
properties.notes: badge(_:Int). A value of 0 hides the badge automatically. Used by tab items and list rows.

properties.name: label
properties.notes: badge(_:LocalizedStringKey) / badge(Text?). Keep it as short as possible per HIG.

properties.name: prominence
