properties.notes: Applied via .badgeProminence(_:) on the enclosing container (e.g. List). increased renders the attention-grabbing red badge; decreased is the muted style.

components.higNotes: Use a badge for optional, supplementary information. Keep contents short (a count or one word). A count of 0 hides the badge. Badges appear in list rows, tab bars, toolbar items, and menus. Control emphasis with .badgeProminence on the container, not on the badge itself.

components.title: Symbol Effect
components.summary: Animates SF Symbols with built-in effects (bounce, pulse, variableColor, scale, appear/disappear, replace, wiggle, breathe, rotate) driven by a value change or an isActive flag.
components.summary_note: Discrete effects (bounce) need value/isActive triggers; indefinite effects (pulse, variableColor) animate continuously. wiggle/breathe/rotate are iOS 18+.

properties.name: effect
properties.notes: bounce/wiggle/rotate are discrete (need a trigger); pulse/variableColor/breathe are indefinite (run while active); scale/appear/disappear/replace are transition/state effects. wiggle, breathe, rotate require iOS 18 / macOS 15.

properties.name: trigger
properties.notes: value = symbolEffect(_:options:value:) fires on each change of a monitored value; isActive = symbolEffect(_:options:isActive:) runs an indefinite effect while true.

properties.name: isActive
properties.notes: Drives indefinite effects (pulse/variableColor/breathe) when trigger is isActive.

properties.name: options
properties.notes: Configure repeat behavior and speed, e.g. .repeating, .repeat(3), .speed(2).

properties.name: effectsRemoved
properties.notes: symbolEffectsRemoved(_:) clears inherited symbol effects from descendant symbols.

components.higNotes: Use symbol effects to communicate state changes and draw attention without separate animation code. Match the effect to meaning: bounce for a confirmed action, pulse/variableColor for ongoing activity, replace for a value swap. Discrete effects need a trigger; don't loop indefinite effects without reason. Honor Reduce Motion.

components.title: Alert
components.summary: Presents a modal system alert with a title, optional message, and action buttons for confirmations and critical errors that interrupt the user.
components.summary_note: The modern data-driven alert API; the old Alert value type is deprecated.

properties.name: title
properties.notes: Primary alert title. First parameter of the modifier.

properties.name: message
properties.notes: Optional supporting text rendered by the message: closure.

properties.name: isPresented
properties.notes: Binding that drives presentation. There is also an item-driven variant alert(_:isPresented:presenting:actions:message:) carrying associated data.

properties.name: buttonCount
properties.notes: Number of Button views in the actions: closure. SwiftUI lays out buttons automatically; a single default Cancel is synthesized if none provided.

properties.name: hasDestructive
properties.notes: When true include a Button(role: .destructive). Rendered in red; placed appropriately by the system.

properties.name: hasCancel
properties.notes: When true include a Button(role: .cancel). Bolded and positioned per platform.

components.higNotes: Reserve alerts for essential, interruptive information that needs acknowledgement or a decision (destructive confirmation, critical error). Keep the title short and the message a single clear sentence. Use button roles (.destructive, .cancel) instead of styling. For non-critical choices over a control, prefer confirmationDialog. Don't use alerts for routine feedback.

components.title: Confirmation Dialog
components.summary: Presents an action sheet / popover of contextual choices anchored to the user's action, with optional destructive and cancel roles.
components.summary_note: Renders as an action sheet on iPhone, a popover on iPad/Mac.

properties.name: title
properties.notes: Dialog title. Visibility controlled by titleVisibility.

properties.name: titleVisibility
properties.notes: automatic hides the title on compact iPhone sheets but shows it where space allows; force with .visible.

properties.name: isPresented
properties.notes: Binding driving presentation. An item-driven presenting: variant carries associated data.

properties.name: buttonCount
properties.notes: Number of choice buttons in the actions: closure. Dialogs comfortably hold several options, unlike alerts.

properties.name: hasDestructive
properties.notes: Include a Button(role: .destructive) for the dangerous choice; rendered in red.

properties.name: hasMessage
properties.notes: When true provide a message: closure with supporting context.

components.higNotes: Use a confirmation dialog to confirm a single action with multiple options, especially destructive ones, anchored to where the user tapped. Put the destructive action first with .destructive role; the system adds Cancel on iPhone. Prefer it over an alert when offering choices rather than acknowledging information. Keep options short and parallel.

components.title: Frame (Fixed Size)
components.summary: Proposes a fixed width and/or height to the modified view, positioning it within the resulting frame by an alignment.

properties.name: width
properties.notes: Range 0...500 for the showroom. Pass nil to leave the dimension flexible; expose a 'set width' toggle to allow nil.

properties.name: height
properties.notes: Range 0...500. nil leaves height driven by content.

properties.name: alignment
properties.notes: Positions the content within the frame when the frame is larger than the content.

components.higNotes: Avoid hardcoding pixel sizes that fight Dynamic Type. Prefer flexible frames and let content size itself; reserve fixed frames for genuinely fixed-dimension elements like avatars or icons.
components.codeTemplate:
Text("Framed")
    .frame(width: {width}, height: {height}, alignment: .{alignment})

components.title: Frame (Flexible / Min-Ideal-Max)
components.summary: Constrains a view between minimum, ideal, and maximum bounds; .infinity makes the view expand to fill available space.

properties.name: minWidth
properties.notes: Range 0...400. nil leaves the lower bound unconstrained.

properties.name: maxWidth
properties.notes: Use .infinity to expand to fill the parent's proposed width.

properties.name: maxHeight
properties.notes: Use .infinity to fill vertically.

properties.name: alignment
properties.notes: Positions content inside the resolved frame.

components.higNotes: maxWidth: .infinity is the idiomatic way to make buttons and rows span the full width. Pair with alignment to control where content sits inside the expanded frame.
components.codeTemplate:
Text("Flexible")
    .frame(maxWidth: {maxWidth}, alignment: .{alignment})

components.title: Padding
components.summary: Adds space around the view along the chosen edges; with no arguments uses a system-default amount that adapts to context.

properties.name: edges
properties.notes: Which edges receive padding.

properties.name: length
properties.notes: Range 0...64. Pass nil to use the system default adaptive padding.

components.higNotes: Prefer system default padding (no length argument) for standard layouts; it adapts across platforms and size classes. Reserve explicit values for design-system spacing tokens.
components.codeTemplate:
Text("Padded")
    .padding(.{edges}, {length})

components.title: Offset
components.summary: Shifts the view's rendered position by a CGSize without affecting its layout footprint (siblings see the original frame).

properties.name: x
properties.notes: Range -100...100. Positive moves right.

properties.name: y
properties.notes: Range -100...100. Positive moves down.

components.higNotes: Offset is purely visual and does not change layout; use it for nudges and animations, not for structural positioning. For layout-affecting moves, use padding or a stack.
components.codeTemplate:
Text("Offset")
    .offset(x: {x}, y: {y})

components.title: Position
components.summary: Centers the view at an absolute point in the parent's coordinate space, then claims all available space for layout.

properties.name: x
properties.notes: Range 0...300. X coordinate of the view's center in parent space.

properties.name: y
properties.notes: Range 0...300. Y coordinate of the view's center.

components.higNotes: position consumes all proposed space and centers the child at the given point. Use sparingly; stacks and alignment guides are usually clearer for structured layout.
components.codeTemplate:
Text("Positioned")
    .position(x: {x}, y: {y})

components.title: Fixed Size
components.summary: Fixes the view at its ideal size on the chosen axes, overriding the parent's proposed size (e.g. prevents Text truncation).

properties.name: horizontal
properties.notes: When true, the view keeps its ideal width regardless of the parent proposal.

properties.name: vertical
properties.notes: When true, the view keeps its ideal height.

components.higNotes: The most common use is .fixedSize(horizontal: false, vertical: true) to let multi-line Text grow vertically instead of truncating. Avoid fixing both axes unless the content is genuinely intrinsic.
components.codeTemplate:
Text("A very long line of text that would otherwise truncate")
    .fixedSize(horizontal: {horizontal}, vertical: {vertical})

components.title: Layout Priority
components.summary: Sets the relative priority a view has when a stack divides limited space among its children.

properties.name: value
properties.notes: Range -10...10. Higher values get space first; default for all views is 0.

components.higNotes: Raise layoutPriority on the element that should win when an HStack/VStack is tight (e.g. a title over a trailing badge). Keep the scale small and intentional.

components.title: Alignment Guide
components.summary: Overrides how a view reports its position for a named alignment guide, enabling custom cross-stack alignment.

properties.name: alignment
properties.notes: The named guide to override. Horizontal guides for HStack cross-alignment, vertical for VStack.

properties.name: offset
properties.notes: Range -50...50. Demonstrates returning d[guide] + offset from computeValue.

components.higNotes: An advanced layout tool. Reach for it only when standard stack alignments and alignment IDs cannot express the relationship. Document the guide math because it is non-obvious to future readers.

components.title: Safe Area Inset
components.summary: Insets the view's safe area by an auxiliary view pinned to an edge (e.g. a floating bar over a scroll view).

properties.name: edge
properties.notes: Edge the auxiliary content pins to. Vertical edges (top/bottom) and horizontal edges (leading/trailing) use different overloads.

properties.name: alignment
properties.notes: Alignment of the inset content along the cross axis.

properties.name: spacing
properties.notes: Range 0...32. Gap between the primary content and the inset view; nil uses the default.

components.higNotes: Use this to keep content clear of floating controls (toolbars, action bars). Scrollable content correctly insets so the last items remain reachable above the bar.

components.title: Safe Area Padding
components.summary: Extends the safe area by a fixed amount on the chosen edges without inserting an auxiliary view.

properties.name: edges
properties.notes: Edges to pad within the safe area.

properties.name: length
properties.notes: Range 0...48. nil uses the system default padding.

components.higNotes: Prefer over manual padding when you want scroll content to respect added margins while still scrolling edge-to-edge. Common for horizontally scrolling carousels.

components.title: Container Relative Frame
components.summary: Sizes a view relative to its nearest container (scroll view, window) rather than its immediate parent, optionally dividing into spaced columns.

properties.name: axes
properties.notes: Which axes are sized relative to the container.

properties.name: count
properties.notes: Range 1...5. Divides the container into N columns/rows (count:span:spacing: overload).

properties.name: span
properties.notes: Range 1...5. How many of the N divisions this view spans.

properties.name: spacing
properties.notes: Range 0...32. Gap between divisions when count > 1.

properties.name: alignment
properties.notes: Alignment of content within the resolved frame.

components.higNotes: The modern way to build snapping carousels: combine with .scrollTargetBehavior(.viewAligned). Use count/span to show fractional peeks of the next card.

components.title: Ignores Safe Area
components.summary: Lets the view extend under the safe area (notch, home indicator, keyboard) on the specified regions and edges.

properties.name: regions
properties.notes: Which safe-area regions to ignore. .keyboard prevents content shifting when the keyboard appears.

properties.name: edges
properties.notes: Edges on which to extend beyond the safe area.

components.higNotes: Reserve for full-bleed backgrounds and media. Never place interactive controls or critical text in the ignored region — keep them within the safe area.
components.codeTemplate:
Color.accentColor
    .ignoresSafeArea(.{regions}, edges: .{edges})

components.title: Foreground Style
components.summary: Sets the style (color, hierarchical level, gradient, or material) used to fill foreground content such as text and symbols.

properties.name: primary
properties.notes: Primary fill style. Hierarchical styles (.secondary etc.) adapt to context. Supports up to three layered styles via the multi-argument overload.

properties.name: color
properties.notes: Live color when a solid Color is chosen for primary.

components.higNotes: Prefer hierarchical styles (.secondary/.tertiary) and semantic colors over raw .white/.black so content adapts to Dark Mode and vibrancy. Supersedes the deprecated foregroundColor.
components.codeTemplate:
Label("Styled", systemImage: "star.fill")
    .foregroundStyle({primary})

components.title: Background
components.summary: Layers a style or view behind the modified view, optionally clipped to a shape; can also extend into ignored safe-area edges.

properties.name: style
properties.notes: Background fill. Materials provide system blur; provide an opaque fallback for Reduce Transparency.

properties.name: shape
properties.notes: Optional clip shape for the background fill (the in: parameter).

properties.name: alignment
properties.notes: Alignment of a background view built with the @ViewBuilder overload.

components.higNotes: Use materials for layered, translucent surfaces; always supply an opaque fallback under Reduce Transparency. Avoid raw .white/.black backgrounds — use semantic .background or design tokens.
components.codeTemplate:
Text("Card")
    .padding()
    .background({style}, in: {shape})

components.title: Overlay
components.summary: Layers a style or view in front of the modified view, sized to match it, aligned by the given alignment.

properties.name: style
properties.notes: Overlay fill when using the style+shape overload (e.g. a scrim or stroke gradient).

properties.name: shape
properties.notes: Clip shape for the style overlay.

properties.name: alignment
properties.notes: Alignment for a @ViewBuilder overlay, e.g. a badge in the corner.

components.higNotes: Ideal for badges, strokes, and scrims that must track the host view's size. Keep overlays non-interactive unless intended; combine with allowsHitTesting(false) for decorative layers.

components.title: Border
components.summary: Draws a rectangular border of the given style and width along the view's edges.

properties.name: content
properties.notes: Border style — typically a Color, but any ShapeStyle works.

properties.name: width
properties.notes: Range 0...12. Border line width.

components.higNotes: border draws a square-cornered stroke that ignores clip shapes. For rounded borders use .overlay(RoundedRectangle().stroke(...)) instead.
components.codeTemplate:
Text("Bordered")
    .padding()
    .border({content}, width: {width})

components.title: Clip Shape
components.summary: Clips the view to the outline of any Shape, hiding anything outside the shape's path.

properties.name: shape
properties.notes: The clipping shape. Use .rect(cornerRadii:) for per-corner control.

properties.name: style
properties.notes: Anti-aliasing / even-odd fill rule. Default is fine for most cases.

components.higNotes: Preferred replacement for the deprecated cornerRadius modifier. Apply before shadow so the shadow follows the clipped silhouette.
components.codeTemplate:
Image("photo")
    .resizable()
    .scaledToFill()
    .frame(width: 120, height: 120)
    .clipShape({shape})

components.title: Clipped
components.summary: Clips the view to its own bounding rectangle, hiding content that overflows the frame.

properties.name: antialiased
properties.notes: Smooths the clip edge at a small performance cost.

components.higNotes: Use to prevent oversized children (e.g. a scaled image) from spilling past their frame. For non-rectangular clips, use clipShape.
components.codeTemplate:
Image("photo")
    .resizable()
    .scaledToFill()
    .frame(width: 100, height: 100)
    .clipped(antialiased: {antialiased})

components.title: Corner Radius (Deprecated)
components.summary: Clips the view to a rounded rectangle of the given radius. Deprecated as of OS 26.5 — use clipShape or fill instead.

properties.name: radius
properties.notes: Range 0...48. Corner radius applied uniformly to all four corners.

properties.name: antialiased
properties.notes: Smooths the rounded edges.

components.higNotes: Deprecated in OS 26.5. Migrate to .clipShape(.rect(cornerRadius:)) or .clipShape(RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius:)). Include in the showroom only as a labeled legacy/deprecated example.
components.codeTemplate:
Color.accentColor
    .frame(width: 120, height: 80)
    .cornerRadius({radius}, antialiased: {antialiased})

components.title: Mask
components.summary: Uses the alpha channel of a mask view to control which parts of the modified view are visible.

properties.name: alignment
properties.notes: Alignment of the mask content relative to the masked view.

properties.name: maskShape
properties.notes: The mask content; opaque areas show the view, transparent areas hide it. Gradient masks create fades.

components.higNotes: Great for gradient fade-outs at scroll edges and shaped reveals. Heavy masks can be expensive; pair with drawingGroup() if animating.

components.title: Shadow
components.summary: Draws a drop shadow behind the view with configurable color, blur radius, and offset.

properties.name: color
properties.notes: Shadow color including its opacity.

properties.name: radius
properties.notes: Range 0...40. Blur radius of the shadow.

properties.name: x
properties.notes: Range -20...20. Horizontal offset.

properties.name: y
properties.notes: Range -20...20. Vertical offset.

components.higNotes: Use subtle shadows for elevation; keep them consistent via design tokens. Apply after clipShape so the shadow follows the clipped outline. For inner/colored shadows on shapes use .shadow(.inner(...)) ShapeStyle modifiers.
components.codeTemplate:
Text("Elevated")
    .padding()
    .background(.regularMaterial, in: RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius: 12))
    .shadow(color: {color}, radius: {radius}, x: {x}, y: {y})

components.title: Opacity
components.summary: Sets the transparency of the view and all its content, from fully transparent (0) to fully opaque (1).

properties.name: opacity
properties.notes: Range 0...1. Multiplies the existing opacity of the content.

components.higNotes: Use for fades and disabled-state dimming. Avoid driving opacity to 0 to 'hide' interactive views — they remain hittable; use conditional rendering or allowsHitTesting(false).
components.codeTemplate:
Text("Faded")
    .opacity({opacity})

components.title: Tint
components.summary: Overrides the accent color for controls within the view hierarchy (buttons, toggles, progress, selection).

properties.name: tint
properties.notes: Tint applied to controls. Some overloads accept any ShapeStyle. nil reverts to the inherited accent.

components.higNotes: Prefer tint over deprecated accentColor. Use sparingly and consistently; the system tint already conveys interactivity, so reserve custom tints for brand or semantic meaning.

components.title: Glass Effect (Liquid Glass)
components.summary: Applies a Liquid Glass material to the view — a dynamic translucent surface that refracts and reflects its surroundings.

properties.name: glass
properties.notes: Glass variant. Chain .tint(_:) and .interactive(_:) for tinted/interactive glass.

properties.name: shape
properties.notes: Shape the glass is clipped to (the in: parameter). Defaults to Capsule when omitted.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: Toggles the glass effect on/off without removing the modifier.

components.higNotes: Adopt for floating controls and overlays per iOS 26 design. Group adjacent glass elements in a GlassEffectContainer for correct blending and morphing. Always gate with #available(iOS 26, *) and provide a material fallback.
components.codeTemplate:
Image(systemName: "star.fill")
    .padding()
    .glassEffect({glass}, in: {shape}, isEnabled: {isEnabled})

components.title: Background Extension Effect
components.summary: Extends the view's content beyond the safe area by mirroring and blurring its edges, creating a seamless full-bleed backdrop (iOS 26).

properties.name: enabled
properties.notes: Showroom toggle to apply/remove the modifier and compare against a plain background.

components.higNotes: Designed to pair with edge-to-edge media and Liquid Glass layouts so artwork bleeds gracefully under sidebars and toolbars. Use behind imagery, not behind legible text.
components.codeTemplate:
Image("hero")
    .resizable()
    .scaledToFill()
    .backgroundExtensionEffect()

components.title: Rotation Effect (2D)
components.summary: Rotates the view in 2D by an angle around an anchor point, without affecting layout.

properties.name: angle
properties.notes: Range -360...360 degrees. Positive is clockwise.

properties.name: anchor
properties.notes: Point the rotation pivots around.

components.higNotes: Layout footprint stays at the unrotated bounds. Combine with animation for spinners; guard looping rotations with accessibilityReduceMotion.
components.codeTemplate:
Image(systemName: "arrow.up")
    .rotationEffect({angle}, anchor: .{anchor})

components.title: Scale Effect
components.summary: Scales the view by independent x/y factors around an anchor, without affecting its layout footprint.

properties.name: scaleX
properties.notes: Range 0...3. Horizontal scale factor. Use the single-CGFloat overload for uniform scaling.

properties.name: scaleY
properties.notes: Range 0...3. Vertical scale factor.

properties.name: anchor
properties.notes: Point that stays fixed while scaling.

components.higNotes: Use for press feedback and emphasis. Scaling raster images blurs them; scale vector content where possible. Layout footprint remains unscaled.
components.codeTemplate:
Image(systemName: "heart.fill")
    .scaleEffect(x: {scaleX}, y: {scaleY}, anchor: .{anchor})

components.title: Rotation 3D Effect
components.summary: Rotates the view in 3D around an arbitrary axis with optional perspective foreshortening.
components.minOS: iOS 13.0 / macOS 10.15 / tvOS 13.0 / watchOS 6.0 (perspective variant deprecated on visionOS)

properties.name: angle
properties.notes: Range -180...180 degrees.

properties.name: axis
properties.notes: Rotation axis. (0,1,0) flips horizontally like a card.

properties.name: anchor
properties.notes: Anchor point of the rotation in the view plane.

properties.name: anchorZ
properties.notes: Range -100...100. Z-depth of the anchor.

properties.name: perspective
properties.notes: Range 0...2. Strength of perspective foreshortening. Deprecated on visionOS; use the no-perspective overload there.

components.higNotes: Use for card flips and depth transitions. On visionOS prefer rotation3DEffect without perspective. Guard continuous 3D animation with accessibilityReduceMotion.
components.codeTemplate:
CardFront()
    .rotation3DEffect({angle}, axis: (x: {axis.x}, y: {axis.y}, z: {axis.z}), anchor: .{anchor}, anchorZ: {anchorZ}, perspective: {perspective})

components.title: Projection Effect
components.summary: Applies an arbitrary 3D ProjectionTransform (CATransform3D-style matrix) to the view.

properties.name: transform
properties.notes: The 3x3 projection matrix to apply. For common cases prefer rotationEffect/scaleEffect.

components.higNotes: Low-level; reach for the named transform modifiers first. Use projectionEffect only for custom matrices (skews, shears) not expressible otherwise.
components.codeTemplate:
Text("Projected")
    .projectionEffect({transform})

components.title: Transform Effect (2D Affine)
components.summary: Applies a 2D affine transform (translate, scale, rotate, skew) to the view's rendering.

properties.name: transform
options: CGAffineTransform(a: 1, b: 0, c: 0.3, d: 1, tx: 0, ty: 0)
properties.notes: The affine matrix. The last option demonstrates a horizontal skew.

components.higNotes: Useful for skews and combined affine transforms. Does not affect layout. For simple rotate/scale, prefer the dedicated modifiers for clarity and animatability.
components.codeTemplate:
Text("Skewed")
    .transformEffect({transform})

components.title: Blur
components.summary: Applies a Gaussian blur to the view's rendered content.

properties.name: radius
properties.notes: Range 0...30. Larger radius blurs more.

properties.name: opaque
properties.notes: When true, treats edge pixels as opaque, avoiding transparent fringing.

components.higNotes: Blur is GPU-bound; avoid animating large blurred areas every frame. For frosted backgrounds prefer Materials over manual blur, which adapt to vibrancy and Reduce Transparency.
components.codeTemplate:
Image("photo")
    .blur(radius: {radius}, opaque: {opaque})

components.title: Brightness
components.summary: Adds a constant to each color channel, lightening (positive) or darkening (negative) the view.

properties.name: amount
properties.notes: Range -1...1. 0 leaves the view unchanged; 1 forces white, -1 forces black.

components.higNotes: A simple additive adjustment, not perceptual. For press states prefer opacity or overlay scrims, which read more naturally than raw brightness shifts.
components.codeTemplate:
Image("photo")
    .brightness({amount})

components.title: Contrast
components.summary: Multiplies the color separation from gray, increasing (>1) or decreasing (<1) contrast.

properties.name: amount
properties.notes: Range 0...3. 1 is unchanged; 0 collapses to gray; negative inverts.

components.higNotes: Distinct from the system Increase Contrast accessibility setting (read via accessibilityContrast). Use this modifier only for deliberate visual effects, not to satisfy contrast requirements.
components.codeTemplate:
Image("photo")
    .contrast({amount})

components.title: Saturation
components.summary: Adjusts color intensity; 0 produces grayscale, values above 1 oversaturate.

properties.name: amount
properties.notes: Range 0...2. 0 is grayscale; 1 is unchanged.

components.higNotes: Useful for de-emphasizing inactive media (e.g. desaturate a disabled thumbnail). Combine with opacity for a clear disabled affordance.
components.codeTemplate:
Image("photo")
    .saturation({amount})

components.title: Grayscale
components.summary: Desaturates the view toward gray by the given amount.

properties.name: amount
properties.notes: Range 0...1. 0 unchanged; 1 fully gray.

components.higNotes: Idiomatic for indicating disabled or inactive imagery. Pair with reduced opacity for a stronger disabled read.
components.codeTemplate:
Image("photo")
    .grayscale({amount})

components.title: Color Invert
components.summary: Inverts each color channel of the view's content.

properties.name: enabled
properties.notes: Showroom toggle to apply/remove the inversion for before/after comparison.

components.higNotes: A blunt effect; rarely appropriate for production UI. Do not use to fake Dark Mode — adopt semantic colors and color schemes instead.
components.codeTemplate:
Image("photo")
    .colorInvert()

components.title: Color Multiply
components.summary: Multiplies the view's colors by a tint color, darkening toward that hue.

properties.name: color
properties.notes: .white leaves content unchanged; darker/colored values tint and darken it.

components.higNotes: Multiply blending only darkens. To tint white artwork toward a brand color, prefer foregroundStyle on template/symbol images for crisper, more predictable results.
components.codeTemplate:
Image("photo")
    .colorMultiply({color})

components.title: Hue Rotation
components.summary: Shifts every color's hue around the color wheel by the given angle.

properties.name: angle
properties.notes: Range 0...360 degrees. Animatable for rainbow cycling effects.

components.higNotes: Fun for playful, animated accents but disorienting if overused. Guard any continuous hue cycling with accessibilityReduceMotion.
components.codeTemplate:
Image("photo")
    .hueRotation({angle})

components.title: Blend Mode
components.summary: Sets how the view composites with the content behind it (multiply, screen, overlay, etc.).

properties.name: blendMode
properties.notes: Compositing operation against the backdrop.

components.higNotes: Blend modes composite against whatever is behind the view, so results depend on the backdrop. Wrap interacting layers in compositingGroup() to control the blending scope.
components.codeTemplate:
Text("BLEND")
    .font(.largeTitle.bold())
    .blendMode(.{blendMode})

components.title: Compositing Group
components.summary: Renders the view and its children as a single flattened layer before applying effects like opacity, blend mode, or shadow.

properties.name: enabled
properties.notes: Toggle to show how grouping changes how opacity/shadow apply to overlapping children.

components.higNotes: Apply before opacity or shadow when overlapping children would otherwise composite individually (e.g. avoid double-darkened overlaps). Defines the blend scope for blendMode.

components.title: Drawing Group (Metal)
components.summary: Flattens the view into an offscreen Metal-rendered image, improving performance for complex composited or animated effects.

properties.name: opaque
properties.notes: When true the offscreen buffer has no alpha channel.

properties.name: colorMode
properties.notes: Color space used for offscreen rendering.

components.higNotes: A performance tool for heavy effect stacks (many blurs/gradients/shadows). It rasterizes content, so do not wrap text you need crisp at all scales. Measure before and after; it can hurt simple views.
components.codeTemplate:
ComplexGradientArt()
    .drawingGroup(opaque: {opaque}, colorMode: .{colorMode})

components.title: Disabled
components.summary: Disables user interaction for the view and its controls, dimming them and blocking input.

properties.name: disabled
properties.notes: When true, blocks interaction and applies the disabled appearance. Read inside children via @Environment(\.isEnabled).

components.higNotes: Preferred over manual dimming because it also updates accessibility traits (VoiceOver announces 'dimmed/disabled'). Avoid disabling without explaining why the control is unavailable.
components.codeTemplate:
Button("Submit") { submit() }
    .disabled({disabled})

components.title: Allows Hit Testing
components.summary: Controls whether the view participates in hit testing; when false, touches pass through to views behind it.

properties.name: enabled
properties.notes: When false, the view (and children) ignore touches but stay visible and enabled-looking.

components.higNotes: Use for purely decorative overlays so they don't intercept taps. Unlike disabled, it does not dim the view or change accessibility traits.
components.codeTemplate:
DecorativeOverlay()
    .allowsHitTesting({enabled})

components.title: Content Shape
components.summary: Defines the hit-testing (and optionally preview/drag) shape of the view, enlarging or restricting its tappable region.

properties.name: kind
properties.notes: Which interaction the shape applies to. Default overload applies to .interaction.

properties.name: shape
properties.notes: The shape used for hit testing. Rectangle over an HStack makes the whole row tappable, including spacers.

components.higNotes: Use .contentShape(Rectangle()) to make an entire row tappable instead of just its opaque content. Also shapes context-menu/drag previews via the kind parameter.

components.title: Z-Index
components.summary: Sets the front-to-back stacking order of the view within its parent layout (higher draws on top).

properties.name: value
properties.notes: Range -10...10. Default is 0; ties resolve in declaration order.

components.higNotes: Affects sibling ordering within a single container (ZStack/overlay). Essential for keeping a view on top during matchedGeometryEffect/transition animations where default order would clip it.

components.title: Aspect Ratio
components.summary: Constrains the view's dimensions to a given width-to-height ratio, fitting or filling the parent context. Pass nil to preserve a resizable view's intrinsic ratio.

properties.name: aspectRatio
properties.notes: Range 0.25...4. Width-to-height ratio. nil keeps the content's own aspect ratio (use a 'set ratio' toggle to expose nil).

properties.name: contentMode
properties.notes: .fit scales to be fully visible inside the frame; .fill scales to cover it (may overflow — pair with clipped()).

components.higNotes: The idiomatic way to keep media at a consistent ratio across layouts. There is also an aspectRatio(_:contentMode:) overload that accepts a CGSize. For images, scaledToFit()/scaledToFill() are convenience shorthands.
components.codeTemplate:
Image("photo")
    .resizable()
    .aspectRatio({aspectRatio}, contentMode: .{contentMode})

components.title: Scaled To Fit / Fill
components.summary: Scales the view to fit inside (or fill) its parent while preserving aspect ratio — convenience shorthands for aspectRatio(nil, contentMode:).

properties.name: mode
properties.notes: Selects scaledToFit() (.fit) vs scaledToFill() (.fill). Both are parameterless modifiers; this control just picks which one the showroom applies.

components.higNotes: scaledToFill() commonly overflows the frame, so combine it with .frame(...) plus .clipped() or .clipShape(...) to crop. scaledToFit() is equivalent to aspectRatio(nil, contentMode: .fit).
components.codeTemplate:
Image("photo")
    .resizable()
    .scaledToFill()
    .frame(width: 120, height: 120)
    .clipped()

components.title: Redacted
components.summary: Applies a redaction (such as a placeholder shimmer) to the view hierarchy, replacing real content with a privacy- or loading-style obscured rendering.

properties.name: reason
properties.notes: .placeholder renders generic shapes (loading skeleton); .privacy obscures sensitive content; [] removes redaction. Read inside children via @Environment(\.redactionReasons).

components.higNotes: The native way to build loading skeletons (.placeholder) and to protect sensitive content while screen sharing (.privacy). Use unredacted() or privacySensitive(_:) to opt subviews in/out. Combine with .redacted on a stub layout for skeleton screens.

components.title: Hidden
components.summary: Hides the view unconditionally while keeping it in the layout — it occupies space and affects sibling positioning but is not drawn and cannot be interacted with.

properties.name: hidden
properties.notes: Showroom toggle to apply/remove .hidden() for before/after comparison. hidden() itself takes no arguments; to conditionally remove a view from layout entirely, use an if statement instead.

components.higNotes: Use to reserve layout space for a view you don't want drawn (e.g. keep an HStack's spacing stable). To omit a view from layout entirely, prefer a conditional `if` over .hidden(). Unlike opacity(0), a hidden view also drops out of hit testing.

components.title: Glass Effect
components.summary: Applies the Liquid Glass material to a view, clipping it to a shape.
components.higNotes: Liquid Glass is for the navigation/control layer that floats above content — toolbars, custom controls, floating action elements. Do not stack glass on glass, and avoid using it for large background fills. Let content show through; keep glass elements compact.

properties.name: variant
properties.notes: Glass.regular adapts to its background; .clear is more transparent for media-rich backdrops; .identity is a no-op placeholder used to morph in/out within a container.

properties.name: tint
properties.notes: Applied via Glass.tint(_:). nil keeps the neutral system tint. Use sparingly for meaning, not decoration.

properties.name: isInteractive
properties.notes: Applied via Glass.interactive(_:). Adds touch-reactive scale/bounce/shimmer for tappable glass elements.

properties.name: shape
properties.notes: The clip shape for the glass. Default is a Capsule via DefaultGlassEffectShape.

properties.name: cornerRadius
properties.notes: Only relevant when shape is RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius:).

components.title: Glass Effect Container
components.summary: Groups multiple glass shapes so they render together and morph/blend into one another.
components.higNotes: Wrap clusters of glass elements (e.g. a toolbar row of glass buttons) in a single container so the system renders them as one optical layer and improves performance. Required for glassEffectID morphing and glassEffectUnion to work.

properties.name: spacing
properties.notes: Controls how soon adjacent glass shapes begin blending as they approach. Higher spacing = blending begins sooner / from farther apart.

components.title: Glass Effect ID (Morph)
components.summary: Assigns a stable identity to a glass shape so it morphs smoothly as it appears, moves, or merges.
components.higNotes: Use to create fluid morph transitions between glass elements (e.g. a button expanding into a panel). Must be applied inside a GlassEffectContainer and paired with a @Namespace. Drive appearance/removal with withAnimation.

properties.name: id
properties.notes: Stable identity matched across state changes. nil disables morphing for this element.

properties.name: namespace
properties.notes: From @Namespace private var namespace. Shared across the views that morph into one another.

components.title: Glass Effect Union
components.summary: Merges several glass shapes sharing an id into one continuous glass shape.
components.higNotes: Use when a group of adjacent glass elements should read as a single connected surface (e.g. a segmented cluster). Requires a GlassEffectContainer and a shared @Namespace.

properties.name: id
properties.notes: Elements sharing this id unify into one glass shape. nil opts the element out of the union.

properties.name: namespace
properties.notes: Shared @Namespace.ID across the unioned elements.

components.title: Glass Effect Transition
components.summary: Controls how a glass shape animates as it is added to or removed from the hierarchy.
components.higNotes: Pair with glassEffectID inside a container for coordinated insert/remove animation. .materialize is the default 'liquid' appear/disappear; .matchedGeometry morphs from a matched element; .identity disables the transition.

properties.name: transition
properties.notes: materialize = fluid scale+blur appear; matchedGeometry = morph from a matched-id element; identity = no transition.

components.codeTemplate:
Image(systemName: "star.fill")
    .padding()
    .glassEffect()
    .glassEffectTransition(.{transition})

components.title: Glass Button Style
components.summary: Renders a button with Liquid Glass border artwork, optionally prominent (accent-filled).
components.higNotes: Use .glass for standard floating actions and .glassProminent for the single primary action on a glass surface. Combine with .buttonBorderShape and .tint. Don't overuse prominent glass — one primary per context.

properties.name: style
properties.notes: .glass = neutral glass border; .glassProminent = tinted/filled glass for the primary action.

properties.name: tint
properties.notes: Drives the prominent fill and interactive highlight via .tint(_:).

properties.name: buttonBorderShape
properties.notes: Shapes the glass border artwork.

properties.name: controlSize
properties.notes: Standard control sizing applied via .controlSize.

components.codeTemplate:
Button("Action") { }
    .buttonStyle(.{style})
    .buttonBorderShape(.{buttonBorderShape})
    .controlSize(.{controlSize})
    .tint({tint})

components.title: Toolbar Spacer
components.summary: Inserts fixed or flexible spacing between toolbar items, splitting them into separate glass groups.
components.higNotes: Use to break a toolbar into distinct glass clusters (e.g. separate a destructive action from navigation actions). A .flexible spacer pushes groups to opposite edges; a .fixed spacer adds a measured gap within a group.

properties.name: sizing
properties.notes: .flexible expands to fill available space and separates glass groups; .fixed uses a precise width.

properties.name: placement
properties.notes: Where the spacer (and its surrounding group) sits in the toolbar.

components.title: Scroll Edge Effect Style
components.summary: Configures the soft/hard blur-fade applied to scroll content meeting an edge under floating bars.
components.higNotes: The scroll edge effect keeps content legible as it passes under translucent glass bars. Use .soft for a gradual fade under bars; .hard for a crisp cutoff when content must not bleed into a bar. Leave .automatic unless a specific layout demands otherwise.

properties.name: style
properties.notes: nil falls back to system automatic. .soft = gradual blur fade; .hard = sharp edge.

properties.name: edges
properties.notes: Which edges receive the effect.

components.title: Scroll Edge Effect Hidden
components.summary: Hides the scroll edge blur-fade effect on specified edges.
components.higNotes: Hide the edge effect only when content already provides its own visual separation from the bar (e.g. an opaque header). Otherwise keep it for legibility.

properties.name: isHidden
properties.notes: true removes the edge effect on the given edges.

properties.name: edges
properties.notes: Which edges to hide the effect on.

components.codeTemplate:
ScrollView { /* content */ }
    .scrollEdgeEffectHidden({isHidden}, for: .{edges})

components.title: Background Extension Effect
components.summary: Mirrors and blurs a view into surrounding safe-area regions for an immersive edge-to-edge backdrop.
components.higNotes: Use sparingly on a single background element (commonly a banner in the detail column of a NavigationSplitView) so it extends under sidebar/inspector glass. Watch performance and visual clarity; never apply to interactive content.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: Toggles the mirrored-blur extension. Use the isEnabled overload to gate it dynamically.

components.codeTemplate:
Image("banner")
    .resizable()
    .scaledToFill()
    .backgroundExtensionEffect(isEnabled: {isEnabled})

components.title: Implicit Animation (animation:value:)
components.summary: Animates view changes implicitly whenever a specified equatable value changes.
components.higNotes: Prefer the value-scoped overload (animation(_:value:)) over the deprecated global form. Keep animations purposeful and brief. Always honor Reduce Motion by gating or swapping to a crossfade.

properties.name: animation
properties.notes: nil removes animation for the change. Curve/spring family selected here.

properties.name: duration
properties.notes: Only applies to timing-curve animations (easeInOut/linear/etc.); ignored by parameterless springs.

properties.name: value
properties.notes: The observed value; the animation runs each time it changes.

components.codeTemplate:
circle
    .scaleEffect(isBig ? 1.5 : 1.0)
    .animation(.{animation}, value: {value})

components.title: withAnimation
components.summary: Explicitly animates state changes performed inside its closure.
components.higNotes: Use for event-driven changes (button taps, gestures). Use withAnimation(_:completion:) on iOS 17+ to run code after the animation finishes. Keep durations short; respect Reduce Motion.

properties.name: animation
properties.notes: nil performs the change without animation.

properties.name: useCompletion
properties.notes: Switches to withAnimation(_:completion:) (iOS 17+) to run a closure when the animation completes.

components.title: Spring Animations (bouncy/smooth/snappy)
components.summary: Physically based spring animation presets with tunable duration and bounce.
components.higNotes: Springs feel more natural than timing curves for UI motion. .smooth has no bounce, .snappy has a small bounce, .bouncy has more. Prefer the duration/bounce parameterization over raw stiffness/damping.

properties.name: preset
properties.notes: Family of spring. interpolatingSpring is velocity-preserving for chained gestures.

properties.name: duration
properties.notes: Perceptual settling duration of the spring.

properties.name: bounce
properties.notes: 0 = no overshoot (critically damped), higher = more bounce. Ignored by .smooth which is fixed at 0.

components.codeTemplate:
view
    .offset(y: moved ? 120 : 0)
    .animation(.{preset}(duration: {duration}, extraBounce: {bounce}), value: moved)

components.title: Phase Animator
components.summary: Cycles a view through a sequence of discrete phases, animating between each.
components.higNotes: Use for multi-step canned animations (e.g. tap feedback that scales then settles). The trigger overload runs the sequence once per change; the trigger-less overload loops continuously — gate looping with Reduce Motion.

properties.name: phases
properties.notes: Ordered phases the content interpolates through.

properties.name: looping
properties.notes: false uses the trigger overload (one cycle per change); true uses the trigger-less continuously looping overload.

properties.name: animation
properties.notes: Animation used between phases; can vary per phase.

properties.name: trigger
properties.notes: Value whose change restarts the sequence (non-looping mode).

components.title: Keyframe Animator
components.summary: Drives complex multi-track animations over time using independent keyframe tracks.
components.higNotes: Use when multiple properties must animate on independent timelines (scale, rotation, offset). Each track uses CubicKeyframe/SpringKeyframe/LinearKeyframe/MoveKeyframe. Use the repeating overload only for ambient motion and gate with Reduce Motion.

properties.name: repeating
properties.notes: false uses the trigger overload; true loops indefinitely (initialValue/repeating overload).

properties.name: keyframeType
properties.notes: Interpolation type per keyframe segment.

properties.name: trigger
properties.notes: Value change that restarts the timeline (non-repeating mode).

properties.name: duration
properties.notes: Per-keyframe segment durations sum to the total; this drives the template's keyframe durations.

components.title: Transition
components.summary: Animates how a view is inserted into and removed from the hierarchy.
components.higNotes: Transitions only animate when the insertion/removal is itself inside a withAnimation or an .animation scope. blurReplace (iOS 17+) is the modern crossfade-with-blur. Keep transitions consistent within a flow.

properties.name: transition
properties.notes: Base transition. .scale and .move/.push take parameters.

properties.name: edge
properties.notes: Edge for .move(edge:) and .push(from:).

properties.name: scaleAnchor
properties.notes: Anchor for .scale(scale:anchor:).

properties.name: combineWithOpacity
properties.notes: When true, .combined(with: .opacity) is appended.

components.title: Asymmetric Transition
components.summary: Uses different transitions for view insertion versus removal.
components.higNotes: Use when entering and leaving should feel directional (e.g. slide up to appear, fade to dismiss). Keep the pairing intuitive so motion reinforces the spatial model.

properties.name: insertion
properties.notes: Transition used when the view appears.

properties.name: removal
properties.notes: Transition used when the view is removed.

components.title: Matched Geometry Effect
components.summary: Animates a view morphing between two layouts by matching frame geometry across a namespace.
components.higNotes: Use for hero transitions (grid thumbnail expanding to detail). Exactly one source per id at a time; drive the swap with withAnimation. For SwiftUI navigation, consider matchedTransitionSource/navigationTransition on iOS 18+.

properties.name: id
properties.notes: Matched identity shared by source and destination views.

properties.name: namespace
properties.notes: From @Namespace, shared by both views.

properties.name: properties
properties.notes: Which geometry properties to match.

properties.name: anchor
properties.notes: Anchor used to align the matched geometry.

properties.name: isSource
properties.notes: Marks which view provides the authoritative geometry.

components.title: Custom Transition
components.summary: A user-defined transition implementing the Transition protocol for bespoke insert/remove motion.
components.higNotes: Implement body(content:phase:) and switch on the TransitionPhase (willAppear/identity/didDisappear). Use .animation(_:) inside the transition to set its curve. Keep custom motion subtle and reversible.

properties.name: rotationDegrees
properties.notes: Example tunable: rotation applied during the non-identity phases.

properties.name: scaleAmount
properties.notes: Example tunable: scale applied during the non-identity phases.

properties.name: animation
properties.notes: Curve applied to the custom transition.

components.title: Symbol Effect
components.summary: Animates an SF Symbol with built-in effects like bounce, pulse, variableColor, or wiggle.
components.higNotes: Use symbol effects for meaningful feedback, not decoration. wiggle/breathe/rotate were added in iOS 18. Use the value: overload for one-shot effects on change and isActive: for indefinite effects (e.g. a loading pulse).

properties.name: effect
properties.notes: Effect family. variableColor/breathe/rotate/wiggle suit indefinite states; bounce suits one-shot feedback.

properties.name: trigger
properties.notes: Discrete mode: switch chooses value: (one-shot per change) vs isActive: (indefinite while true).

properties.name: repeating
properties.notes: Maps to SymbolEffectOptions.repeating for continuous playback.

properties.name: repeatCount
properties.notes: SymbolEffectOptions.repeat(_:) count; ignored when repeating is continuous.

properties.name: speed
properties.notes: SymbolEffectOptions.speed(_:) multiplier.

components.codeTemplate:
Image(systemName: "bell.fill")
    .symbolEffect(.{effect}, options: .repeat({repeatCount}).speed({speed}), value: {trigger})

components.title: Symbol Effects Removed
components.summary: Removes inherited symbol effects from a subtree so descendant symbols stay static.
components.higNotes: Use to opt a specific symbol out of an effect applied by an ancestor (e.g. a static badge inside an animated group).

properties.name: isRemoved
properties.notes: true strips inherited effects from this view's symbols.

components.codeTemplate:
Image(systemName: "star.fill")
    .symbolEffectsRemoved({isRemoved})

components.title: Content Transition
components.summary: Animates content changes in place (numeric rolls, symbol replace, opacity, interpolation).
components.higNotes: Use .numericText(countsDown:) for animated counters and .symbolEffect for symbol swaps. Must be wrapped in an animation to take effect. Ideal for live-updating values.

properties.name: transition
properties.notes: numericText animates digit rolls; symbolEffect animates SF Symbol replacement.

properties.name: countsDown
properties.notes: For .numericText(countsDown:) — direction of the digit roll.

components.codeTemplate:
Text("\(count)")
    .contentTransition(.{transition})
    .animation(.default, value: count)

components.title: Blur
components.summary: Applies a Gaussian blur to a view.
components.higNotes: Use for de-emphasis (e.g. sensitive content behind an unlock prompt) or depth. Heavy blur is GPU-costly; prefer materials for translucency over manual blur.

properties.name: radius
properties.notes: Blur strength in points.

properties.name: opaque
properties.notes: true blurs against an opaque background (no edge bleed transparency).

components.codeTemplate:
image
    .blur(radius: {radius}, opaque: {opaque})

components.title: Material Background
components.summary: Applies a system blur material as a background for translucency and vibrancy.
components.higNotes: On iOS 26 prefer glassEffect for floating control surfaces; reserve materials for legacy/secondary surfaces. Always provide an opaque fallback under Reduce Transparency.

properties.name: material
properties.notes: Thicker materials are more opaque and obscure more background.

properties.name: shape
properties.notes: Clip shape for the material background.

properties.name: cornerRadius
properties.notes: Used when shape is RoundedRectangle.

components.codeTemplate:
content
    .padding()
    .background(.{material}, in: {shape})

components.title: Color Filter Effects
components.summary: Per-pixel color adjustment filters applied to a view (hue, saturation, brightness, contrast, grayscale, color multiply).
components.higNotes: Use color filters for de-emphasis, theming, or live photo-style adjustments. Stack them sparingly — each is GPU work. hueRotation/saturation/grayscale animate smoothly and are good for selection/dimming feedback. Prefer foregroundStyle/tint for semantic color; reserve filters for visual treatment of arbitrary content (images, snapshots).

properties.name: filter
properties.notes: Which filter modifier to apply. hueRotation takes an Angle; brightness/contrast/saturation/grayscale take a Double; colorMultiply takes a Color.

properties.name: amount
properties.notes: Drives the Double-valued filters: saturation (1 = normal), brightness (0 = normal, range -1...1), contrast (1 = normal), grayscale (0 = none, 1 = full).

properties.name: hueAngle
properties.notes: Degrees of hue rotation; only used when filter is hueRotation.

properties.name: multiplyColor
properties.notes: Multiplied into each pixel; only used when filter is colorMultiply. White is a no-op.

components.codeTemplate:
image
    .saturation({amount})
    .hueRotation(.degrees({hueAngle}))
    .brightness({amount})
    .contrast({amount})
    .grayscale({amount})
    .colorMultiply({multiplyColor})

components.title: Shadow
components.summary: Draws a drop shadow behind a view with configurable color, blur radius, and offset.
components.higNotes: Use shadows for elevation and depth, not decoration. Keep them soft and subtle; harsh shadows read as dated. For inner shadows or layered drop/inner shadows on filled shapes prefer ShapeStyle.shadow(_:) with ShadowStyle.drop/.inner. On Liquid Glass surfaces let the system provide elevation rather than stacking manual shadows.

properties.name: color
properties.notes: Shadow color including its opacity. Default system shadow is a translucent black.

properties.name: radius
properties.notes: Blur radius of the shadow. 0 is a crisp edge.

properties.name: x
properties.notes: Horizontal offset of the shadow.

properties.name: y
properties.notes: Vertical offset of the shadow.

properties.name: shadowStyle
properties.notes: When applied via .foregroundStyle(.shadow(.drop/.inner(...))) on a shape, selects a drop (outer) or inner shadow. iOS 16+.

components.codeTemplate:
RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius: 16)
    .fill(.blue)
    .shadow(color: {color}, radius: {radius}, x: {x}, y: {y})

components.title: Metal Shader Effect
components.summary: Applies a custom Metal stitchable shader as a per-pixel color filter, full-layer effect, or geometric distortion.
components.higNotes: Shader effects require a Metal [[ stitchable ]] function in a .metal file referenced via ShaderLibrary. colorEffect modifies each pixel's color; layerEffect samples the rendered layer (for blur/displacement reading neighbors); distortionEffect remaps geometry. Not available on watchOS. Gate animated shaders with Reduce Motion and watch GPU cost. Pass uniforms as Shader arguments.

properties.name: effectKind
properties.notes: colorEffect: half4 fn(float2 pos, half4 color, args). layerEffect: half4 fn(float2 pos, SwiftUI::Layer layer, args). distortionEffect: float2 fn(float2 pos, args).

properties.name: maxSampleOffset
properties.notes: Required for layerEffect/distortionEffect — the maximum distance the shader samples/moves a pixel, so SwiftUI can size the render buffer. Ignored by colorEffect.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: Toggles the shader on/off without removing the modifier.

properties.name: uniform
properties.notes: Example tunable uniform passed into the Shader via ShaderLibrary.default.myShader(.float(uniform)).

components.title: Mesh Gradient
components.summary: A 2D grid of positioned colors interpolated into a smooth multi-color gradient surface.
components.higNotes: Use mesh gradients for rich, organic backgrounds and hero surfaces — they pair well with Liquid Glass floating controls layered on top. Animate the point positions for a living-gradient effect, gated by Reduce Motion. Keep contrast adequate for any foreground content/text.

properties.name: width
properties.notes: Number of columns in the control-point grid.

properties.name: height
properties.notes: Number of rows in the control-point grid. points.count must equal width * height.

properties.name: colorSpace
properties.notes: Interpolation color space for blending between vertex colors.

properties.name: smoothsColors
properties.notes: true uses cubic interpolation for smoother color transitions; false uses linear.

properties.name: background
properties.notes: Color shown outside the mesh bounds.

components.codeTemplate:
MeshGradient(
    width: {width},
    height: {height},
    points: [
        .init(0,0), .init(0.5,0), .init(1,0),
        .init(0,0.5), .init(0.5,0.5), .init(1,0.5),
        .init(0,1), .init(0.5,1), .init(1,1),
    ],
    colors: [.red, .purple, .indigo, .orange, .white, .blue, .yellow, .green, .mint],
    colorSpace: .{colorSpace},
    smoothsColors: {smoothsColors}
)

components.title: Scroll Transition
components.summary: Applies visual effects to a view as a function of its position within the containing scroll view.
components.higNotes: Use scroll transitions for subtle in/out emphasis as rows enter and leave the viewport (fade, scale, rotate). Keep effects light so content stays legible mid-scroll. The transition is symmetric across leading and trailing edges unless you use the topLeading/bottomTrailing overload. Honor Reduce Motion.

properties.name: configuration
properties.notes: interactive tracks scroll position continuously; animated snaps between phases with an animation; identity disables the transition.

properties.name: axis
properties.notes: Which scroll axis drives the transition. nil/automatic uses the innermost scroll view's axis.

properties.name: effect
properties.notes: Example visual effect to apply per phase. The closure receives (EmptyVisualEffect, ScrollTransitionPhase).

properties.name: identityScale
properties.notes: Scale applied in the non-identity (entering/leaving) phases; 1.0 at the identity phase.

components.codeTemplate:
row
    .scrollTransition(.{configuration}, axis: .{axis}) { content, phase in
        content
            .opacity(phase.isIdentity ? 1 : 0)
            .scaleEffect(phase.isIdentity ? 1 : {identityScale})
    }

components.title: Geometry-Driven Visual Effect
components.summary: Applies visual effects (offset, scale, blur, color filters) computed from the view's own geometry without affecting layout.
components.higNotes: visualEffect gives a closure access to the view's GeometryProxy so you can drive effects from frame/position (e.g. parallax, distance-based fade) without GeometryReader wrapping and without disturbing layout. Effects applied here are non-layout-affecting. Use visualEffect3D for depth on visionOS.

properties.name: effect
properties.notes: Which non-layout visual effect to derive from geometry.

properties.name: sourceMetric
properties.notes: Which GeometryProxy frame value feeds the effect (e.g. global midY for parallax).

properties.name: magnitude
properties.notes: Scales how strongly the geometry value maps into the chosen effect.

components.codeTemplate:
content
    .visualEffect { effect, proxy in
        effect
            .scaleEffect(1 + proxy.frame(in: .global).minY / 1000 * {magnitude})
    }

components.title: Safe Area Bar
components.summary: Places a floating bar of content along an edge, inset into the safe area so scroll content flows under it with the scroll edge effect.
components.higNotes: safeAreaBar is the iOS 26 companion to scroll edge effects: it reserves safe-area space for a floating glass bar (e.g. a bottom action bar) so content scrolls beneath it and gets the soft/hard edge treatment automatically. Prefer it over manual safeAreaInset for glass bars in iOS 26.

properties.name: edge
properties.notes: Which edge the bar attaches to.

properties.name: alignment
properties.notes: Alignment of the bar content within the reserved region.

properties.name: spacing
properties.notes: Spacing between the bar and the main content. nil uses the system default.

components.title: Accessibility Label
components.summary: Overrides the short, localized name VoiceOver reads for an element, replacing any inferred label.

properties.name: label
properties.notes: The spoken name. Keep it short, a noun phrase, no control type word (no 'button').

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: accessibilityLabel(_:isEnabled:) overload lets you conditionally apply the label.

properties.name: targetContent
properties.notes: Demonstrates the case where a label is mandatory: icon-only controls have no inferable text.

components.higNotes: Every actionable, icon-only, or image element needs a concise label. Do not include the trait noun ('button', 'image') — VoiceOver appends it from traits. Capitalize like a label, omit ending punctuation.
components.codeTemplate:
Image(systemName: "heart.fill")
    .accessibilityLabel({label}, isEnabled: {isEnabled})

components.title: Accessibility Value
components.summary: Supplies the current value VoiceOver reads after the label, for elements whose state changes.

properties.name: value
properties.notes: Current value spoken after label and before traits. Use human phrasing ('40 percent' not '0.4').

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: accessibilityValue(_:isEnabled:) overload conditionally applies the value.

components.higNotes: Provide a value only when the element has dynamic state (slider %, progress, stepper count). Pair with an adjustable action so VoiceOver users can change it. Spell numbers as people would say them.
components.codeTemplate:
ProgressView(value: 0.4)
    .accessibilityValue({value}, isEnabled: {isEnabled})

components.title: Accessibility Hint
components.summary: Adds a longer description of what activating an element does; spoken after a pause if hints are enabled.

properties.name: hint
properties.notes: Action-result phrasing, full sentence. Spoken only when VoiceOver hints are on.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: accessibilityHint(_:isEnabled:) overload conditionally applies the hint.

components.higNotes: Use sparingly — hints are a fallback, not the primary description. Start with a verb describing the outcome ('Plays the song'). Never duplicate the label. Users can disable hints, so do not put essential info here.
components.codeTemplate:
Button("Favorite") { }
    .accessibilityHint({hint}, isEnabled: {isEnabled})

components.title: Accessibility Traits
components.summary: Describes how an element behaves so assistive tech speaks the right role and offers the right gestures.

properties.name: addedTraits
properties.notes: OptionSet; combine with array literal. isToggle added iOS 17. Use isHeader for section titles to enable heading navigation.

properties.name: removedTraits
properties.notes: accessibilityRemoveTraits strips an inferred trait, e.g. remove isImage from a decorative glyph styled as text.

components.higNotes: Apply traits that match real behavior. isHeader powers the rotor's Headings navigation. isSelected marks the active item in a set. Do not fake isButton on non-interactive content. Prefer native controls that supply traits automatically.
components.codeTemplate:
Text("Section Title")
    .accessibilityAddTraits({addedTraits})
    .accessibilityRemoveTraits({removedTraits})

components.title: Accessibility Input Labels
components.summary: Provides alternate spoken names Voice Control and Switch Control accept to target an element.

properties.name: inputLabels
properties.notes: Ordered by preference; the first is the primary Voice Control phrase. Offer natural alternatives users might say.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: accessibilityInputLabels(_:isEnabled:) overload conditionally applies them.

components.higNotes: Add input labels when the visible label differs from what a Voice Control user would say, or for icon-only controls. Keep phrases short and speakable; avoid punctuation and symbols.

components.title: Accessibility Hidden
components.summary: Removes an element and its children from the accessibility tree without affecting visual layout.

properties.name: hidden
properties.notes: When true the element is invisible to VoiceOver. Use for purely decorative imagery or duplicated content.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: accessibilityHidden(_:isEnabled:) overload conditionally applies the hiding.

components.higNotes: Hide only decorative or redundant elements. Never hide interactive controls or essential information. Prefer combining children into one element over hiding meaningful content.
components.codeTemplate:
Image("decorative-pattern")
    .accessibilityHidden({hidden}, isEnabled: {isEnabled})

components.title: Accessibility Element (Children Behavior)
components.summary: Merges a subtree into a single element or controls how its children are exposed to assistive tech.

properties.name: children
properties.notes: .ignore makes a single opaque element (default); .combine merges children's labels/traits/actions; .contain keeps children navigable as a container.

components.higNotes: Combine a card's stacked Text/Image into one swipe stop with .combine so VoiceOver reads it as a unit. Use .contain for scroll containers and grids that should remain individually navigable. Avoid .ignore when children carry distinct meaning.

components.title: Accessibility Children (Synthetic)
components.summary: Replaces an element's children with synthetic, non-visual accessibility elements you build in a ViewBuilder.

properties.name: children
properties.notes: ViewBuilder content; SwiftUI hides the views visually and uses them only to generate a11y elements. Ideal for Canvas / custom drawing.

components.higNotes: Use for custom-drawn content (Canvas, GraphicsContext) that has no inherent elements. Give each synthetic child a clear label and value so VoiceOver can navigate the data points.

components.title: Accessibility Representation
components.summary: Substitutes a custom view's accessibility with that of a standard control for richer assistive behavior.

properties.name: representation
properties.notes: Returns a standard control whose full accessibility (traits, adjustable actions) is borrowed by the custom view.

components.higNotes: Reach for this when you build a custom control that mimics a system one — wrap a hidden Slider/Toggle so users get the native adjustable gestures and value semantics for free.

components.title: Dynamic Type Size
components.summary: Constrains or overrides the Dynamic Type size for a subtree and lets you read the user's current size.

properties.name: size
properties.notes: Single value pins the size; .large is the system default. accessibility1-5 are the larger AX sizes.

properties.name: range
properties.notes: dynamicTypeSize(_:) range overload clamps user choice. Never hard-cap below accessibility5 unless layout truly cannot adapt.

properties.name: isAccessibilitySize
properties.notes: Read-only computed on DynamicTypeSize; true for accessibility1-5. Use to switch to a stacked layout at AX sizes.

components.higNotes: Support all sizes through accessibility5. Use semantic text styles so text scales automatically. Switch HStack→VStack at .isAccessibilitySize. Avoid clamping ranges unless a fixed-size element (e.g. a watch complication) demands it.

components.title: ScaledMetric
components.summary: Property wrapper that scales a numeric value (padding, icon size) in step with the user's Dynamic Type size.

properties.name: wrappedValue
properties.notes: The base value at the default text size; the wrapper returns a scaled result. Common for icon frames and spacing.

properties.name: relativeTo
properties.notes: init(wrappedValue:relativeTo:) ties scaling to a specific text style's metrics rather than the default body.

components.higNotes: Scale non-text dimensions (icon size, custom row height, spacing) so they grow with text. Anchor with relativeTo: matching the adjacent text style. Do not scale fixed-asset dimensions that would break layout (e.g. hairline dividers).

components.title: Accessibility Action
components.summary: Adds default, escape, magic-tap, or named custom actions VoiceOver and Switch Control can invoke.

properties.name: kind
properties.notes: Standard action slot. .escape dismisses (two-finger scrub), .magicTap is the global two-finger double-tap, .showMenu opens context menu.

properties.name: name
properties.notes: accessibilityAction(named:_:) creates a named custom action surfaced in the VoiceOver actions rotor.

properties.name: intent
properties.notes: accessibilityAction(_:intent:) / (named:intent:) wire an action to an App Intent for system integration (iOS 18+).

components.higNotes: Expose every gesture-only action (swipe-to-delete, drag) as a named accessibility action so it is reachable without the gesture. Reserve .magicTap for the single most important action of a screen.

components.title: Accessibility Adjustable Action
components.summary: Handles VoiceOver swipe-up/down increment and decrement gestures for custom value controls.

properties.name: handler
properties.notes: Direction is .increment or .decrement. Update the bound value and keep accessibilityValue in sync.

components.higNotes: Pair with accessibilityValue so VoiceOver announces the new value after each adjustment. Use for custom sliders, ratings, segmented pickers, and steppers built from scratch.

components.title: Accessibility Scroll Action
components.summary: Responds to VoiceOver's three-finger scroll on custom paginated or carousel content.

properties.name: handler
properties.notes: Edge indicates scroll direction; advance your page/index accordingly. Built-in scroll views handle this automatically.

components.higNotes: Only needed for custom scroll/paging containers that do not use ScrollView/TabView. Move to the correct page based on the edge and post a focus/announcement update.

components.title: Accessibility Rotor
components.summary: Adds a custom VoiceOver rotor letting users jump directly to a curated set of elements or text ranges.

properties.name: rotorLabel
properties.notes: User-visible rotor name shown in the VoiceOver rotor wheel.

properties.name: entries
properties.notes: Entry IDs must match accessibilityRotorEntry / ForEach IDs in the same ScrollView so SwiftUI can scroll them into view.

properties.name: entryLabel
properties.notes: Key path to a user-visible label per entry; shown on macOS when listing rotor entries.

properties.name: systemRotor
properties.notes: Use the AccessibilitySystemRotor overload to replace a built-in rotor when default detection misses off-screen elements.

properties.name: note-overloads
properties.notes: The draft only models accessibilityRotor(_:entries:). Apple ships four overloads: accessibilityRotor(_:entries:), (_:entries:entryID:entryLabel:), (_:entries:entryLabel:), and (_:textRanges:) for text-range rotors. The textRanges overload is distinct enough to mention; consider adding a 'rotorKind' picker [element entries, text ranges].

components.higNotes: Attach to a ScrollView or an element directly inside one. Provide rotors for content users frequently seek (unread, VIPs, headings) — especially items lazily loaded off-screen that the system rotor cannot find.

components.title: Accessibility Focus State
components.summary: Reads and programmatically moves VoiceOver/Switch Control focus to a specific element.

properties.name: binding
properties.notes: accessibilityFocused(_:) Bool overload. Set the state to true to move focus here; reads true when focused.

properties.name: equalsValue
properties.notes: accessibilityFocused(_:equals:) drives focus among many elements via a shared @AccessibilityFocusState enum.

components.higNotes: Move focus only in response to meaningful events — surface a validation error, reveal new content, dismiss a sheet. Avoid stealing focus during routine updates; it disorients assistive-tech users.

components.title: Accessibility Custom Content
components.summary: Attaches extra label/value pairs that VoiceOver users can expand on demand via the More Content rotor.

properties.name: label
properties.notes: Short name for the extra detail. Reuse an AccessibilityCustomContentKey to update the same slot.

properties.name: value
properties.notes: The detail value spoken when the user expands custom content.

properties.name: importance
properties.notes: .high content is spoken automatically; .default is spoken only when the user requests more content.

components.higNotes: Use to keep the primary label concise while still offering rich secondary detail (flight times, metadata) without overwhelming every swipe. Mark only truly essential extras as .high.
components.codeTemplate:
FlightRow(flight: flight)
    .accessibilityElement(children: .combine)
    .accessibilityCustomContent({label}, {value}, importance: .{importance})

components.title: Accessibility Heading
components.summary: Marks an element as a heading at a given level so VoiceOver's heading rotor can navigate the hierarchy.

properties.name: level
properties.notes: Establishes a heading level for hierarchical navigation. Combine with isHeader trait for full support.

components.higNotes: Mark section titles as headings so users can skim with the Headings rotor. Keep levels consistent and nested correctly (h1 then h2), mirroring the visual hierarchy.
components.codeTemplate:
Text("Today")
    .accessibilityAddTraits(.isHeader)
    .accessibilityHeading(.{level})

components.title: Accessibility Text Content Type
components.summary: Tells VoiceOver the semantic kind of text so it adapts navigation and speech to that content.

properties.name: value
properties.notes: Hints how text should be navigated/spoken — e.g. .sourceCode reads punctuation, .messaging groups conversation turns.

components.higNotes: Set on large or specialized text views (code editors, chat transcripts, documents) so VoiceOver granularity and punctuation handling match the content domain.
components.codeTemplate:
CodeEditorView(text: $source)
    .accessibilityTextContentType(.{value})

components.title: Reduce Motion
components.summary: Read-only flag indicating the user prefers minimal animation; gate motion-heavy effects behind it.

properties.name: reduceMotion
properties.notes: Read via @Environment(\.accessibilityReduceMotion). When true, replace parallax/zoom/looping animation with a cross-fade or none.

components.higNotes: Honor Reduce Motion for any large, looping, parallax, or spring animation. Provide a subtle cross-fade or instant change instead. Essential affordance animations may remain if they are small and non-disorienting.

components.title: Reduce Transparency
components.summary: Read-only flag indicating the user prefers opaque backgrounds over translucent materials.

properties.name: reduceTransparency
properties.notes: Read via @Environment(\.accessibilityReduceTransparency). When true, swap .ultraThinMaterial / glass for a solid fill.

components.higNotes: Provide an opaque fallback for every blurred/material/glass surface. Translucency reduces legibility; respecting this preference is mandatory for backgrounds behind text.

components.title: Differentiate Without Color
components.summary: Read-only flag indicating the user needs cues beyond color (shapes, icons, text) to distinguish meaning.

properties.name: differentiateWithoutColor
properties.notes: Read via @Environment(\.accessibilityDifferentiateWithoutColor). When true add a glyph/shape/label alongside any color-coded status.

components.higNotes: Never rely on color alone to convey state (success/error, on/off, selected). When this is true, add a checkmark, shape, pattern, or text label so color-blind users get the same information.

components.title: Increase / Differentiate Contrast
components.summary: Read-only flag (.standard / .increased) reflecting the user's Increase Contrast preference.

properties.name: colorSchemeContrast
properties.notes: Read via @Environment(\.colorSchemeContrast). When .increased, raise foreground/background contrast and strengthen borders.

components.higNotes: When contrast is increased, use higher-contrast colors, add borders to controls, and avoid low-opacity text. Prefer asset-catalog 'High Contrast' color variants so the system swaps them automatically.

components.title: Invert Colors Handling
components.summary: Excludes media from Smart Invert and reports whether Invert Colors is active.

properties.name: ignoresInvert
properties.notes: accessibilityIgnoresInvertColors(_:) exempts photos/video/icons from color inversion under Smart Invert.

properties.name: invertColors
properties.notes: Read-only @Environment(\.accessibilityInvertColors); true when the user enabled Invert Colors.

components.higNotes: Apply accessibilityIgnoresInvertColors to images, video, and color-critical content so Smart Invert does not produce wrong colors. Leave text and UI chrome subject to inversion.
components.codeTemplate:
Image("product-photo")
    .accessibilityIgnoresInvertColors({ignoresInvert})

components.title: Button Shapes / On-Off Labels
components.summary: Read-only flag indicating the user wants visible shapes around tappable text-style buttons.

properties.name: showButtonShapes
properties.notes: Read via @Environment(\.accessibilityShowButtonShapes). When true, add an explicit background/border to plain text buttons.

components.higNotes: Native button styles honor this automatically. For custom borderless/plain buttons, add a visible shape (background, underline, border) when the flag is true so users can tell what is tappable.

components.title: Large Content Viewer
components.summary: Shows an enlarged HUD of an element's label/image when a Larger Text user long-presses it.

properties.name: enabled
properties.notes: accessibilityShowsLargeContentViewer() opts an element into the large-content HUD.

properties.name: label
