properties.notes: accessibilityShowsLargeContentViewer { } content builder customizes the enlarged label/icon shown in the HUD.

properties.name: isLargeContentViewerEnabled
properties.notes: Read-only @Environment(\.accessibilityLargeContentViewerEnabled) reports whether the system viewer is active.

components.higNotes: Use for small fixed-size controls that cannot scale — tab bar items, toolbar icons. The HUD lets Larger Text users read them on long-press without resizing the control itself.

components.title: Accessibility Sort Priority
components.summary: Adjusts the order in which VoiceOver visits elements within a container, higher first.

properties.name: priority
properties.notes: Higher priority is visited earlier. Default 0. Use to surface a primary action ahead of decorative or secondary elements.

components.higNotes: Override reading order only when the visual order misleads (e.g. a floating action button should be read after the list, or an alert title before its body). Keep it consistent with logical importance.

components.title: Accessibility Activation Point
components.summary: Specifies the exact screen point assistive tech uses when activating a combined or custom element.

properties.name: point
properties.notes: UnitPoint overload maps relative to the element's frame. Use when the default center would miss the real hit target.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: accessibilityActivationPoint(_:isEnabled:) conditionally applies the custom activation point.

components.higNotes: Set when a combined element's tappable region is not at its center — e.g. a row whose only control is a trailing button. Ensures VoiceOver double-tap hits the intended target.
components.codeTemplate:
RowView()
    .accessibilityElement(children: .combine)
    .accessibilityActivationPoint({point}, isEnabled: {isEnabled})

components.title: Accessibility Direct Touch
components.summary: Lets VoiceOver pass touches straight through to a region (e.g. a piano keyboard or drawing canvas).

properties.name: isDirectTouchArea
properties.notes: Marks the region for direct touch so gestures reach your view instead of VoiceOver.

properties.name: options
properties.notes: .requiresActivation makes the user double-tap to engage; .silentOnTouch mutes VoiceOver while interacting.

components.higNotes: Use only for instrument-like, gesture-driven surfaces where per-element VoiceOver navigation makes no sense. Prefer .requiresActivation so users opt in deliberately, and always offer an accessible alternative.
components.codeTemplate:
PianoKeyboardView()
    .accessibilityDirectTouch({isDirectTouchArea}, options: {options})

components.title: Accessibility Zoom Action
components.summary: Handles VoiceOver's zoom gesture so users can magnify custom zoomable content.

properties.name: handler
properties.notes: The action carries .direction (.zoomIn/.zoomOut) and a magnification value; update your zoom level and announce it.

components.higNotes: Add to custom maps, image viewers, or canvases that support pinch-to-zoom so VoiceOver users get an equivalent zoom control. Update accessibilityValue with the new zoom level.
components.codeTemplate:
MapCanvas(scale: $scale)
    .accessibilityZoomAction { action in
        scale *= action.direction == .zoomIn ? 1.25 : 0.8
    }

components.title: Accessibility Drag & Drop Points
components.summary: Exposes drag-and-drop targets to assistive tech as discrete, described points.

properties.name: dragPoint
properties.notes: accessibilityDragPoint(_:description:) marks where a drag begins for the element.

properties.name: dropPoint
properties.notes: accessibilityDropPoint(_:description:) marks a valid drop location with a spoken description.

properties.name: description
properties.notes: Spoken description of what the drag or drop accomplishes.

properties.name: minOS-correction
properties.notes: CORRECTION: accessibilityDragPoint/DropPoint are iOS 16.0+ (verified), not iOS 18.0 as drafted. Correct platforms: iOS 16.0+, iPadOS 16.0+, Mac Catalyst 16.0+, macOS 13.0+, visionOS 1.0+. No watchOS, no tvOS. Draft platforms [iOS, iPadOS, macOS] are otherwise fine but minOS should be 'iOS 16.0'. There is also an isEnabled overload: accessibilityDragPoint(_:description:isEnabled:) / accessibilityDropPoint(_:description:isEnabled:) — add an isEnabled toggle property.

components.higNotes: Make every drag-and-drop interaction reachable without precise pointing. Describe the outcome clearly ('Drop on Trash to delete') and provide both a drag origin and each meaningful drop target.
components.codeTemplate:
DraggableCard()
    .accessibilityDragPoint({dragPoint}, description: {description})
    .accessibilityDropPoint({dropPoint}, description: {description})

components.title: Accessibility Quick Action
components.summary: Surfaces a quick action (prompt or outline) that assistive-tech users can trigger contextually.

properties.name: style
properties.notes: .prompt presents a confirming prompt; .outline highlights the affected region before the action runs.

properties.name: isActive
properties.notes: accessibilityQuickAction(style:isActive:content:) gates whether the quick action is currently offered.

properties.name: content
properties.notes: ViewBuilder of the controls offered in the quick action surface.

properties.name: platforms-correction
properties.notes: CORRECTION: accessibilityQuickAction(style:content:) and (style:isActive:content:) are watchOS 9.0+ ONLY per Apple docs (verified). The draft's platforms array [iOS, iPadOS, macOS, watchOS] and minOS 'iOS 18.0' are WRONG. Set platforms to [watchOS] and minOS to 'watchOS 9.0'.

components.higNotes: Offer quick actions for the highest-value contextual operations on a screen. Keep the action set short and label each clearly so assistive-tech users can act without deep navigation.

components.title: Accessibility Identifier
components.summary: Sets a stable, non-localized identifier used by UI tests and automation, not spoken to users.

properties.name: identifier
properties.notes: Stable test identifier. Never localized; not announced by VoiceOver. Keep unique within a screen.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: accessibilityIdentifier(_:isEnabled:) conditionally applies the identifier.

components.higNotes: Use kebab- or dot-namespaced identifiers for XCUITest targeting. This is an automation hook, not a user-facing label — do not put spoken content here.
components.codeTemplate:
Button("Favorite") { }
    .accessibilityIdentifier({identifier}, isEnabled: {isEnabled})

components.title: Responds To User Interaction
components.summary: Declares whether an element is interactive, so assistive tech can correctly group or skip it.

properties.name: respondsToUserInteraction
properties.notes: Set false for a combined element that looks tappable but is purely informational, so VoiceOver does not imply interactivity.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: accessibilityRespondsToUserInteraction(_:isEnabled:) conditionally applies the value.

components.higNotes: Use to correct mismatches after combining children: if a merged card has no action, mark it non-interactive so VoiceOver does not suggest a double-tap. Keep in sync with real behavior.
components.codeTemplate:
InfoCard()
    .accessibilityElement(children: .combine)
    .accessibilityRespondsToUserInteraction({respondsToUserInteraction}, isEnabled: {isEnabled})

components.title: Speech Options
components.summary: Fine-tunes how VoiceOver pronounces an element: spelling, pitch, punctuation, and queueing.

properties.name: spellsOutCharacters
properties.notes: When true VoiceOver reads each character individually — ideal for codes, license plates, serials.

properties.name: adjustedPitch
properties.notes: Raises (positive) or lowers (negative) speech pitch to signal emphasis or context changes.

properties.name: alwaysIncludesPunctuation
properties.notes: Forces punctuation to be spoken regardless of the user's verbosity setting — useful for code.

properties.name: announcementsQueued
properties.notes: When true, announcements queue instead of interrupting the current utterance.

components.higNotes: Use spellsOutCharacters for opaque alphanumeric strings, pitch changes to mark a status, and includesPunctuation for code or formulas. Apply sparingly — overuse makes speech tiring.
components.codeTemplate:
Text(verbatim: confirmationCode)
    .speechSpellsOutCharacters({spellsOutCharacters})
    .speechAdjustedPitch({adjustedPitch})
    .speechAlwaysIncludesPunctuation({alwaysIncludesPunctuation})
    .speechAnnouncementsQueued({announcementsQueued})

components.title: Accessibility Chart Descriptor
components.summary: Provides an audio-graph and data-table description so VoiceOver users can explore a chart's data.

properties.name: descriptor
properties.notes: Conform a type to AXChartDescriptorRepresentable describing axes, series, and data points for VoiceOver's Audio Graph.

components.higNotes: Make every data visualization explorable: define axes with titles and units, series with data points, so VoiceOver can play the audio graph and read the data table. Required for Swift Charts that convey non-trivial data.
components.codeTemplate:
ChartView(data: data)
    .accessibilityChartDescriptor({descriptor})

components.title: Accessibility Labeled Pair
components.summary: Associates a label view with the content it describes so VoiceOver reads them as a coherent pair.

properties.name: role
properties.notes: Mark one view as .label and its partner as .content; they share a Namespace id to be linked.

properties.name: id
properties.notes: Shared identifier matching the label and its content within the same Namespace.

components.higNotes: Use when a label and value are laid out apart (e.g. a form column or grid) so VoiceOver still announces them together. Keep ids unique per pair within the namespace.

components.title: Accessibility Rotor Entry
components.summary: Tags a view so a custom rotor (defined elsewhere) can locate and scroll to it, even when the element is off-screen or in a different part of the hierarchy.

properties.name: id
properties.notes: Identifier matching an entry's id passed to accessibilityRotor(_:entries:). Lets SwiftUI scroll the tagged view into view when the rotor navigates to it.

properties.name: namespace
properties.notes: Shared @Namespace tying the entry to the rotor definition. The rotor and its entries must use the same namespace.

components.higNotes: Pair with accessibilityRotor when the elements a rotor references are lazily loaded or live far from the rotor's host. The id/namespace pair lets the system bring the right element on-screen and move VoiceOver focus to it. Without it, custom rotors cannot reliably reach off-screen content in a LazyVStack.

components.title: Accessibility Linked Group
components.summary: Links accessibility elements that are far apart in the hierarchy so users can quickly jump between them (e.g. a list item and its detail view).

properties.name: id
properties.notes: Identifier separating sets of linked elements within one namespace. Elements with matching id and namespace are linked.

properties.name: namespace
properties.notes: Shared @Namespace organizing the linked elements. All views marked with this namespace and id link together.

components.higNotes: Use to let assistive-tech users hop between the same content shown in two places — a row in a list and the same item in a detail pane. Keep ids unique per logical group within the namespace.

components.title: Accessibility Label (View Builder)
components.summary: Supplies an element's accessibility label from a view (rather than a string), so labels can be composed from other views including localized Text.

properties.name: content
properties.notes: ViewBuilder returning the view(s) whose text becomes the spoken label. Receives a placeholder representing the labeled view; lets you build the label from existing views instead of a literal string.

components.higNotes: Reach for the ViewBuilder overload when the label is best assembled from views you already have (e.g. concatenating several Text values) rather than a single string literal. The string/Text/LocalizedStringKey overloads remain the common case; this one avoids manually flattening composed content into one string.

components.title: Chart
components.summary: The SwiftUI view that hosts marks and renders a data-driven chart, generating scales, axes, and legends automatically.
components.summaryLong: Root container. Everything in this category is configured by attaching chart* view modifiers to a Chart and by composing ChartContent marks inside its builder.
components.higNotes: Choose the chart type that matches the comparison the user must make: bars for category comparison, lines for trends over time, area for cumulative magnitude, sector for part-to-whole. Keep marks under ~7 series; rely on automatic scales/legends before customizing. Always supply accessible value labels via .value(_:).

properties.name: plotHeight
properties.notes: Drives .frame(height:) on the Chart for the showroom. Range 120...500.

properties.name: showLegend
properties.notes: Maps to .chartLegend(_:). Legend appears only when marks are grouped via foregroundStyle(by:).

properties.name: legendPosition
properties.notes: Maps to .chartLegend(position:). tvOS/watchOS layout space is tight; prefer bottom.

properties.name: xAxisVisible
properties.notes: false maps to .chartXAxis(.hidden).

properties.name: yAxisVisible
properties.notes: false maps to .chartYAxis(.hidden).

properties.name: plotBackground
properties.notes: Maps to .chartPlotStyle { $0.background(...) }.

properties.name: scrollableX
properties.notes: Enables .chartScrollableAxes(.horizontal) for long content; pair with .chartXVisibleDomain.

properties.name: selectionEnabled
properties.notes: Enables .chartXSelection(value:) tap/drag selection with a scrolling RuleMark annotation.

components.codeTemplate:
Chart {
    BarMark(
        x: .value("Category", item.category),
        y: .value("Value", item.value)
    )
}
.frame(height: {plotHeight})
.chartLegend({showLegend}, position: {legendPosition})
.chartXAxis({xAxisVisible} ? .automatic : .hidden)
.chartYAxis({yAxisVisible} ? .automatic : .hidden)
.chartPlotStyle { plotArea in
    plotArea.background({plotBackground})
}
.chartScrollableAxes({scrollableX} ? .horizontal : [])
.chartXSelection(value: {selectionEnabled} ? $selectedX : .constant(nil))

components.title: BarMark
components.summary: Represents data as rectangular bars; supports vertical, horizontal, 1D, interval (Gantt), and stacked layouts.
components.higNotes: Use bars to compare discrete categories or show binned distributions (histograms). Horizontal bars scale to many categories and label easily. Stack only when the parts sum to a meaningful whole; otherwise group side-by-side with position(by:).

properties.name: orientation
properties.notes: Vertical = category on x, value on y. Horizontal swaps the axes in the initializer.

properties.name: stacking
properties.notes: normalized = 100% stacked; center = diverging; unstacked overlays. Passed as stacking: parameter.

properties.name: barWidth
properties.notes: width: .ratio(0.6), .fixed(20), or .inset(4). Controls thickness.

properties.name: cornerRadius
properties.notes: Maps to .cornerRadius(_:). Range 0...20. Rounds bar ends.

properties.name: foregroundStyle
properties.notes: Single-series color via .foregroundStyle(_:).

properties.name: groupByCategory
properties.notes: true adds .foregroundStyle(by:) + .position(by:) to color and offset bars per subcategory.

properties.name: opacity
properties.notes: Maps to .opacity(_:). Range 0...1.

properties.name: annotationPosition
properties.notes: Drives .annotation(position:) to label each bar with its value.

components.title: LineMark
components.summary: Connects data points with line segments to show trends; supports multiple series, interpolation, and symbols.
components.higNotes: Use lines for continuous trends over an ordered/temporal domain. Group multiple lines by series for comparison and color via foregroundStyle(by:). Add symbols only when individual points carry meaning or for accessibility differentiation.

properties.name: interpolationMethod
properties.notes: Maps to .interpolationMethod(_:). monotone/catmullRom smooth curves; step* for discrete jumps.

properties.name: lineWidth
properties.notes: Set via .lineStyle(StrokeStyle(lineWidth:)). Range 0.5...10.

properties.name: lineCap
properties.notes: StrokeStyle.lineCap.

properties.name: dashed
properties.notes: true sets StrokeStyle(dash: [6, 4]) for a dashed line.

properties.name: symbol
properties.notes: Maps to .symbol(_:). Marks each data point; aids accessibility/color-blind users.

properties.name: foregroundStyle
properties.notes: Single-series via .foregroundStyle(_:); use groupBySeries for multi.

properties.name: groupBySeries
properties.notes: true adds .foregroundStyle(by:) to render one colored line per series category.

components.codeTemplate:
LineMark(
    x: .value("Date", item.date),
    y: .value("Value", item.value)
)
.interpolationMethod({interpolationMethod})
.lineStyle(StrokeStyle(
    lineWidth: {lineWidth},
    lineCap: {lineCap},
    dash: {dashed} ? [6, 4] : []
))
.symbol({symbol})
.foregroundStyle({foregroundStyle})

components.title: AreaMark
components.summary: Fills the region under a line or between two bounds; supports stacked, normalized, streamgraph, and range-area charts.
components.higNotes: Use area to emphasize cumulative magnitude or part-to-whole over a continuous domain. Use normalized for 100% composition, center for streamgraphs, and a yStart/yEnd range for min-max bands. Apply gradients sparingly so data remains legible.

properties.name: stacking
properties.notes: center yields a streamgraph; normalized yields a 100% area chart.

properties.name: interpolationMethod
properties.notes: Smooths or steps the fill boundary.

properties.name: rangeMode
properties.notes: true uses the yStart/yEnd initializer to draw a min-max band instead of fill-to-baseline.

properties.name: useGradient
properties.notes: true applies a LinearGradient foregroundStyle fading to clear at the bottom.

properties.name: foregroundStyle
properties.notes: Base fill color.

properties.name: opacity
properties.notes: Range 0...1; areas often read better translucent.

properties.name: groupByCategory
properties.notes: true adds .foregroundStyle(by:) for stacked multi-series areas.

components.codeTemplate:
AreaMark(
    x: .value("Date", item.date),
    y: .value("Value", item.value),
    stacking: {stacking}
)
.interpolationMethod({interpolationMethod})
.foregroundStyle({useGradient} ? AnyShapeStyle(LinearGradient(colors: [{foregroundStyle}, .clear], startPoint: .top, endPoint: .bottom)) : AnyShapeStyle({foregroundStyle}))
.opacity({opacity})

components.title: PointMark
components.summary: Plots individual data points as symbols for scatter plots and bubble charts.
components.higNotes: Use points for scatter plots showing correlation between two quantitative variables. Encode a third dimension via symbolSize (bubble) or foregroundStyle(by:). Differentiate categories with both color and symbol shape for color-blind accessibility.

properties.name: symbol
properties.notes: Maps to .symbol(_:).

properties.name: symbolSize
properties.notes: Maps to .symbolSize(_:) in points². Range 10...300. Drive by a data value for bubble charts.

properties.name: foregroundStyle
properties.notes: Point fill color.

properties.name: opacity
properties.notes: Range 0...1; lowering opacity reveals overlapping points.

properties.name: groupByCategory
properties.notes: true adds .foregroundStyle(by:) and .symbol(by:) for categorical scatter.

components.codeTemplate:
PointMark(
    x: .value("X", item.x),
    y: .value("Y", item.y)
)
.symbol({symbol})
.symbolSize({symbolSize})
.foregroundStyle({foregroundStyle})
.opacity({opacity})

components.title: RuleMark
components.summary: Draws a horizontal or vertical reference line, threshold, or interval span across the plot.
components.higNotes: Use rules for thresholds, averages, goals, or to mark a selected x position. Style them subtly (thin, often dashed, muted color) so they support rather than dominate the primary marks. Label them with .annotation for context.

properties.name: orientation
properties.notes: horizontal uses y: (threshold); vertical uses x: (marker). Interval variants use xStart/xEnd or yStart/yEnd.

properties.name: lineWidth
properties.notes: StrokeStyle(lineWidth:). Range 0.5...6.

properties.name: dashed
properties.notes: true sets dash: [4, 4]; thresholds read best dashed.

properties.name: foregroundStyle
properties.notes: Reference-line color.

properties.name: annotationPosition
properties.notes: Drives .annotation(position:) for the label.

components.title: RectangleMark
components.summary: Fills rectangular cells defined by intervals on both axes; ideal for heat maps and matrix charts.
components.higNotes: Use rectangles for heat maps that encode a quantitative value as color across two categorical axes. Pair with .chartForegroundStyleScale and a continuous color gradient, and always show a legend so the color-to-value mapping is clear.

properties.name: valueColor
properties.notes: Base hue; intensity comes from foregroundStyle(by:) + chartForegroundStyleScale gradient.

properties.name: useContinuousScale
properties.notes: true encodes value as a continuous color via .foregroundStyle(by: .value(...)) with a gradient scale.

properties.name: cellWidth
properties.notes: width: controls horizontal cell size; .inset adds spacing between cells.

properties.name: cellHeight
properties.notes: height: controls vertical cell size.

properties.name: opacity
properties.notes: Range 0...1.

components.title: SectorMark
components.summary: Renders a pie or donut sector sized by angular proportion of a total; donut when innerRadius > 0.
components.higNotes: Limit to 5-7 sectors and group the remainder into 'Other'. Require positive values. A small angularInset and cornerRadius improve readability without distorting proportions. Prefer horizontal bars when precise comparison matters.

properties.name: innerRadiusRatio
properties.notes: innerRadius: .ratio(_). 0 = pie, >0 = donut. Range 0...0.9.

properties.name: outerRadiusInset
properties.notes: outerRadius: .inset(_). Range 0...40.

properties.name: angularInset
properties.notes: Gap between sectors. Range 0...8. Improves contrast/accessibility.

properties.name: cornerRadius
properties.notes: Maps to .cornerRadius(_:). Range 0...12. Keep small to avoid distortion.

properties.name: groupByCategory
properties.notes: true adds .foregroundStyle(by:) so each category gets a distinct color + legend.

components.codeTemplate:
SectorMark(
    angle: .value("Value", item.value),
    innerRadius: .ratio({innerRadiusRatio}),
    outerRadius: .inset({outerRadiusInset}),
    angularInset: {angularInset}
)
.cornerRadius({cornerRadius})
.foregroundStyle(by: .value("Category", item.name))

components.title: BarPlot (vectorized)
components.summary: Vectorized bar content that plots an entire data collection with one plot for high-performance large datasets.
components.higNotes: Use vectorized plots when rendering large collections; they are far more performant than per-element ForEach of BarMark. Apply KeyPath-based modifiers before simple-value modifiers, as the API requires.

properties.name: xKeyPath
properties.notes: x: .value("X", \.keyPath). KeyPath into the element type.

properties.name: yKeyPath
properties.notes: y: .value("Y", \.keyPath).

properties.name: foregroundStyle
properties.notes: Simple-value modifier; apply after KeyPath modifiers.

properties.name: groupByKeyPath
properties.notes: true uses .foregroundStyle(by: .value("Group", \.group)) for categorical coloring.

components.title: LinePlot (vectorized / function)
components.summary: Vectorized line content for whole collections and for plotting mathematical, parametric, or piecewise functions.
components.higNotes: Prefer LinePlot for large series and for analytic functions (y = f(x), parametric x/y over t). Combine multiple function plots with distinct foregroundStyle(by:) and set explicit chartXScale/chartYScale domains to frame the curve.

properties.name: mode
properties.notes: collection plots data; function plots y=f(x); parametric plots (x,y) over t with a domain.

properties.name: lineWidth
properties.notes: .lineStyle(StrokeStyle(lineWidth:)). Range 0.5...10.

properties.name: lineCap
properties.notes: StrokeStyle.lineCap.

properties.name: foregroundStyle
properties.notes: Curve color.

properties.name: opacity
properties.notes: Range 0...1.

components.title: AreaPlot (vectorized / function)
components.summary: Vectorized area content for whole collections and for filling under a plotted function.
components.higNotes: Use AreaPlot to shade the region under an analytic function or to render large area series efficiently. Use the yStart/yEnd function form for filled bands between two curves.

properties.name: mode
properties.notes: band uses yStart/yEnd closures to fill between two functions.

properties.name: foregroundStyle
properties.notes: Fill color.

properties.name: useGradient
properties.notes: true applies a LinearGradient fading to clear.

properties.name: opacity
properties.notes: Range 0...1.

components.title: PointPlot (vectorized)
components.summary: Vectorized point content for plotting large scatter collections with one performant plot.
components.higNotes: Use PointPlot for dense scatter plots over large datasets. Encode extra dimensions with symbolSize (by KeyPath) and foregroundStyle(by:) and keep opacity below 1 to reveal density.

properties.name: xKeyPath
properties.notes: x: .value("X", \.keyPath).

properties.name: yKeyPath
properties.notes: y: .value("Y", \.keyPath).

properties.name: symbolSize
properties.notes: .symbolSize(_:) in points². Range 10...200.

properties.name: foregroundStyle
properties.notes: Point color.

properties.name: opacity
properties.notes: Range 0...1.

components.title: SectorPlot (vectorized)
components.summary: Vectorized sector content that renders an entire pie or donut from a collection with one plot.
components.higNotes: Same composition rules as SectorMark (5-7 slices, positive values, small inset). Use the vectorized form when the slice collection is large or data-driven.

properties.name: angleKeyPath
properties.notes: angle: .value("Value", \.keyPath).

properties.name: innerRadiusRatio
properties.notes: innerRadius: .ratio(_). Range 0...0.9.

properties.name: angularInset
properties.notes: Range 0...8.

properties.name: foregroundStyleKeyPath
properties.notes: .foregroundStyle(by: .value("Category", \.keyPath)).

components.title: RulePlot & RectanglePlot (vectorized)
components.summary: Vectorized rule and rectangle content for plotting collections of reference lines or rectangular cells with one plot each.
components.higNotes: Use the vectorized RulePlot/RectanglePlot for data-driven collections of reference lines or heat-map cells where a per-element ForEach of RuleMark/RectangleMark would be expensive.

properties.name: markType
properties.notes: Switches between RulePlot and RectanglePlot template output.

properties.name: foregroundStyle
properties.notes: Mark color.

properties.name: lineWidth
properties.notes: Rule stroke width. Range 0.5...6.

properties.name: opacity
properties.notes: Range 0...1.

components.title: Chart3D
components.summary: A three-dimensional chart container for spatial visualizations such as surface plots and 3D scatter.
components.higNotes: New in OS 26. Use 3D charts only when the third spatial dimension genuinely aids comprehension (e.g. terrain, multivariate surfaces). Provide pose/camera controls and clear axis labels. Not available on tvOS or watchOS.

properties.name: cameraProjection
properties.notes: Maps to .chart3DCameraProjection(_:).

properties.name: pose
properties.notes: Drives .chart3DPose(_:) initial orientation.

properties.name: interactive
properties.notes: Allows drag-to-rotate of the 3D scene.

properties.name: zAxisLabel
properties.notes: 3D adds a third spatial axis. Configure z extent via .chartZScale(domain:); SurfacePlot/3D PointMark/RuleMark/RectangleMark all take an explicit z value.

components.title: Axis Customization
components.summary: Customizes axis ticks, grid lines, and value labels via chartXAxis/chartYAxis with AxisMarks builders.
components.higNotes: Keep axes clean: limit tick density, format value labels for the data type (dates, currency), and use grid lines sparingly. Hide redundant axes. Use AxisMarkValues.automatic(desiredCount:) to control tick count instead of hardcoding.

properties.name: axis
properties.notes: Selects chartXAxis vs chartYAxis to customize.

properties.name: axisPosition
properties.notes: AxisMarks(position:) location of the axis.

properties.name: tickValues
properties.notes: AxisMarks(values:). desiredCount controls tick density.

properties.name: desiredTickCount
properties.notes: Used with .automatic(desiredCount:). Range 2...12.

properties.name: showGridLines
properties.notes: Toggles AxisGridLine() in the AxisMarks builder.

properties.name: showTicks
properties.notes: Toggles AxisTick() in the builder.

properties.name: showValueLabels
properties.notes: Toggles AxisValueLabel().

properties.name: labelOrientation
properties.notes: AxisValueLabel(orientation:). Rotate labels to avoid collisions.

components.title: Scales & Domains
components.summary: Controls the mapping from data to plot via chartXScale/chartYScale domain, range, and scale type.
components.higNotes: Let scales be automatic unless a fixed domain aids comparison across charts or you must include zero. Log scales suit data spanning orders of magnitude; always label them clearly so the nonlinearity is obvious.

properties.name: axis
properties.notes: Selects chartXScale vs chartYScale.

properties.name: scaleType
properties.notes: Passed via .chartYScale(type:). Quantitative scales only for numeric data.

properties.name: useFixedDomain
properties.notes: true supplies an explicit domain: closed range instead of automatic.

properties.name: domainLowerBound
properties.notes: Lower bound when useFixedDomain is true. Range -100...100.

properties.name: domainUpperBound
properties.notes: Upper bound when useFixedDomain is true. Range 0...1000.

properties.name: zAxisAvailable
properties.notes: chartZScale(domain:range:type:) exists for Chart3D (OS 26). The spec's axis enum (x/y) omits the z option used by 3D charts.

components.title: Foreground Style Scale & Legend Grouping
components.summary: Maps categorical or continuous data to colors via foregroundStyle(by:) and customizes the color scale and legend.
components.higNotes: Use color to encode a single categorical or quantitative dimension. Provide a legend whenever color carries meaning. Choose color-blind-safe palettes and pair color with shape (symbol) where differentiation is critical.

properties.name: scaleMode
properties.notes: discrete maps categories to a palette; continuous maps a value range to a gradient.

properties.name: paletteColor1
properties.notes: First color in the discrete range / gradient start.

properties.name: paletteColor2
properties.notes: Second color.

properties.name: paletteColor3
properties.notes: Third color (discrete only).

components.title: Chart Selection & Scrolling Annotations
components.summary: Adds interactive value selection (tap/drag) on an axis, surfacing a binding used to draw a RuleMark with a scrolling annotation.
components.higNotes: Use chartXSelection for scrubbing data on touch. Reflect the selection with a RuleMark plus an .annotation that overflow-resolves within the plot bounds so it never clips. Combine with .chartScrollableAxes and .chartXVisibleDomain for paged time-series.

properties.name: selectionAxis
properties.notes: chartXSelection / chartYSelection / both.

properties.name: selectionMode
properties.notes: value binds a single Plottable; range binds a ClosedRange for brushing.

properties.name: scrollableAxes
properties.notes: Maps to .chartScrollableAxes(_:).

properties.name: visibleDomainLength
properties.notes: .chartXVisibleDomain(length:) number of units visible at once. Range 5...100.

properties.name: annotationOverflow
properties.notes: AnnotationOverflowResolution strategy so the scrolling annotation stays in bounds.

components.title: SurfacePlot (3D surface)
components.summary: 3D-only chart content that renders a mathematical surface y = f(x, z); the sole mark type exclusive to Chart3D.
components.summary_long: Renders a continuous surface from a closure (x, z) -> y inside a Chart3D. Supports multiple surfaces in one chart, explicit x/y/z scale domains, and surface-specific foreground styles (heightBased gradient, normalBased shading).
components.higNotes: New in OS 26 and Chart3D-only. Use heightBased to make surface shape readable via a y-value gradient; normalBased shades by facing direction. Set explicit chartXScale/chartYScale/chartZScale domains to frame the surface. Not available on tvOS or watchOS.

properties.name: surfaceStyle
properties.notes: Maps to .foregroundStyle(.heightBased) / .normalBased / a solid color. heightBased gradients by y-value; normalBased shades by surface normal.

properties.name: foregroundStyle
properties.notes: Solid fill used when surfaceStyle is solid.

properties.name: xDomainLength
properties.notes: .chartXScale(domain: -x...x). Range 1...10. Frames the x extent of the surface.

properties.name: zDomainLength
properties.notes: .chartZScale(domain: -z...z). Range 1...10. Frames the z extent of the surface.

components.title: Chart Overlay & Background (ChartProxy)
components.summary: Overlays or backs the plot area with custom SwiftUI content, using ChartProxy to convert between data values and view coordinates for custom interactivity.
components.summary_long: The foundational customization/interaction surface predating chartXSelection. .chartOverlay { proxy in ... } and .chartBackground { proxy in ... } give a ChartProxy plus a GeometryProxy so you can hit-test gestures, draw custom highlights, and map data<->position via proxy.value(atX:) / proxy.position(forX:). Used for custom tooltips, crosshairs, and lollipop selection on iOS 16 where chartXSelection (17+) is unavailable.
components.higNotes: Use chartOverlay for custom touch interaction and annotations layered above marks; chartBackground for static decoration behind them. Convert gesture locations with ChartProxy and the supplied GeometryProxy. Keep overlay content lightweight; prefer the higher-level chartXSelection on iOS 17+ for simple scrubbing.

properties.name: layer
properties.notes: Switches between .chartOverlay (above marks) and .chartBackground (behind marks).

properties.name: alignment
properties.notes: alignment: parameter positioning the custom content within the plot area.

properties.name: showCrosshair
properties.notes: Demo toggle: draw a crosshair/rule at the gesture location using proxy.value(atX:) -> proxy.position(forX:).

properties.name: accentColor
properties.notes: Color of the custom overlay marker / crosshair.

components.title: Tap Gesture
components.summary: Recognizes one or more taps (or clicks) and fires on completion.
components.summaryNote: On tvOS available 16.0+; counts clicks of the Siri Remote select button.

properties.name: count
properties.notes: Number of taps/clicks required before the gesture succeeds. Range 1...5 is sensible for a showroom; 1 = single tap, 2 = double tap.

properties.name: attachMode
properties.notes: Showroom-only switch: how the gesture is attached. .onTapGesture(count:perform:) is the shorthand; .gesture(_:) wraps an explicit TapGesture; high-priority and simultaneous demonstrate composition with child gestures.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: Drives gesture(_:isEnabled:). Disabling stops recognition without removing the modifier (iOS 18+ overload).

components.higNotes: Use single tap for the primary action; reserve double-tap for secondary/zoom. Keep tap targets at least 44x44 pt. Prefer a Button over a raw tap gesture for anything that reads as a control, so it inherits accessibility traits and focus behavior automatically.

components.title: Spatial Tap Gesture
components.summary: Recognizes taps and reports the tap location in a chosen coordinate space.

properties.name: count
properties.notes: Number of taps required before the gesture reports its location.

properties.name: coordinateSpace
properties.notes: Coordinate space the reported location (Value.location) is resolved into. Use .local for view-relative, .global for window-relative.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: Drives gesture(_:isEnabled:).

components.higNotes: Use when the action depends on where the user tapped (e.g. dropping a pin, placing an annotation). For purely binary actions, prefer plain TapGesture or a Button. Surface the resolved location visibly so the spatial nature is clear.

components.title: Long Press Gesture
components.summary: Succeeds when the user presses and holds for at least a minimum duration.
components.summaryNote: tvOS 14.0+. The .onLongPressGesture view modifier shorthand is unavailable on tvOS; use the gesture type there.

properties.name: minimumDuration
properties.notes: Seconds the press must be held before the gesture succeeds. Slider range 0.1...5.0, step 0.1. Default 0.5.

properties.name: maximumDistance
properties.notes: Maximum points the finger/cursor may move before the gesture fails. Range 0...100, default 10. Only on the .onLongPressGesture modifier and the LongPressGesture initializer overload.

properties.name: usePressingChanged
properties.notes: Showroom toggle: wire onPressingChanged to reflect the in-progress pressing state (true while finger is down before success). Lets you animate during the hold.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: Drives gesture(_:isEnabled:) / the modifier's enablement.

components.higNotes: Long press reveals secondary actions; pair it with a context menu (.contextMenu) where possible since that is the discoverable native pattern. Give clear in-progress feedback (scale, haptic) so users know the hold registered. Don't make long press the only way to reach an action.

components.title: Drag Gesture
components.summary: Tracks a continuous dragging motion, reporting translation, location, and velocity.
components.summaryNote: Not available on tvOS. minimumDistance:coordinateSpace: initializer with CoordinateSpaceProtocol is iOS 17+/macOS 14+; predictedEndTranslation and velocity are exposed on Value.

properties.name: minimumDistance
properties.notes: Distance in points the touch must move before the drag is recognized. Range 0...100. Default 10; set to 0 for an immediately-tracking drag.

properties.name: coordinateSpace
properties.notes: Coordinate space in which Value.location / startLocation are reported.

properties.name: trackingMode
properties.notes: Showroom-only: whether the demo binds via @GestureState .updating (auto-resets on end), state via .onChanged (persists), or both. Demonstrates the rubber-band reset behavior of GestureState.

properties.name: showVelocity
properties.notes: Overlay Value.velocity and Value.predictedEndTranslation, useful for momentum/flick demos.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: Drives gesture(_:isEnabled:).

components.higNotes: Drag is for direct manipulation: move, reorder, dismiss. Provide a draggable affordance and respect the natural direction of motion. Use predictedEndTranslation/velocity for momentum-aware snapping. Don't conflict with system edge gestures; use defersSystemGestures only when a fullscreen experience truly needs it.

components.title: Magnify Gesture
components.summary: Recognizes a pinch and tracks the magnification scale factor and anchor.
components.summaryNote: macOS 14.0+. Replaces the deprecated MagnificationGesture. Value exposes magnification, velocity, and startAnchor.

properties.name: minimumScaleDelta
properties.notes: Minimum change in scale before the gesture begins. Range 0...0.5. Default 0.01 — small so it starts responsively.

properties.name: trackingMode
properties.notes: Showroom-only: bind scale through @GestureState .updating (resets to 1.0 on end) versus .onChanged accumulating into @State.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: Drives gesture(_:isEnabled:).

components.higNotes: Pinch-to-zoom is the expected gesture for scaling content like photos and maps. Multiply the live magnification onto a committed base scale and clamp to sensible min/max. Combine with RotateGesture via .simultaneously for natural photo manipulation. Honor the gesture's startAnchor so zoom is centered on the pinch.

components.title: Rotate Gesture
components.summary: Recognizes a two-finger rotation and tracks the rotation angle and anchor.
components.summaryNote: macOS 14.0+. Replaces the deprecated RotationGesture. Value exposes rotation (Angle), velocity, and startAnchor.

properties.name: minimumAngleDelta
properties.notes: Minimum rotation (in degrees) before the gesture starts. Slider drives Angle(degrees:), range 0...30. Default 1 degree.

properties.name: trackingMode
properties.notes: Showroom-only: live angle via @GestureState .updating (resets on end) versus accumulating into @State on .onChanged.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: Drives gesture(_:isEnabled:).

components.higNotes: Rotation suits free transform of content (photos, stickers, shapes). Add the live rotation to a committed base angle, and rotate about the gesture's anchor. Pair with MagnifyGesture using .simultaneously for combined scale-and-rotate manipulation.

components.title: Rotate Gesture 3D
components.summary: Recognizes 3D rotation, tracking angle and axis, optionally constrained to one axis.
components.summaryNote: visionOS only. Value exposes rotation as a Rotation3D (RealityFoundation). Included for completeness; not renderable on iOS/macOS showroom targets.

properties.name: constrainedToAxis
properties.notes: Optional axis to constrain rotation to; the supplied axis is normalized. nil allows free 3D rotation.

properties.name: minimumAngleDelta
properties.notes: Minimum rotation before the gesture starts.

components.higNotes: visionOS spatial manipulation only. Constrain to a single axis when free rotation would be disorienting. Not applicable to the iOS/iPadOS/macOS/tvOS/watchOS showroom build.

components.title: Spatial Event Gesture
components.summary: Tracks multiple simultaneous spatial events (touches/clicks) with per-event identity and phase.
components.summaryNote: macOS 15.0+, watchOS 11.0+. Delivers a SpatialEventCollection; each Event has a stable id, phase (.active/.ended/.cancelled), location, timestamp, pose, and chirality. Ideal for multi-touch playgrounds.

properties.name: coordinateSpace
properties.notes: Coordinate space for each event's reported location.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: Drives gesture(_:isEnabled:).

components.higNotes: Use for advanced multi-point interactions (particle fields, multi-finger drawing) where you need per-event identity across frames. Track and clean up state per event id inside onChanged, and clear all state in onEnded; events can reach .ended or .cancelled while the gesture stays active.

components.title: Simultaneous Gesture Composition
components.summary: Combines two gestures so both can recognize and update at the same time.
components.summaryNote: Built via the .simultaneously(with:) operator. Value is a struct with optional first/second sub-values.

properties.name: firstGesture
properties.notes: Showroom selector for the first composed gesture. Classic pairing is Magnify + Rotate.

properties.name: secondGesture
properties.notes: Showroom selector for the second composed gesture.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: Drives gesture(_:isEnabled:) on the composed gesture.

components.higNotes: Use simultaneous composition when two manipulations genuinely co-occur, like pinch-zoom and rotate on a photo. Both sub-values arrive together; guard for nil per sub-gesture. Avoid composing gestures that fight for the same touches.

components.title: Sequenced Gesture Composition
components.summary: Runs a second gesture only after the first one succeeds, in order.
components.summaryNote: Built via the .sequenced(before:) operator. Value is an enum: .first(_) while the first runs, .second(_, _) once it succeeds.

properties.name: firstGesture
properties.notes: Gesture that must succeed first. Classic pattern: long press to 'pick up', then drag.

properties.name: secondGesture
properties.notes: Gesture that becomes active only after the first succeeds.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: Drives gesture(_:isEnabled:) on the composed gesture.

components.higNotes: Use sequencing for two-phase interactions where the first phase confirms intent, like long-press-then-drag to reorder list rows. Give clear feedback at the transition (haptic + scale) so users know phase two armed. Switch on the Value enum to drive each phase's UI.

components.title: Exclusive Gesture Composition
components.summary: Recognizes two gestures but lets only the first to succeed win, ignoring the other.
components.summaryNote: Built via the .exclusively(before:) operator. Value is an enum: .first(_) or .second(_). The first gesture has priority.

properties.name: firstGesture
properties.notes: Higher-priority gesture; if it succeeds the second is ignored.

properties.name: secondGesture
properties.notes: Fallback gesture, recognized only if the first fails.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: Drives gesture(_:isEnabled:) on the composed gesture.

components.higNotes: Use exclusivity to disambiguate gestures competing for the same touches, e.g. prefer a drag but fall back to a tap. Order matters — put the gesture you want to win first. Switch on the Value enum to react to whichever won.

components.title: Gesture State (@GestureState + updating)
components.summary: A property wrapper holding transient gesture state that auto-resets when the gesture ends.
components.summaryNote: Used with Gesture.updating(_:body:) which returns a GestureStateGesture. State rubber-bands back to its initial value automatically on gesture end/cancel.

properties.name: resetTransaction
properties.notes: Showroom toggle: set transaction.animation inside the updating body so the auto-reset animates. Off = snap back instantly.

properties.name: stateType
properties.notes: Showroom selector for which value type the demo's @GestureState carries, mapped to a matching gesture.

properties.name: initialValue
properties.notes: The value the state returns to when the gesture ends. Defined at declaration via @GestureState(initialValue:) or the wrapper's default.

components.higNotes: Prefer @GestureState over @State for values that only matter during the gesture: it guarantees a clean reset and avoids stale state if the gesture cancels. Commit the final value into a separate @State in onEnded. Set transaction.animation in the updating body to animate the spring-back.

components.title: Gesture Attach Modifiers (.gesture / .highPriorityGesture / .simultaneousGesture)
components.summary: View modifiers that attach a gesture and control its priority relative to child gestures.
components.summaryNote: iOS 18+/macOS 15+ add the gesture(_:isEnabled:) and gesture(_:name:isEnabled:) overloads. Priority modifiers determine whether this view's gesture beats, ties, or loses to gestures on child views.

properties.name: priority
properties.notes: .gesture = lowest (child wins ties); .highPriorityGesture = this view wins over children; .simultaneousGesture = both recognize together.

properties.name: mask
properties.notes: including: parameter. .all allows this gesture + subview gestures; .gesture only this; .subviews only children; .none disables all.

properties.name: name
properties.notes: iOS 18+ gesture(_:name:isEnabled:): a name to reference the gesture from GestureMask / dependency APIs.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: iOS 18+ overload: toggle recognition without removing the modifier.

components.higNotes: Reach for .highPriorityGesture when a parent must intercept a gesture that a child (e.g. a scroll view or button) would otherwise consume. Use GestureMask to surgically disable subview gestures. Avoid stacking many high-priority gestures, which makes precedence hard to reason about.

components.title: Defers System Gestures
components.summary: Asks the system to delay its own edge gestures so your view's gestures take priority first.
components.summaryNote: iOS/iPadOS only. Delays system edge swipes (e.g. Home, Control Center, Notification Center) along the given edges so an app gesture can run first; the user can still trigger the system gesture with a second swipe.

properties.name: edges
properties.notes: Which screen edges to defer the system gesture on.

components.higNotes: Use sparingly and only in immersive, full-screen experiences (games, drawing) where edge swipes would disrupt the core interaction. Deferring system gestures degrades discoverability of system affordances, so scope it to the smallest edge set required.

components.title: Window Drag Gesture
components.summary: Recognizes a drag and moves the containing window, for custom title-bar / drag regions.
components.summary_note: macOS only. Pair with allowsWindowActivationEvents(_:) so dragging works even when the window is inactive. Listed in the Gestures collection under 'Recognizing gestures that change over time' but absent from the draft.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: Drives gesture(_:isEnabled:); disabling stops window-drag recognition without removing the modifier.

properties.name: allowsWindowActivationEvents
properties.notes: Showroom toggle wiring the companion .allowsWindowActivationEvents(_:) modifier so the drag still works when the window is inactive (the canonical pairing in Apple's docs).

components.higNotes: Use to build custom window chrome (toolbars, drag handles, frameless windows) on macOS. Keep an obvious draggable affordance and avoid covering standard title-bar areas. Enable window activation events so an inactive window can be moved in one motion.

components.title: Apple Pencil Gestures (double-tap & squeeze)
components.summary: Responds to Apple Pencil double-tap and squeeze gestures, honoring the user's system preference.
components.summary_note: iOS/iPadOS/Mac Catalyst 17.5+, macOS 14.5+, visionOS 26.2+. Two modifiers: onPencilDoubleTap (PencilDoubleTapGestureValue) and onPencilSqueeze (PencilSqueezeGesturePhase). Honor preferredPencilDoubleTapAction / preferredPencilSqueezeAction. An entire 'Recognizing Apple Pencil gestures' section of the collection is missing from the draft.

properties.name: gestureKind
properties.notes: Showroom selector: onPencilDoubleTap delivers a discrete value; onPencilSqueeze delivers a phase (.began/.changed/.ended) you can use to drive a contextual palette.

properties.name: respectPreferredAction
properties.notes: Showroom toggle: read @Environment(\.preferredPencilDoubleTapAction)/(\.preferredPencilSqueezeAction) and bail when it is .ignore, demonstrating the recommended honoring of the user's system setting.

components.higNotes: Respect the user's chosen system action for double-tap and squeeze via the preferredPencil*Action environment values; only override with explicit in-app configuration. Use squeeze to surface a contextual palette anchored at the hover pose. Don't make a Pencil gesture the only path to an action.

components.title: Long Touch Gesture (tvOS)
components.summary: Recognizes a long touch on the tvOS Siri Remote surface without an actual click.
components.summary_note: tvOS only. Distinct from LongPressGesture: it fires when the finger rests on the remote's touch surface without pressing. Listed in the Gestures collection's long-press section but omitted from the draft.

properties.name: minimumDuration
properties.notes: Seconds the finger must rest on the touch surface before the gesture succeeds. Range 0.1...5.0, default 0.5.

properties.name: useTouchingChanged
properties.notes: Showroom toggle: wire onTouchingChanged to reflect the in-progress touching state (true while the finger rests before success).

components.higNotes: tvOS-only interaction tied to the Siri Remote touch surface. Provide clear focus-engine feedback since there is no on-screen cursor. Reserve for secondary affordances; don't gate the primary action behind a long touch.

components.title: Hand Gesture Shortcut
components.summary: Maps a system hand gesture (e.g. double-pinch on watchOS) to a control's primary action.
components.summary_note: iOS/iPadOS/Mac Catalyst 18.0+, macOS 15.0+, tvOS 18.0+, watchOS 11.0+. Takes a HandGestureShortcut (e.g. .primaryAction); performing the system hand gesture is equivalent to interacting with the control. Listed in 'Defining custom gestures'; omitted from the draft.

properties.name: shortcut
properties.notes: The hand-gesture shortcut to associate with this control. On watchOS, .primaryAction maps to the double-pinch (AssistiveTouch) gesture. Target is resolved leading-to-trailing in the active scene.

properties.name: isEnabled
properties.notes: Whether the shortcut is active for this control.

components.higNotes: Primarily a watchOS interaction (double-pinch). Attach the shortcut to the single most important control in the scene so the system resolves it unambiguously. Don't assign the same shortcut to multiple competing controls. Pair with a clear visual hint that the gesture is available.

components.title: @State
components.summary: A property wrapper for transient, view-owned source-of-truth value-type state that re-renders the view on change.
components.higNotes: Use @State only for transient UI state that the view itself owns (a toggle, a sheet flag, an editing string). Mark it private. For shared or model data use @Observable + @State-held reference, or pass a Binding. Never use @State to mirror a value that has another owner.

properties.name: wrappedValue
properties.notes: The stored source of truth. In a showroom expose a representative editable value (a Bool driven by toggle, an Int by stepper, a String by text).

properties.name: valueKind
properties.notes: Drives which demo control renders. Bool→Toggle, Int→Stepper, Double→Slider, String→TextField, Date→DatePicker.

properties.name: isPrivate
properties.notes: House convention: @State should always be declared private. Toggle is documentation-only; emit `private` keyword when on.

properties.name: initialValueExpression
properties.notes: Literal initializer used in the generated declaration; also usable with State(initialValue:) for computed defaults in init.

components.title: @Binding
components.summary: A two-way reference to a source of truth owned elsewhere, letting a child view read and mutate the parent's state.
components.higNotes: Pass a Binding to a child when the child needs to mutate a value the parent owns. Obtain it with the $ projected value of @State/@Bindable/@Observed. Prefer passing a focused Binding to a single field over passing the whole model when only one property is edited.

properties.name: wrappedValue
properties.notes: The value read/written through the binding; control type follows the wrapped value type.

properties.name: source
properties.notes: How the binding is constructed: $state projection, Binding.constant(_:), Binding(get:set:), or $bindable.property.

properties.name: useConstant
properties.notes: When on, generate Binding.constant(value) for previews/disabled demos — read-only, writes are ignored.

properties.name: animation
properties.notes: Use binding.animation(_:) to animate downstream changes triggered through the binding.

components.title: @Observable (Observation macro)
components.summary: A macro that makes a reference type observable so SwiftUI tracks per-property reads and updates only views that depend on changed properties.
components.higNotes: Prefer @Observable over the legacy ObservableObject/@Published for all new model types. SwiftUI tracks individual property access, so views invalidate only on the specific properties they read. Hold the instance with @State for a view-owned model, @Environment for an injected one, or let for an external reference.

properties.name: ownershipPattern
properties.notes: How the observable instance reaches the view: @State private var model = Model(); @Environment(Model.self); plain let model: Model; or Model.shared.

properties.name: isMainActor
properties.notes: House convention: annotate UI-facing observable services @MainActor. Emits @MainActor before @Observable.

properties.name: ignoredProperties
properties.notes: Properties marked @ObservationIgnored are excluded from change tracking (caches, derived non-UI fields).

properties.name: trackedProperty
properties.notes: Demo property the view reads; mutating it invalidates only views observing it.

components.title: @Bindable
components.summary: A property wrapper that creates bindings to the mutable properties of an @Observable object, including ones injected via @Environment.
components.higNotes: Use @Bindable to get $-prefixed bindings into an Observable model's properties. As a stored property when the model is passed in, or as a local `@Bindable var x = x` inside body (or a ForEach element) to bind into a @State/@Environment-held observable. Don't use it to own the model — that's @State's job.

properties.name: usage
properties.notes: @Bindable var model: Model (passed in); @Bindable var model = model (local); or bridging an @Environment(Model.self) value to bindings.

properties.name: boundProperty
properties.notes: Which model property a control binds to via $model.<property>.

properties.name: controlForProperty
properties.notes: Demo control wired to the bound property.

components.title: @Environment (key path)
components.summary: Reads a built-in or custom EnvironmentValues entry by key path, propagated implicitly down the view hierarchy.
components.higNotes: Use @Environment to read ambient configuration (color scheme, dynamic type size, locale, dismiss action, scene phase) rather than threading it through initializers. Reading an environment value creates a dependency: the view invalidates when that value changes. Prefer the most specific built-in value over inventing a custom one.

properties.name: keyPath
properties.notes: The environment key path to read. Showroom should let you pick any built-in EnvironmentValues key. Some are read-only actions (dismiss, openURL, refresh).

properties.name: readByType
properties.notes: iOS 17+: @Environment(Model.self) reads an injected Observable by type instead of by key path. When on, generate the type-based form.

properties.name: observableType
properties.notes: Type used with @Environment(Type.self) when readByType is on. Optional variant @Environment(Type.self) yields Type? if not injected.

components.title: EnvironmentValues
components.summary: The collection of all environment values flowing through a view hierarchy; the keyed store @Environment reads and the environment modifiers write.
components.higNotes: Treat EnvironmentValues as the canonical place to look up ambient state. Extend it with @Entry for custom values. Don't overload the environment with model data better passed explicitly; reserve it for cross-cutting configuration consumed deep in the tree.

properties.name: builtInValueGroup
properties.notes: Categorizes the built-in values for browsing: appearance (colorScheme, tint), accessibility (reduceMotion, sizeCategory), layout (sizeClass, layoutDirection), localization (locale, calendar, timeZone), actions (dismiss, openURL, openWindow, refresh), lifecycle (scenePhase), data (modelContext, managedObjectContext).

properties.name: subscriptAccess
properties.notes: Custom values read/written via values[MyKey.self] when not using @Entry.

components.title: .environment(_:_:) (key path writer)
components.summary: Sets a value for a writable EnvironmentValues key path on a view's subtree.
components.higNotes: Override an environment value for a whole subtree from the container. Prefer purpose-built modifiers (.foregroundStyle, .font, .environment(\.locale, ...)) where they exist. Setting at the right level avoids re-specifying configuration on every descendant.

properties.name: keyPath
properties.notes: Writable key path to override. Read-only action values (dismiss, openURL) cannot be set here.

properties.name: value
properties.notes: The value to inject; type follows the key path's value type.

components.title: .environment(_:) (Observable injector)
components.summary: Injects an @Observable object into the environment so descendants read it by type with @Environment(Type.self).
components.higNotes: This is the modern replacement for .environmentObject. Inject Observable model objects at the root of the subtree that needs them. Reading by type keeps call sites clean and avoids fatal crashes from missing keys when you use the optional read form.

properties.name: object
properties.notes: The Observable instance to inject. Hold it with @State at the injection site so it persists across body re-evaluations.

properties.name: allowNilInjection
properties.notes: Optional overload .environment(value as Model?) injects an optional, so descendants reading @Environment(Model.self) get nil instead of crashing when absent.

components.title: .transformEnvironment(_:transform:)
components.summary: Mutates an existing environment value in place for a subtree, deriving the new value from the inherited one.
components.higNotes: Use when the new value depends on the inherited one (append to a list, scale a number, clamp a size). Prefer .environment(_:_:) when you simply set an absolute value.

properties.name: keyPath
properties.notes: Key path of the value to transform.

properties.name: transform
properties.notes: Closure mutating the inherited value in place.

components.title: EnvironmentKey
components.summary: The protocol that defines a custom environment value's default — backs custom EnvironmentValues entries (manual alternative to @Entry).
components.higNotes: Define custom environment values only for genuinely cross-cutting configuration consumed far from where it's set. Prefer the @Entry macro (iOS 13+) for new code — it generates the key and accessor for you. Use the manual EnvironmentKey conformance only when you need a named key type for reuse.

properties.name: defaultValue
properties.notes: Required static defaultValue returned when no ancestor sets the value.

properties.name: valueType
properties.notes: The associated Value type. Reference-type values should be sendable/immutable to be safe across the tree.

properties.name: accessorName
properties.notes: Computed property added to EnvironmentValues to expose the key by name.

components.title: @Entry
components.summary: A macro that generates a custom entry for EnvironmentValues, Transaction, ContainerValues, or FocusedValues in one line.
components.higNotes: Preferred modern way to add custom environment/transaction/container/focused values. For EnvironmentValues/Transaction/ContainerValues you supply a default. For FocusedValues the type must be Optional and the default is always nil.

properties.name: targetStructure
properties.notes: Which value collection to extend. FocusedValues entries must be Optional with no explicit default.

properties.name: name
properties.notes: Name of the generated property.

properties.name: valueType
properties.notes: Stored type. For FocusedValues must be an Optional.

properties.name: defaultValue
properties.notes: Default for Environment/Transaction/Container entries; omitted (always nil) for FocusedValues.

components.title: @AppStorage
components.summary: A property wrapper that reads and writes a UserDefaults value and re-renders the view when that default changes.
components.higNotes: Use @AppStorage for small, simple, app-wide preferences (theme, onboarding-seen, toggles). Never store sensitive data or large blobs in UserDefaults. House convention: model singleton services persist richer state via JSON in UserDefaults rather than scattering @AppStorage across views.

properties.name: key
properties.notes: UserDefaults key. Keep keys namespaced and stable across releases.

properties.name: wrappedValue
properties.notes: Default value if the key is absent. Supported: Bool, Int, Double, String, URL, Data, and RawRepresentable with Int/String raw value (plus their Optionals).

properties.name: valueType
properties.notes: Constrains the supported storage types and which demo control renders.

properties.name: store
properties.notes: Optional UserDefaults suite (e.g. App Group for widget sharing). Defaults to the value of .defaultAppStorage or .standard.

components.title: .defaultAppStorage(_:)
components.summary: Sets the UserDefaults store that @AppStorage uses by default for a view subtree.
components.higNotes: Use to point a subtree's @AppStorage at an App Group suite (shared with widgets/extensions) without specifying store: on every wrapper. Apply once near the root of the feature that shares defaults.

properties.name: store
properties.notes: The defaults suite to use for all descendant @AppStorage without an explicit store:.

components.codeTemplate:
SettingsView()
    .defaultAppStorage(UserDefaults(suiteName: {suiteName})!)

components.title: @SceneStorage
components.summary: A property wrapper for lightweight per-scene state that the system automatically saves and restores across app launches.
components.higNotes: Use @SceneStorage for UI restoration state scoped to a scene (selected tab, scroll position, draft text). Each scene has its own storage; data isn't shared between scenes and is destroyed when the scene is. Keep it lightweight — never store model data or sensitive data here.

properties.name: key
properties.notes: Identifier unique within the scene's storage.

properties.name: wrappedValue
properties.notes: Default value; supported types match AppStorage (Bool, Int, Double, String, URL, Data, RawRepresentable Int/String, and Optionals).

properties.name: valueType
properties.notes: Constrains storable types and the demo control.

components.title: @FocusState
components.summary: A property wrapper that reads and programmatically moves keyboard/system focus among focusable views in a scene.
components.higNotes: Use @FocusState to drive and observe focus: advance fields on submit, focus the first invalid field on validation, or dismiss the keyboard by setting it to nil/false. The wrapped value must be Bool or an Optional Hashable. Avoid binding the same value to two fields (ambiguous) — use distinct enum cases.

properties.name: valueShape
properties.notes: Bool form for a single focusable view; Optional Hashable (often an enum) to track which of several fields is focused.

properties.name: fieldEnum
properties.notes: The Hashable type enumerating focusable targets when using the optional form.

properties.name: boundModifier
properties.notes: focused(_:) binds a Bool to whether the view (or a descendant) has focus; focused(_:equals:) matches a specific enum case for granular control.

properties.name: programmaticFocus
properties.notes: Set the wrapped value to move focus; set to nil/false to resign focus and dismiss the keyboard.

components.title: .focused(_:) / .focused(_:equals:)
components.summary: Modifiers that associate a focusable view with a @FocusState binding, by Boolean presence or by matching a specific value.
components.higNotes: Attach .focused(_:equals:) to each field with its distinct case for multi-field forms; use .focused(_:) with a Bool for a single field or to observe whether a container or its descendants hold focus.

properties.name: binding
properties.notes: Projected value of a @FocusState property.

properties.name: equalsValue
properties.notes: The value this view's focus corresponds to; omitted in the Bool overload.

components.codeTemplate:
TextField("Field", text: $text)
    .focused({binding}, equals: {equalsValue})

components.title: \.isFocused (Environment)
components.summary: A read-only environment value indicating whether the nearest focusable ancestor currently has focus.
components.higNotes: Read \.isFocused to restyle a control (highlight, scale) based on whether its focus scope is active — common on tvOS for focus-driven UI and on macOS/iPad with hardware keyboards.

properties.name: isFocused
properties.notes: Read-only; reflects focus of the enclosing focusable region. Pair with .focusable() to define the region.

components.title: @FocusedValue
components.summary: Reads a value published by the currently focused view or an ancestor, reflecting the value closest to focus.
components.higNotes: Use @FocusedValue (with .focusedValue/.focusedSceneValue) to wire commands, menu bar items, and toolbar actions to whatever document or selection is focused — central to macOS multi-window apps. The wrapped value is always Optional (nil when nothing focused publishes the key).

properties.name: keyPath
properties.notes: Custom FocusedValues entry (declared with @Entry, Optional type). iOS 17+/macOS 14+ also support @FocusedValue(MyType.self).

properties.name: readByType
properties.notes: Type-based read @FocusedValue(Type.self) introduced alongside the @Entry-driven keying; otherwise read by key path.

components.title: @FocusedBinding
components.summary: Reads a Binding published by the focused view, unwrapping it so commands can both read and write the focused view's value.
components.higNotes: Use @FocusedBinding when a command needs to mutate (not just read) a value owned by the focused view — e.g. a menu toggle that flips a property of the focused document. Publish with .focusedSceneValue(_:) passing a Binding.

properties.name: keyPath
properties.notes: FocusedValues entry whose value is a Binding. The wrapper unwraps it to an Optional Value with two-way access.

components.title: .focusedValue / .focusedSceneValue
components.summary: Publishes a value or binding into FocusedValues so focused-value readers (commands, menus) can observe it.
components.higNotes: Use .focusedValue when the value should be observed only while the modified view is focused; use .focusedSceneValue to publish at scene scope (the active window), which is what menu-bar commands typically read.

properties.name: scope
properties.notes: View-focus scope vs. scene (active-window) scope.

properties.name: keyPath
properties.notes: FocusedValues entry to publish into.

properties.name: value
properties.notes: Value (or Binding) to publish. Pass $binding when paired with @FocusedBinding readers.

components.codeTemplate:
DocumentView()
    .{scope}({keyPath}, {value})

components.title: PreferenceKey
components.summary: A protocol defining a typed value that flows up the view tree from descendants to ancestors, with a reduce strategy for conflicts.
components.higNotes: Use a PreferenceKey to report data from children to a container (measured sizes, a title, a list of section anchors). Provide a defaultValue and a reduce(value:nextValue:) that merges contributions from multiple subviews. Prefer built-in mechanisms (navigationTitle, layout) when they exist.

properties.name: valueType
properties.notes: The Value associated type. Common: CGFloat (max width), [Anchor<CGRect>], String.

properties.name: defaultValue
properties.notes: Value used when no view sets the preference.

properties.name: reduceStrategy
properties.notes: How reduce(value:nextValue:) combines multiple subview contributions.

components.title: .preference(key:value:)
components.summary: Sets a value for a custom PreferenceKey on a view, contributing it upward to ancestor readers.
components.higNotes: Attach .preference to the descendant that knows the value (often inside a background GeometryReader for sizes). Multiple contributions get merged by the key's reduce.

properties.name: key
properties.notes: The preference key type.

properties.name: value
properties.notes: Value to contribute. Frequently derived from a GeometryReader in a background.

components.title: .onPreferenceChange(_:perform:)
components.summary: Runs a closure in the container whenever the reduced value for a PreferenceKey changes.
components.higNotes: Read aggregated child preferences in the parent here, typically copying into @State to drive layout. Avoid heavy work in the closure; the Value must be Equatable. Beware update loops if the state you set changes the preference.

properties.name: key
properties.notes: The key to observe (its Value must be Equatable).

properties.name: perform
properties.notes: Closure receiving the new reduced value; usually stores it into @State.

components.title: .transformPreference(_:_:)
components.summary: Modifies the value of a PreferenceKey in place as it propagates up, deriving it from the current value.
components.higNotes: Use to adjust a preference contribution mid-tree (offset a measured value, tag an entry) without replacing it outright. Prefer .preference when setting an absolute value.

properties.name: key
properties.notes: Key whose propagating value to transform.

properties.name: transform
properties.notes: In-place mutation of the inherited preference value.

components.title: .anchorPreference(key:value:transform:)
components.summary: Reports a geometric Anchor (bounds, point) up the tree via a PreferenceKey, resolved later in a coordinate space.
components.higNotes: Use anchor preferences for overlays/backgrounds that must align to a descendant's frame regardless of layout nesting (tooltips, selection highlights, connecting lines). Resolve the Anchor with a GeometryProxy in overlayPreferenceValue/backgroundPreferenceValue.

properties.name: key
properties.notes: Preference key whose Value holds Anchor data.

properties.name: anchorSource
properties.notes: Which geometry to capture as the anchor.

properties.name: transform
properties.notes: Maps the captured anchor into the key's Value (often wrapping in an array or dictionary).

components.title: .overlayPreferenceValue / .backgroundPreferenceValue
components.summary: Builds an overlay or background using the reduced value of a PreferenceKey, with a GeometryProxy to resolve anchors.
components.minOS: iOS 13.0 / iPadOS 13.0 / macOS 10.15 / tvOS 13.0 / watchOS 6.0 (alignment overload: iOS 18.0+)
components.higNotes: Use these to draw decorations positioned from child anchors collected via preferences — selection rings, callouts, badges. Resolve Anchor values with proxy[anchor]. The iOS 18 alignment overloads let you align the generated layer.

properties.name: layer
properties.notes: Whether the generated content sits above (overlay) or below (background) the view.

properties.name: key
properties.notes: Key whose reduced value feeds the builder.

properties.name: alignment
properties.notes: Available on the iOS 18+ alignment overloads to position the generated layer.

properties.name: content
properties.notes: View builder receiving the preference value; use a GeometryReader/proxy to resolve anchors to rects.

components.codeTemplate:
ContainerView()
    .{layer}PreferenceValue({key}) { value in
        GeometryReader { proxy in
            let rect = proxy[value]
            RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius: 8)
                .stroke()
                .frame(width: rect.width, height: rect.height)
                .offset(x: rect.minX, y: rect.minY)
        }
    }

components.title: .onChange(of:initial:_:)
components.summary: Runs an action when an observed Equatable value changes, optionally firing once on initial appearance, with old/new values.
components.minOS: iOS 17.0 / iPadOS 17.0 / macOS 14.0 / tvOS 17.0 / watchOS 10.0 (current two-parameter form)
components.higNotes: Use onChange to react to value changes — but prefer deriving state during body or using task(id:) for async work. The iOS 17 form gives both old and new values; the zero/two-parameter and initial: variants replaced the deprecated single-parameter API.

properties.name: value
properties.notes: The value to observe; must be Equatable.

properties.name: initial
properties.notes: When true, fires the action once when the view first appears, in addition to on change.

properties.name: closureShape
properties.notes: Action signature: () -> Void or (oldValue, newValue) -> Void.

components.codeTemplate:
TextField("Query", text: $query)
    .onChange(of: {value}, initial: {initial}) { {closureShape == "oldNew" ? "oldValue, newValue" : ""} in
        performSearch()
    }

components.title: .onReceive(_:perform:)
components.summary: Runs an action when a Combine Publisher emits a value, bridging external event streams into a view.
components.higNotes: Use onReceive to respond to Combine publishers — timers, NotificationCenter, system notifications. For modern Swift concurrency prefer .task with an AsyncSequence where possible. Keep the closure light and route work to a model.

properties.name: publisher
properties.notes: Any Combine Publisher with Failure == Never.

properties.name: perform
properties.notes: Closure receiving each emitted element.

components.title: @StateObject (legacy)
components.summary: A property wrapper that instantiates and owns an ObservableObject for a view's lifetime — the pre-Observation way to own reference model data.
components.higNotes: Legacy: for new code prefer @Observable + @State. Use @StateObject only when interoperating with existing ObservableObject types. It guarantees a single instance across body re-evaluations (unlike @ObservedObject).

properties.name: wrappedValue
properties.notes: The owned ObservableObject; constructed in an autoclosure so it's created exactly once.

properties.name: publishedDriver
properties.notes: Demonstrates that @Published properties on the object trigger view updates.

components.title: @ObservedObject (legacy)
components.summary: Observes an externally-owned ObservableObject passed into a view — the pre-Observation way to receive a reference model.
components.higNotes: Legacy: prefer passing an @Observable object as a plain let in new code. @ObservedObject does not own its object — never initialize the source of truth here, or it will be recreated on each parent update. Use $ to derive bindings to its @Published properties.

properties.name: wrappedValue
properties.notes: The observed object, owned elsewhere (typically a @StateObject upstream).

properties.name: bindingProperty
properties.notes: A @Published property to bind via $model.<property>.

components.title: @EnvironmentObject / .environmentObject(_:) (legacy)
components.summary: Reads an ObservableObject injected into the environment by type — the pre-Observation counterpart to @Environment(Type.self).
components.higNotes: Legacy: prefer .environment(_:) + @Environment(Type.self) with @Observable. @EnvironmentObject crashes at runtime if no ancestor injected the object via .environmentObject(_:) — always inject before reading. Useful only when bridging existing ObservableObject code.

properties.name: objectType
properties.notes: The ObservableObject type read from the environment.

properties.name: injected
properties.notes: States: when no ancestor injects the object, reading it crashes — the 'error' state demonstrates this requirement.

components.title: @ScaledMetric
components.summary: A dynamic property that scales a numeric value relative to the current Dynamic Type size, keeping custom dimensions (icon sizes, paddings) proportional to the user's text-size setting.
